# Research Log

_One dated entry per day. Appended by /pre-market at 07:00 WIB. Optional midday addendum._
_Read by /market-open to determine execution plan. Never delete entries._

## 2026-04-19 (Sunday — simulation run)

### Macro Snapshot

| Indicator | Value | Change | Note |
|-----------|-------|--------|------|
| IHSG | 7,621 | +2.07% WoW | Up from 7,307 on Apr 9; +7.24% over past month; +18.38% YoY |
| IDR/USD | 17,115 | -0.97% MoM | Rupiah weakening; Apr range 16,870–17,194 |
| Newcastle coal (USD/ton) | 133.55 | -0.15% DoD | Retreating from Mar surge above $140; still +20% MoM |
| Palm oil (CPO, MYR/ton) | 4,450–4,580 | Sideways | Export volume down 30–39% in first 10 days Apr; oil rally partial offset |
| Nickel (USD/ton) | 17,635 | +2.7% vs Apr 1 | New HPM formula effective Apr 15 boosted sentiment; range $17,000–17,635 |
| Asia overnight (Apr 18) | Nikkei -1.75% (58,476); HSI -1.01%; CSI300 -0.17% | Profit-taking Fri after Thu surge | US-Iran deal optimism faded; Brent near $98 |
| Foreign flow (IDX) | Net SELL ~IDR 2–2.1T | Persistent outflow | Exception: BlackRock/JPMorgan added BBRI shares in Apr |

### Sector Momentum

| Sector | Trend | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Banking | BULLISH | BI rate held 4.75%; BBRI ex-div Apr 21 (IDR 209/share, 10.1% yield); BlackRock + JPMorgan accumulating; BBCA at 34% discount to 10-yr avg P/E |
| Coal / Energy | CAUTIOUSLY BULLISH | Newcastle up ~20% in Mar, now consolidating at $133; ITMG P/E 9.4x, yield 11%; DMO policy and output pressure cap upside |
| CPO / Agri | NEUTRAL-NEGATIVE | Exports weak (-30–39% early Apr); prices sideways at MYR 4,450–4,580; ringgit strength headwind |
| Property | NEUTRAL | BI holding rates limits immediate rate-cut catalyst; IDR weakness dents sentiment |
| Consumer | NEUTRAL | No immediate catalyst visible; IDR weakness pressures imported input costs |
| Telco | NEUTRAL | No data catalyst this week |

### Top 3 Candidates

#### 1. BBRI — Institutional accumulation + dividend yield play ahead of Apr 21 ex-date

- Thesis: BBRI is Indonesia's largest micro/SME lender trading at IDR 3,430 with a 10.1% dividend yield and a cash dividend of IDR 209/share going ex on April 21 — one of the most attractive income plays on the IDX right now.
- Thesis: BlackRock (19M shares) and JPMorgan (680K shares) both added in April, signaling that smart foreign money is accumulating despite the broader foreign outflow trend — a divergence that historically precedes a re-rating.
- Catalyst: Ex-dividend date April 21, 2026 (IDR 209/share); Q1 2026 earnings release April 29, 2026.
- Proposed entry: IDR 3,430 | Stop: IDR 3,190 (-7% cut-loss rule) | Target: IDR 3,900 (+13.7% upside)
- Conviction: HIGH

#### 2. ANTM — Policy-driven nickel ore price reset creates earnings upgrade cycle

- Thesis: Aneka Tambang is a state-owned miner with 18.1M wmt of nickel ore quota in 2026 (+12.7% YoY); the new ESDM HPM formula effective April 15 raised benchmark ore prices from ~$17/wmt to ~$40/wmt for 1.2% grade ore — a structural margin expansion that the market has not yet fully priced.
- Thesis: ANTM also has a gold production target of 935 kg (+25.8%) acting as a second revenue driver, and the stock is up 15.6% YTD with analyst targets at IDR 3,950, suggesting limited downside from current levels.
- Catalyst: New HPM Ministerial Decree No. 144 took effect April 15 — first full-quarter impact visible in Q2 results; nickel LME at $17,635 (multi-week high) adding to realization price tailwind.
- Proposed entry: IDR 3,940 | Stop: IDR 3,664 (-7%) | Target: IDR 4,600 (+16.8% upside, toward analyst TP of IDR 3,950–4,600)
- Conviction: MEDIUM-HIGH

#### 3. ITMG — Deep-value coal with 11% dividend yield; Newcastle rebound puts a floor under earnings

- Thesis: Indo Tambangraya Megah trades at only 9.4x trailing P/E with an 11.05% dividend yield — statistically cheap for a profitable exporter with no debt, at a time when Newcastle coal is holding above $130 after a 20%+ surge in March.
- Thesis: ITMG's next earnings (May 7) should reflect elevated Q1 coal prices, and with DMO-capped domestic sales, its export realizations in the $130+ range should translate to stronger-than-consensus margins.
- Catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings release May 7, 2026; continued coal price strength driven by supply tightness post-March surge.
- Proposed entry: IDR 27,050 | Stop: IDR 25,157 (-7%) | Target: IDR 31,500 (+16.4% upside)
- Conviction: MEDIUM

### Flagged Risks

- IDR/USD weakness (17,115 and trending toward 17,200) could prompt BI to intervene or signal hawkishness, weighing on rate-sensitive plays and equity multiples broadly.
- Foreign fund outflows remain persistent at ~IDR 2T net sell per week — a continuing headwind for index-level and large-cap liquidity; any escalation in outflows could trigger stop-outs.
- Asian markets showed sharp Friday profit-taking (Nikkei -1.75%, HSI -1%): if this carries into Monday open, IDX may gap down, creating adverse entry conditions for all three candidates.
- BBRI ex-dividend April 21 — stock will trade ex-div (adjusted down ~IDR 209) on that date; entry before ex-date carries dividend capture risk if price falls more than the dividend amount post ex-date.
- Newcastle coal has already surged 20%+ in March and is now consolidating; any downside surprise from China demand or Indian import data could cap ITMG upside quickly.
- Indonesia's CPO export volumes falling 30–39% early April — do not add AALI, LSIP, or agri exposure until exports recover.

### Plan for /market-open (Monday 2026-04-21)

- [ ] Monitor IHSG open direction: if gap-up or flat open, proceed with limit orders; if gap-down >0.5%, hold fire and reassess at 09:30 WIB
- [ ] BBRI: Place limit BUY at IDR 3,430 (or best offer within IDR 3,400–3,460 range). Position size: IDR 60,000,000 (20% of account). Note this is the ex-dividend date — confirm open price adjustment and adjust limit accordingly. Stop at IDR 3,190.
- [ ] ANTM: Place limit BUY at IDR 3,940 if stock holds above IDR 3,900 support at open. Position size: IDR 60,000,000 (20%). Stop at IDR 3,664. Do not chase above IDR 4,050.
- [ ] ITMG: Watch first 30 minutes of trading. If price holds IDR 27,000–27,200 range, place limit BUY at IDR 27,050. Position size: IDR 60,000,000 (20%). Stop at IDR 25,157.
- [ ] Do NOT open all 3 on the same day if market is weak at open — prioritize BBRI (highest conviction) first, ANTM second, ITMG third.
- [ ] Set 10% trailing stops on all filled positions immediately after execution.
- [ ] Cap total deployed capital at IDR 180,000,000 (3 positions × 20%) — leave IDR 120,000,000 in reserve.

### Top 3 Candidates

#### 1. ITMG — Massive Q1 earnings beat (114% above estimates) + coal above $130

- Thesis: ITMG printed Q1 2026 EPS of IDR 890 vs consensus IDR 416 (114% beat) while trading at 9.4x P/E with 11% dividend yield — the market has not re-rated the stock to reflect this earnings power.

#### 3. ANTM — HPM nickel ore price reform = structural margin expansion

- Thesis: Nickel at $17,200–17,635 (multi-week high) combined with HPM reform = meaningfully higher Q2 realization prices vs Q1, a sequential earnings upgrade.
- Catalyst: Full-quarter impact of Decree 144 in Q2 2026 results; gold production target +25.8% YoY as secondary driver.
- Proposed entry: IDR 3,900–3,960 | Stop: IDR 3,627–3,683 (-7%) | Target: IDR 4,600 (+16.5%)
- Conviction: **MEDIUM-HIGH**

### Flagged Risks

- **IDR weakness** (17,115): BI Governor said rate cut scope is "increasingly limited" — removes near-term re-rating catalyst for rate-sensitive sectors.

- **BBRI foreign selling**: IDR 1.42T net foreign sell on BBRI in Apr 6–10; institutional buying is a counterbalance but the outflow is a weight.
- **Ex-div gap BBRI**: Stop must be placed from ex-div adjusted open price, not pre-div close.
- **CPO sector**: Do not add any agri/CPO exposure — exports -34% with no recovery signal.

### Top 3 Candidates

#### 1. ITMG — 114%-beat earnings + coal above $130 = deep-value re-rating

- Thesis: ITMG printed Q1 2026 EPS IDR 890 vs consensus IDR 416 — a 114% earnings beat — while still trading at ~9.4x P/E with 11% dividend yield. The market has not re-rated to reflect this earnings power, and the coal price floor ($130+) is intact.
- Thesis: Newcastle coal at $131–134 is holding above the $130 support level with a Middle East risk premium embedded; any analyst upgrade ahead of the May 7 official print could trigger rapid multiple expansion.
- Catalyst: Q1 EPS beat already public; May 7 official Q1 results filing; potential analyst upgrades in coming days.
- Proposed entry: IDR 27,000–27,200 | Stop: IDR 25,110 (10% trailing GTC) | Target: IDR 31,500 (+16.4%)
- Conviction: **HIGH** (upgraded — beat now confirmed via search)

#### 2. BBRI — Ex-dividend April 21 + institutional accumulation + Q1 earnings April 29

- Thesis: BBRI goes ex-dividend Monday April 21 (IDR 209/share cash, 10.1% annual yield); BlackRock added +19M shares and JPMorgan +680K shares in April — institutional accumulation against the foreign outflow trend.
- Thesis: Q1 2026 earnings release April 29 is the re-rating event; strong micro/SME loan growth and BI stability expected to support beat; US-Iran ceasefire reduces EM risk premium and benefits foreign flows back into Indonesia.
- Catalyst: Ex-dividend April 21 (stock adjusts down ~IDR 209 at open); Q1 earnings April 29.
- Proposed entry: IDR 3,200–3,250 (ex-div adjusted open) | Stop: IDR 2,990 (10% trailing GTC) | Target: IDR 3,700 (+14–16%)
- Conviction: **HIGH**

#### 3. ANTM — HPM nickel reform at $17,985/ton = structural margin upgrade not yet in price

- Thesis: ESDM Decree 144 (effective April 15) raised the nickel ore correction factor from 17% to ~30% for ANTM's 18.1M wmt of production — a structural margin expansion worth hundreds of billions in incremental EBITDA per quarter, not yet reflected in consensus estimates.
- Thesis: LME nickel now at $17,985 (+4.81% MoM, upgraded from prior $17,635 estimate) means both the HPM formula change AND the underlying nickel price are working in ANTM's favour simultaneously.
- Catalyst: First full-quarter impact of Decree 144 in Q2 2026 results (reported ~August); stock rose +1.03% to IDR 3,940 on Apr 15 announcement but has not fully discounted Q2 earnings impact.
- Proposed entry: IDR 3,900–3,960 | Stop: IDR 3,628–3,683 (10% trailing GTC) | Target: IDR 4,600 (+16.5%)
- Conviction: **MEDIUM-HIGH**

### Flagged Risks

- **BI rate cut scope shrinking**: Governor explicitly said cuts are "increasingly limited" due to IDR weakness and Middle East capital outflows. Removes near-term re-rating tailwind for BBRI and property; do not add property exposure.
- **Monday risk-ON vs Friday risk-OFF**: Asian markets sold off Friday (Nikkei -1.75%, HSI -1%); BUT US-Iran 2-week ceasefire announced with Strait of Hormuz reopening → Monday may gap UP on risk-on. Watch IHSG open vs 7,600 level.
- **BBRI ex-div gap**: Stock will open ex-div Monday (-IDR 209 adjustment). Entry price target shifts from IDR 3,430 to IDR 3,200–3,250. Stop set from actual fill, not pre-div price.
- **Foreign outflow weight**: Net foreign sell IDR 3.31T week of Apr 6–10; IDR 37.14T YTD. Persistent headwind on large-caps; any EM risk-off event could amplify selling.
- **CPO sector**: Exports -34–39% with no recovery signal — zero exposure to AALI, LSIP, or agri plays.
- **3-trade weekly limit**: All 3 trades planned for Monday April 21 would hit the weekly cap immediately. Priority: ITMG (highest conviction) → BBRI → ANTM.

- **Tuesday April 21**: BBRI opens EX-DIV (~IDR 3,211–3,221, adjusted −IDR 209). Stop placed from Tuesday's actual open.

### Top 3 Candidates

#### 1. ITMG — 114% Q1 earnings beat + coal $131 (HIGH)
- Thesis: ITMG printed IDR 890 EPS vs IDR 415 consensus (114% beat); still trading 9.4× P/E with 11% yield — market has not re-rated. Coal at $131 is above breakeven with a floor intact.
- Thesis: May 7 official Q1 results filing and likely analyst upgrades will force multiple expansion. Stock at 27,050 is still in the buy range.
- Catalyst: May 7 Q1 earnings official print; analyst upgrade cycle already underway.
- Entry: IDR 27,000–27,200 | Stop: IDR 25,110 (10% GTC) | Target: IDR 31,500 (+16.4%)
- Conviction: **HIGH**

#### 2. BBRI — Cum-div capture Monday + Q1 earnings Apr 29 (HIGH)
- Thesis: Buy Monday April 20 at cum-div price ~IDR 3,420–3,430 to capture IDR 209 dividend ex-div Tuesday; BlackRock +19M shares and JPMorgan +680K in April = institutional accumulation against the foreign outflow tide.
- Thesis: Q1 earnings April 29 is the re-rating catalyst; strong micro/SME lending trends expected to produce beat.
- Catalyst: Ex-dividend April 21; Q1 earnings April 29.
- Entry: IDR 3,380–3,440 (cum-div Monday) | Stop: IDR 3,190 (−7%) | Target: IDR 3,900 (+13–15%)
- Conviction: **HIGH**

#### 3. ANTM — HPM structural re-rating at entry support (MEDIUM-HIGH)
- Thesis: ESDM Decree 144 (Apr 15) raised ANTM ore correction factor 17%→30%; 18.1M wmt quota means hundreds of billions IDR in incremental quarterly EBITDA not yet in consensus. Stock at 3,900 is at support.
- Thesis: ANTM +4% today and +4.29% WoW shows momentum confirming the HPM thesis; nickel at $17,635 adds commodity tailwind. Q2 is first full-quarter impact.
- Catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (~August) first full HPM quarter; gold production +25.8% YoY secondary driver.
- Entry: IDR 3,900–3,960 | Stop: IDR 3,628 (10% GTC) | Target: IDR 4,600 (+16.5%)
- Conviction: **MEDIUM-HIGH**

### Flagged Risks

- **ANTM already at lower bound**: At 3,900 today. If it opens Monday at or above 3,960, do NOT buy. Gap above 4,050 = skip.
- **BBRI ex-div gap Tuesday**: Entry Monday is cum-div; stock drops ~IDR 209 at Tuesday open. Stop must be placed from Tuesday's actual adjusted open, not Monday's cum-div price.
- **Monday gap-up**: US-Iran ceasefire may spike IHSG to 7,700+. Stick to limit orders — do not chase.
- **Weekly limit exhausted on Day 1**: All 3 trades on Day 1 (if conditions allow) = 3/3 weekly cap used. No flexibility rest of week. Accept this as the correct approach given all three ideas have HIGH/MEDIUM-HIGH conviction.
- **Coal drifting**: Newcastle down from $133.55 → $131. Still above $130 floor. Watch for a break below $130 — would weaken ITMG thesis materially.

- [ ] **ITMG** (Priority 1 — HIGH): BUY limit IDR 27,000–27,200. Size IDR 59,400,000 (~19.8%). Stop: 10% GTC immediately (floor IDR 25,110). Target IDR 31,500. Max chase IDR 27,800.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6)

#### 1. BBRI — Ex-div today, Q1 earnings Apr 29, re-rating setup

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: BBRI's dividend yield 10.13% underpins valuation; ex-div mechanical drop IDR 209 from cum-price 3,340 opens a cleaner entry window around 3,131.
- Thesis: Q1 2026 earnings April 29 — BI rate hold preserves NIM; micro loan growth resilient; prior beats (+6.9% EPS, +13.52% revenue surprise history).
- Valuation: P/B ~1.9x vs big-bank median; yield cushion on downside.
- Catalyst: Ex-div Apr 21 (today); Q1 results Apr 29. Two discrete catalysts within 8 trading days.
- Analyst view: 40 analysts cover; broad buy bias on dividend + earnings story.
- Insider activity: No notable recent director/commissioner transactions surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 3,340 cum (post-ex implied ~3,131) | 52w range includes 3,000s
- Volume: high on ex-date absorption; broad market sold BBRI -4.7% intraday
- Support: IDR 3,069 (prior weekly review trailing-stop ref) | Resistance: IDR 3,410 (prior high)
- Trend: range-bound; sideways consolidation between 3,100–3,410

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 3,130–3,180 (on confirmed post-ex open; no chase above 3,200)
- Hard cut: IDR 2,911 (-7% from 3,130) | Target: IDR 3,500 (+12%, 2:1 R/R to 3,420)
- Risk: per risk-based sizing, (0.005 × 10,020,475,000) / (3,130 − 2,911) × 3,130 ≈ 715,000,000 → capped at 15% regime max = IDR 1.503B
- Position size candidate: ~400,000 shares ≈ IDR 1.252B (12.5% of equity) — well under 15% regime cap
- Conviction: HIGH (two near-term catalysts, dividend yield support, banking sector regime-favoured)

#### 2. ANTM — HPM decree nickel rally, sector momentum

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: HPM decree (effective Apr 15) revises nickel pricing mechanism in producer favour; ANTM is Indonesia's largest nickel producer.
- Thesis: Gold production target 935kg (+25.8% YoY) + nickel 18.1Mt (+12.7% YoY) = dual-commodity leverage.
- Valuation: Historical P/B mid-cycle; riding structural re-rating.
- Catalyst: HPM flow-through into Q2 reported margins; Q1 earnings window approaching.
- Analyst view: Mixed; recent price-target cluster 3,600–4,500.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 3,900 (+4.00%) | Researcher ceiling prior session: 3,960 (do-not-chase line)
- Volume: elevated on nickel breakout
- Support: IDR 3,663 (prior weekly stop ref) | Resistance: IDR 4,070 (Apr high)
- Trend: uptrend; gold-nickel dual tailwind

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 3,800–3,870 (wait for pullback; no chase above 3,900)
- Hard cut: IDR 3,534 (-7%) | Target: IDR 4,300 (+11%, 2:1 R/R)
- Position size candidate: ~350,000 shares ≈ IDR 1.365B (13.6% of equity) — under 15% cap
- Conviction: MEDIUM (strong momentum but entry already extended; price has already moved; chase risk)

#### 3. INCO — Vale nickel breakout, EV supply-chain leverage

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: INCO is Indonesia's largest pure-nickel producer; Q3 2025 matte production +4% QoQ; 2026 focus on reliability + cost baseline.
- Thesis: HPM decree and LME stability together support margin recovery.
- Valuation: Below mid-cycle; turnaround story on operational discipline.
- Catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings release window; LME nickel follow-through.
- Analyst view: Covered by major houses; recent neutral-to-positive.

**Technical context:**

- Volume: strong on breakout

- Trend: uptrend

- Hard cut: IDR 5,022 (-7% from 5,400) | Target: IDR 6,000 (+11%)

- Conviction: MEDIUM (momentum solid; correlated with ANTM — don't size both full)

- Reasoning: IDR 17,128 near record lows; BI held Apr 15; foreign net sell continues; VIX +9% spike on Iran tensions. No trigger to upgrade. Nickel/coal sector strength is commodity-specific, not a broad regime shift.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. BBRI — Post-ex-div recovery with Q1 earnings catalyst Apr 29

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: BBRI is Indonesia's largest retail/micro lender with post-ex-dividend absorption complete. The recent dividend drop created a mechanical entry window at 3,220, and the stock has recovered to 3,480. Institutional accumulation (BlackRock, JPMorgan) provides support.
- Thesis: Q1 2026 earnings Apr 29 is the next re-rating event — 2026 guidance is NIM 7.4-7.8%, loan growth 7-9%, CIR 41-43%. A beat extends the bounce.
- Valuation: Q4 2025 EPS 105.13 (beat 98.34); trading ~2.1x P/B vs sector 2.3x.
- Catalyst: BI rate decision TODAY (Apr 22) — dovish surprise would push BBRI higher; hold at 4.75% already priced in.
- Analyst view: Consensus buy; TP range IDR 4,400-5,000.
- Insider activity: No material director transactions noted recently.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 3,480 | 50-day MA est: ~3,300 | 200-day MA est: ~3,450
- Volume: broadly normal on ex-div recovery
- Support: IDR 3,270 (ex-div low) | Resistance: IDR 3,640 (pre-ex high)
- Trend: recovering from pullback; reclaimed 50-day MA

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 3,400-3,480 (current range) — do NOT chase above 3,510
- Hard cut: IDR 3,236 (-7%) | Target: IDR 3,780 (+8.6%)
- R:R: 1.23:1 — TIGHT. Marginal pass under 2:1 standard; prefer tighter entry
- Position size candidate: ~450,000 shares ≈ IDR 1.566B (15.7% equity) — flag: above regime 15% cap
- Adjust: cap at 425,000 shares ≈ IDR 1.479B (14.8%)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (catalyst near but R:R tight at current price)

#### 2. ANTM — HPM decree sustain; pullback provides better entry

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: ANTM is Indonesia's integrated nickel+gold miner. The HPM Decree 144 effective Apr 15 raises ore correction factor from 17% to 30% and adds Co/Fe/Cr to the HPM formula, lifting limonite ore HPM from ~$18/wmt to $35-40/wmt. ANTM 2026 production quota raised to 18.1M wmt (from 16.4M).
- Thesis: Dual tailwind — nickel ASP lift via HPM + gold price record highs support hedge.
- Valuation: Mid-cycle; integrated model supports premium to pure miners.
- Catalyst: HPM implementation updates; Q1 earnings date TBD.
- Analyst view: Upgrades from multiple houses post-HPM.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 3,960 (pulled back from 4,070 high)
- Volume: elevated on HPM news
- Support: IDR 3,800 | Resistance: IDR 4,100
- Trend: uptrend; pullback to 38% retrace provides re-entry window

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 3,850-3,960 (better than prior research's 3,800-3,870 range — can execute now if BI dovish)
- Hard cut: IDR 3,580 (-7% from 3,850) | Target: IDR 4,300 (+11%)
- R:R: 2.1:1 ✓
- Position size candidate: ~380,000 shares ≈ IDR 1.463B (14.6% equity) — within 15% regime cap
- Conviction: MEDIUM-HIGH (HPM tailwind durable; entry acceptable)

#### 3. INCO — Pure nickel beneficiary

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Largest pure-nickel producer on IDX; HPM lift directly benefits ASP.
- Thesis: LME nickel range-bound $18K but domestic pricing mechanism now decoupling upward.
- Valuation: Below mid-cycle; turnaround on operational discipline.
- Catalyst: Q1 earnings window.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 6,800 (+5.84% recent session)
- Support: IDR 6,400 | Resistance: IDR 7,000
- Trend: breakout continuation; risk of short-term exhaustion

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 6,500-6,700 (wait for pullback)
- Hard cut: IDR 6,045 (-7%) | Target: IDR 7,300 (+9%)
- R:R: 1.3:1 — TIGHT
- Position size candidate: ~180,000 shares ≈ IDR 1.170B (11.7%)
- Conviction: LOW-MEDIUM (correlated with ANTM; if picking, choose ANTM)

#### 4. BMRI — Pre-BI banking play; lower urgency than BBRI

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Largest-cap Indo bank; stable ROE ~20%; dividend support ~5%.
- Catalyst: Q1 earnings mid-to-late April; BI decision today.
- Valuation: 2.3x P/B; in line with sector.

**Technical:** Price IDR 4,730; stable.

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: 4,650-4,730
- Hard cut: 4,325 (-7%) | Target: 5,050 (+7%)
- R:R: 1:1 — INSUFFICIENT. Below 2:1 threshold — SKIP.
- Conviction: LOW (prefer BBRI as banking proxy)

#### 5. MBMA — Nickel ore, debt maturity watch

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Ore exposure benefits HPM uplift; smelter arm potentially pressured.
- Catalyst: Apr 22 debt maturity event — monitor for rollover/refinance news.

**Trade plan:** SKIP until debt event clarity. WATCH only.

### Held Position Updates

- **ITMG**: Last IDR 26,575 (entry 26,075; +1.92% unrealised ≈ IDR +13.65M). Thesis intact: Newcastle coal $132.30 above $130 support; Chinese thermal spot cargo volumes at decade-high March print. Stop at IDR 24,250 (State 1 hard-cut; +7% threshold 27,900 not yet reached). Overnight news: none adverse. Technical: trading near 50-day MA proxy (~22,430 — note low reference); well above 200-day. Broker quote stale (returns entry price); web search indicates 26,575 Apr 21. **Action: HOLD.** No stop adjustment (below +7%).

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: EM OUTFLOW / RISK-CAUTIOUS (unchanged from Apr 19)**
- Reasoning: IDR 17,128 near record lows; foreign net sell continues (1.23B shares Apr 21); IHSG down 2 consecutive days; BI expected to hold at 4.75% today (Apr 22) — no dovish trigger to upgrade regime. HPM decree + Chinese coal imports surge are commodity-specific tailwinds, not broad regime shifts. VIX 19.50 +3.34%, Iran ceasefire expiration risk lingers.
- Position sizing adjustment: max position 15% of equity (down from 20% default); prefer near-term-catalyst ideas.
- Potential regime upgrade trigger: BI dovish surprise (unlikely), sustained foreign inflow reversal, or IDR break below 17,000.

### Flagged Risks

- **BI hawkish surprise** — if BI hikes or signals prolonged hold with no cut path, banking/property gap down.
- **US-Iran ceasefire expiration** — Brent could spike, VIX +; IDX sensitive on consumer, import-heavy.
- **IDR 17,200+ breakout** — would pressure all IDR-denominated paper; foreign sell could accelerate.
- **Foreign flow persistence** — GOTO/BUMI unrelenting sell; speculative beta names vulnerable.
- **ANTM/INCO correlation** — picking both doubles nickel risk; pick one.
- **BBRI R:R tight at current price** — chasing above 3,510 degrades the trade.

### Plan for /market-open (2026-04-22)

- [ ] **HOLD ITMG.** Stop unchanged at 24,250 (State 1 hard-cut). No action.
- [ ] **ANTM — BUY conditional** on pullback to IDR 3,850-3,960 (current range acceptable). Size 380,000 shares ≈ IDR 1.463B (14.6% equity). Hard cut 3,580. Target 4,300. Weekly trades 1/3 → 2/3. Skip if price gaps above 4,050 (chase risk) or below 3,780 (thesis break).
- [ ] **BBRI — BUY conditional** on entry IDR 3,400-3,480 (current range). Size 425,000 shares ≈ IDR 1.479B (14.8% equity). Hard cut 3,236. Target 3,780. R:R marginal — stricter entry required. Skip if price >3,510 (chase).
- [ ] **INCO — WAIT** for pullback to 6,500-6,700. Skip if ANTM filled (correlation).
- [ ] **BMRI — SKIP.** R:R 1:1, insufficient.
- [ ] **MBMA — WATCH** for Apr 22 debt maturity news; no trade until clarity.
- [ ] **BI Rate decision TODAY (Apr 22 afternoon)** — hold at 4.75% expected. If dovish surprise: banking stocks + 1-3% re-rate; consider adding BBRI if not yet filled. If hawkish: reduce size by 25% on any pending entries.
- [ ] **No new coal, property, consumer, telco, tech, construction.** Regime avoid list.
- [ ] If no fills by 11:30: reassess at midday; no-trade day acceptable.

### Macro Snapshot

- **IHSG into Week 2 with technical damage**: Friday closed 7,378 (−1.49%), week −3.35%. No Apr 27 print available pre-open. Foreign flow remained net-sell into Friday close.
- **Rupiah backing off slightly**: 17,174 vs 17,322 high — first sign of stabilization but still in EM-stress range. Insufficient to flip regime.

- **BBRI execution risk at May 2 open:** Stop is GTC but paper-mode broker did not auto-execute on Apr 30 intraday breach. Must manually execute SELL at open regardless of pre-market price action. If BBRI gaps up at open, sell anyway — the rule is the rule.
- **ITMG narrowing buffer:** −4.12% from entry, −3.00% from hard-cut. An IHSG continuation lower on May 2 could test the hard-cut. Pre-commit: if ITMG breaches 24,250 intraday, EXIT per rule (do not wait for May 7 print).
- **IDR record at 17,390:** Intervention risk both directions. A coordinated BI defense could move banks/consumer; a runaway move could pressure ITMG (stronger USD = stronger coal revenue, but volatility risk).
- **Final-day eagerness:** Strongest cumulative alpha (+8.88%) sits at the desk going into the last trading day. Forcing a "victory lap" trade is the textbook destruction-of-edge move. Resist.
- **Coal sustainability:** $137.80 is one print; need confirmation. May 2 print direction matters for ITMG hold-through-trial-close decision (currently HOLD).
- **IDX closed today:** No new market signal until May 2 open. Pre-market plan locked.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. ITMG — HELD coal carry into May 7 Q1 print

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Indonesia's premium-grade coal exporter; 2025 FY profit US$190.94M; cheap on yield/PE relative to global peer; coal-floor restored at $130+ (Newcastle).
- Thesis: May 7 Q1 print is the residual catalyst from the trial-window setup; current Apr 30 mark 25,000 is below entry 26,075 (-4.12%), within 3.00% of hard-cut 24,250.
- Valuation: trailing earnings 890.23 IDR/sh last quarter (114% surprise vs 415.80 est); P/E mid-single-digit; 11%+ dividend yield.
- Catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings release Thu May 7 (sectors.app/marketscreener calendar); coal price floor confirmation (Newcastle $137.80 May 1 → $133.90 May 1 update suggests softer print into May 4).
- Analyst view: Consensus BUY (6 buy / 6 hold / 2 sell of 13 covering analysts).
- Insider activity: none noted.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 25,000 (Apr 30 last verified close) | 50-day MA: ~26,500 (rolling) | 200-day MA: estimated ~25,800
- Volume: avg ~1.5M sh/day (broker.sh) — adequate liquidity
- Support: 24,250 (hard-cut zone); 23,500 (deeper swing)
- Resistance: 26,075 (entry); 27,900 (+7% trail trigger)
- Trend: range-bound with downside bias; testing lower-50 area
- Stop unchanged: hard-cut 24,250 (3.00% buffer)

**Trade plan (HOLD-only, no add):**
- Entry: already 26,075 | Stop: 24,250 | Target: not set (carry through May 7)
- R:R from current mark 25,000: ~3.00% downside to stop vs ~10%+ upside to entry-recovery
- Position size: 7.18% of equity (within 15% regime cap, within 20% per-position cap)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (coal floor intact; tape pressuring; print 3 days out)

Indo 10Y SUN ratcheted to 6.83% (+50bp+ vs Apr 29 print). US fresh records but Asia-EM disconnect persists. Coal floor and nickel range-bound supportive of commodity bias; banks remain the foreign-sell target. Position sizing on hypothetical new entries: max 15% per RISK-STATE.json (no relaxation). Cash buffer: 92.84% (post-BBRI exit) — appropriate. Defensive Quality track suspended for post-trial period (no new entries planned).

### Flagged Risks

- **ITMG narrowing buffer:** −4.12% from entry, only −3.00% from hard-cut 24,250. A continuation of Apr 30 IHSG weakness on today's open could test the cut. Pre-commit: rule-enforced exit on intraday breach, no waiting for May 7 print.
- **IHSG follow-through risk:** Apr 30 −2.03% close + weekend gap risk; first-print May 4 could gap either way. Watch for foreign-flow re-acceleration on the open.
- **IDR fragility at 17,310:** Below the 17,390 record but still in intervention-watch zone. A coordinated BI defense or runaway move could whip Banking and Consumer.
- **Coal price softening:** Newcastle $133.90 (May 1) down from $137.80 (May 1 prior intraday). Need confirmation that $130 floor holds or ITMG thesis re-tests.
- **Post-trial discipline:** Trial window closed Apr 30 (effective). Today is post-trial — no new entries; preserve realized P&L; HOLD ITMG only.
- **Data infra:** market-data.sh / yfinance still blocked; relying on WebSearch with potential lag. Multi-source verification mandatory if ITMG approaches hard-cut intraday (per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-01 lesson).

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. MEDC — Brent shock + record-high 2026 production guidance

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Pure-play Indonesian E&P, 165–170 kboepd 2026 production (+5.8–9% YoY); EBITDA estimated +20% in 2026.
- Thesis: Brent +5.80% to USD 114.44 on Iran/UAE missile incident — geopolitical premium reset; UBS sensitivity model implies 16–19% EPS uplift per 10% Brent move.
- Valuation: P/E mid-single-digit on consensus 2026 EPS; book multiple ~0.6× per sectors.app summary; below sector median.
- Catalyst: Brent shock TODAY (May 4) — hargasaham.id explicitly named MEDC and ENRG as oil-rally beneficiaries; geopolitical de-escalation timeline indeterminate.
- Analyst view: 11 BUY / 1 SELL / 0 HOLD; UBS PT raised IDR 2,000 → 2,570 (Apr); average 12-mo target IDR 1,838.64 (+40% upside from 1,915 base).
- Insider activity: None surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 1,915 (latest WebSearch quote; Apr 23 print 1,755 +3.24%) | 50-day MA: estimated ~1,720 | 200-day MA: estimated ~1,650
- Volume: ADV not directly observable via blocked yfinance. **Cannot fire gate-check Gate 7 (ADV>500k) or Gate 14 (≤10% ADV) without verifiable ADV — would need market-data fix or external lookup before any actual buy.**
- Support: IDR 1,755 (Apr 23 reaction low); deeper IDR 1,600 (Apr 6)
- Resistance: IDR 2,000 (round + prior UBS PT); IDR 2,200 (analyst high)
- Trend: Uptrend on news; price has already pushed above 50-day MA

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR ≤ 1,915 (no chase >3% per Gate 9 = max IDR 1,972) | Stop: IDR 1,781 (−7% hard cut) | Target: IDR 2,200 (+15% to first analyst high) | R:R: ~2.1:1
- Position size (risk-based @ regime cap): `(0.0050 × 9,940,600,000) / (1,915 − 1,781) × 1,915` = ~711,000 sh ≈ IDR 1.36B = 13.7% of equity. **Cap at regime max 15% per RISK-STATE.json.** Round to lots (100): 711,000 sh.
- Conviction: HIGH (catalyst landed today, alignment of macro + analyst flow + Brent +5.8% concrete number)
- ADV: **UNKNOWN — gate-check Gate 7 + 14 cannot be satisfied without verifiable ADV. Pre-trade requirement: surface ADV via market-data wrapper fix or accept skip.**

**Pre-mortem (5-day invalidation events to monitor):**
- Iran-US de-escalation announcement → Brent retraces below $105 → thesis loses geopolitical premium.
- IDR breaks 17,400 sustained → BI emergency response → broad equity de-risking nullifies oil bid.
- MEDC pre-announces Q1 production miss before earnings.

**Intermediate pain (-4% / IDR 1,838):**
- If thesis intact (Brent stays ≥ $108 and no Iran de-escalation): HOLD.
- If Brent retraces to $100–$105: REASSESS — partial trim 50% acceptable.
- If Brent < $100 and IDR > 17,400: COVER fully (don't wait for hard cut).

### Held Position Updates

- **ITMG**: Current price IDR 25,475 (May 4 close, latest verified via WebSearch search hit), unrealized −2.30% from entry 26,075. Thesis INTACT — Newcastle coal $133.90 holds the $130 floor, Q1 2026 earnings May 7 (T−2) is the binary catalyst, BCA Sek BUY PT 30,100. Stop unchanged: hard-cut 24,250 (4.81% buffer). Hard-cut state still active (below +7% trail trigger 27,900). Overnight news: none ITMG-specific; broader Brent shock is positive sentiment spillover for coal/energy complex. Technical: Below 50-day MA; range-bound. **Action: HOLD.** Pre-commit: if intraday May 5 prints < 24,250 → EXIT per rule.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: EM-OUTFLOW / RISK-CAUTIOUS (unchanged; with new oil-shock overlay).**

**Reasoning:** Foreign sell continued through Apr 30 (−IDR 3.31T week); MSCI Indonesia rebalancing FROZEN (BREN/DSSA at risk; broader country weight cut threatened); IDR 17,335 still elevated above 17,000 trigger; BI reserves at $148.2B (lowest since Jul 2024). Counter-positive: April CPI 2.42% (down from 3.48% — major dovish surprise) reopens BI rate-cut runway and boosted IHSG +0.22% May 4. Brent +5.80% on Iran-UAE incident is a transient geopolitical premium overlay — supportive of energy/coal complex but inflation-pressuring for IDR if sustained. **Net regime call: unchanged (EM-OUTFLOW), with overlay favoring oil & gas (MEDC, PGAS) and coal (ITMG hold) over banks/property/consumer.**

**Position sizing adjustment:** max 15% per RISK-STATE.json (no relaxation). Defensive Quality track suspended (no qualifying valuation × σ data accessible).

### Flagged Risks

- **Brent geopolitical premium fragility:** Iran-US/UAE incident is the proximate driver. A de-escalation announcement could compress Brent back below $105 in 24h, killing the MEDC catalyst before any entry decision compounds.
- **MSCI rebalancing risk:** Country weight cut decision in May; broad LQ45 mega-cap selling pressure if confirmed. Banks (BBCA/BMRI/BBRI), TLKM, ASII most exposed.
- **IDR fragility at 17,335:** Brent shock will pressure import bill; if IDR breaks 17,400 sustained, BI emergency response could whip equities — particularly banks and consumer.
- **ITMG buffer narrowing:** Currently 4.81% from hard-cut. Q1 earnings May 7 binary risk — pre-commit to exit on hard-cut breach intraday.
- **Sector exit watch:** Banking has 1 strike (BBRI hard-cut May 1). One more banking failure → sector exit rule fires.
- **Data infra:** market-data.sh/yfinance still blocked since Apr 21. **Hard constraint:** ADV unverifiable for fresh entries — gate-check Gate 7 + Gate 14 cannot fire on most names. ITMG ADV is the only verified figure (1.5M sh).
- **Trial-window status:** Apr 20 – May 2 trial window closed. Today May 5 is Day 12 (post-trial). Weekly review filed Fri May 1 16:00 WIB. Per ITMG carry decision, position remains open into Q1 print (May 7).

- [ ] **MEDC: deferred — ADV verification required before any entry.** Catalyst is genuine and time-sensitive (Brent shock); but gate-check Gate 7/14 cannot satisfy without ADV. Surface ADV via fix or skip cleanly with reason logged. **Do NOT bypass gate-check for "obvious" ideas.**

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. ANTM — Nickel Q1 fundamental beat, score 8 (CANDIDATE)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Aneka Tambang (state-owned diversified miner — ferronickel, gold, bauxite) reported Q1 2026 net income IDR 3.41T (+60% YoY) and revenue IDR 29.32T (+12% YoY). The print materially exceeds typical sector growth rates and validates the LME-nickel-firmness + HPM-policy thesis.
- Thesis: Macro regime explicitly favours Nickel ("HPM policy catalyst"); LME nickel held $19,275 (+0.21%) into broader stability; this is the highest-conviction fundamental beat surfaced in today's scan.
- Valuation: Trades around IDR 3,520 historical reference (Mar 30); 12-month price implied higher post-Q1 print but live close unverified due to data infra.
- Catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings already released (today), still digesting — re-rate runway exists if market under-prices the +60% net.
- Analyst view: Need fresh post-Q1 PTs from BCA/Mandiri Sekuritas/Samuel — not surfaced in WebSearch this morning.
- Insider activity: None surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR ~3,520 historical ref | 50-day MA: n/a (data infra blocked) | 200-day MA: n/a
- Volume: ADV unverifiable via yfinance/GoAPI — **gate-check Gate 7 + Gate 14 cannot fire**
- Support: ~3,400 swing low estimate | Resistance: ~3,800 prior swing high
- Trend: cannot determine technically given data-infra block

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR ~3,500–3,600 (need live verified price first) | Stop: IDR ~3,255 (-7% hard cut placeholder) | Target: IDR ~4,000 (+11–14% upside)
- R:R ratio: ~1.5:1 (provisional — needs live price + ATR confirm)
- Position size: 10–13% of equity provisional; capped at regime max 15%
- Conviction: MEDIUM (Q1 print is genuinely strong; LME nickel supportive; but data-infra block prevents Gate 7/14 verification, and ADV unknown)
- ADV: **UNKNOWN — cannot enter without ADV verification.** Per gate-check rules, position must skip Gate 7 unless live ADV sourced. Action item for /market-open: surface ANTM ADV before any order.

**Action: WATCH-promoted-to-CANDIDATE.** Do not chase blindly. At market-open, verify (a) ANTM live price within 3% of plan, (b) live ADV ≥ 500k shares, (c) sector still green. If all pass, size at 10% (one notch below regime max for first-time verification on a name not previously held).

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: EM-OUTFLOW / RISK-CAUTIOUS (unchanged).**

- Reasoning: Foreign net sell continued IDR 791B even on a green IHSG day (+1.22%) — outflow regime intact. IDR sticky at 17,400 zone; 10Y SUN 6.81%. Q1 GDP 5.61% beat + April CPI 2.42% are cyclically positive but still net dovish for risk assets via BI-cut runway. Brent reversal -5.55% removes Mon's geopolitical premium and re-grounds the energy/coal complex on fundamentals, not war-headlines.
- Position sizing adjustment: max 15% per RISK-STATE.json (no relaxation). Defensive Quality track suspended (no qualifying valuation × σ data accessible — repeat constraint).
- What would shift to CONSTRUCTIVE: foreign-flow inflection (one week of net buy ≥ IDR 1T), IDR break below 17,000, IHSG reclaim 7,200, BI dovish pivot.

### Flagged Risks

- **Foreign net sell continuing despite green tape:** IDR 791B outflow on +1.22% IHSG day = market rebound is domestic-driven only. Sustained foreign sell pressures multi-day rallies.
- **MSCI BREN/DSSA HSC removal in May:** ~$270M tail-outflow risk if executed. LQ45 mega-caps (BBCA, BMRI, BBRI, TLKM, ASII) most exposed to broader weight cut.
- **ITMG Q1 earnings May 7 binary:** Held position with 4.81% buffer to hard-cut. Sector-consensus forecasts EPS -29.5% YoY — the print is downside-skewed.
- **IDR pressure at 17,400:** Brent reversal helps inflation but foreign sell flow + low BI reserves ($148.2B) = persistent rupiah drag. A break above 17,500 sustained could trigger BI emergency response.
- **Banking sector exit watch:** 1 strike (BBRI hard-cut May 1). One more banking failure → sector exit rule fires (close all banking positions). Currently 0 banking exposure, so risk is on re-entry timing.
- **Data infra block (yfinance/GoAPI):** ADV unverifiable for 49 of 50 watchlist tickers (only ITMG broker-verified). Gate-check Gate 7 + Gate 14 cannot fire on any new candidate. **Hard constraint on all market-open entries until ADV verification path restored.** Document if midday infra is restored.
- **Brent reversal volatility:** Mon's +5.80% spike → Tue's -5.55% reversal in 24h. Two-way energy-tape volatility makes oil-sympathy plays (MEDC, PGAS) untradeable until headline noise stabilizes.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. SMGR — Q1 +88.7% net beat, sector reflation confirmed, score 8 (CANDIDATE)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Semen Indonesia (Persero) is the largest cement producer in Indonesia. Q1 2026 net profit Rp 80B (+88.7% YoY), revenue Rp 8.29T (+8.3% YoY), sales volume 8.71M tons. Cost discipline driving operating leverage; net financial costs -35.4% YoY. Maybank switched top pick from INTP to SMGR citing higher exposure to government projects and clearer turnaround trajectory.
- Thesis: Sector reflation confirmed — basic materials sector led IDX with +1.91% on May 6. BI rate-cut runway open (CPI 2.42%), property recovery emerging — both bullish for cement demand.
- Valuation: P/E and P/B vs sector not surfaced today (data infra block). Live price not multi-source verified — last anchor IDR 2,000 May 1 ref. Stock had +4.5% breakout May 5.
- Catalyst: Q1 print already released (~May 4); rerate runway exists if market under-prices the +88.7% net.
- Analyst view: Maybank top pick (BUY); Ciptadana also positive citing IKN demand. Specific PTs not freshly surfaced.
- Insider activity: None surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: ~IDR 2,000 anchor (May 1 ref); +4.5% breakout May 5 implies ~2,090. Live not multi-source verified.
- 50-day MA: n/a (data infra) | 200-day MA: n/a
- Volume: ADV unverifiable via yfinance/GoAPI — **gate-check Gate 7 + Gate 14 cannot fire**. SMGR is LQ45 mega-cap; ADV typically 5-15M shares — credibly liquid but not numerically verified.
- Support: ~1,950 swing low estimate | Resistance: ~2,150-2,200 (post-breakout zone)
- Trend: short-term up (+4.5% breakout on real catalyst)

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR ~2,050 (need live price within 3% of plan per Gate 9) | Stop: IDR ~1,907 (-7% hard cut) | Target: IDR ~2,250 (+10% upside)
- R:R ratio: ~1.4:1 (BELOW 2:1 entry-checklist minimum on these levels)
- Position size: max 15% of equity per regime cap (10-12% recommended for first-time entry)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (real Q1 beat + sector momentum; but R:R weak unless better entry, ADV unverifiable)
- ADV: **UNKNOWN — must verify before order.** Per gate-check Gate 7/14, market-open routine should attempt live ADV via WebSearch or broker quote before any order.

**Action: WATCH-promoted-to-CANDIDATE.** Strongest catalyst-track signal in the scan along with UNVR/AKRA. At market-open: (a) verify live SMGR price within 3% of 2,050 plan, (b) attempt ADV verification, (c) confirm sector still green. Only enter if all three pass AND R:R can be tightened to ≥ 2:1 (would require entry near 1,950 support, which won't trade if momentum continues).

#### 2. UNVR — Q1 +73% net, ice-cream divest dividend pending, score 8 (CANDIDATE)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Unilever Indonesia is the largest consumer staples blue-chip on IDX. Q1 2026 reported net profit +73% YoY to Rp 2.1T (headline), revenue +2.8% YoY to Rp 8.4T. Continuing-operations net income +14.1% YoY to Rp 1.3T (the cleaner read; the +73% headline is inflated by ice-cream divestment proceeds). Net margin expansion 15.1% → 25.4%. Stock surged +8.84% on May 6 on print + dividend hint.
- Thesis: Ice cream divestment dividend distribution decision pending June 2026 EGMS — a clear forward catalyst.
- Valuation: P/E vs sector not freshly surfaced; UNVR has historically been premium-rated (2.5-3x sector). After +8.84% pop the entry is far less attractive.

- Analyst view: UBS upgraded Sell → Neutral (date unconfirmed; pre-print); average 12-month consensus PT IDR 2,381 (high 3,400 / low 1,500); 7 buy / 3 sell. Mixed read.

- Position size: 10-12% of equity (mega-cap, defensive)

- ADV: **UNKNOWN — must verify.** UNVR is large LQ45; ADV typically 10-30M shares — credibly liquid.

#### 3. AKRA — Controlling shareholder ~Rp 282.5B insider buy, score 7 (CANDIDATE)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT AKR Corporindo is a fuel/petroleum/chemicals distributor and infrastructure operator (KEK Gresik industrial estate). Controlling shareholder PT Arthakencana Rayatama (AKRT) executed a 3-tranche purchase totalling 181M shares for Rp 282.526B at IDR 1,520-1,580 over May 4-6, lifting AKRT stake to 64.607%. Stock closed +2.63% to IDR 1,560 on May 6. This is the strongest insider signal surfaced this morning.
- Thesis: Distribution sector quietly bullish — analysts unanimous: 13/0/0 buy ratings, consensus PT IDR 1,605.48 (range 1,363.50 - 1,942.50). 2026 forecast revenue Rp 44.27T, EBITDA Rp 5.03T.
- Valuation: P/E and P/B not freshly surfaced; insider-buying-while-shares-trade-at-IDR-1,520-1,580 is itself an implicit valuation signal from the most-informed party.
- Catalyst: 3-day insider accumulation completed May 6; recommended in Liputan6 May 6 ("Sinyal Kuat Pengendali AKRA").
- Analyst view: 13/0/0 buy/hold/sell consensus; average PT IDR 1,605 (~+3% from current).
- Insider activity: **CONFIRMED** — controlling shareholder Rp 282.5B over 3 days at 1,520-1,580.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 1,560 (+2.63% May 6)
- 50-day MA: n/a (data infra) | 200-day MA: n/a
- Volume: ADV unverifiable; insider transactions of Rp ~94B/day implied liquidity ample on a regular-market basis
- Support: ~1,500 (insider-buy zone) | Resistance: ~1,600 (analyst consensus PT)
- Trend: short-term up; insider-led floor

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 1,560 (Gate 9 tolerance ≤ 1,607 within +3%) | Stop: IDR 1,450 (-7% hard cut) | Target: IDR 1,750 (+12% upside) or insider-PT 1,800 (+15%)
- R:R ratio: ~1.7:1 to consensus PT, ~2.2:1 to upper PT band 1,942 (LOW R:R to consensus, ACCEPTABLE to higher PT)
- Position size: 10-12% of equity (insider catalyst, mid-cap LQ45)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (real insider signal; consensus PT close to current = limited upside without re-rating)
- ADV: **UNKNOWN — must verify before order.** AKRA is LQ45; ADV typically 5-20M shares — credibly liquid.

**Action: WATCH-promoted-to-CANDIDATE.** Insider catalyst is genuine and recent (3-day accumulation, last tranche only yesterday). At market-open: verify (a) live AKRA price within 1,510-1,605 Gate 9 band, (b) ADV verification, (c) sector still flat-or-green. If all pass, size at 10% (one notch under regime max for first-time entry on a name not previously held). Pre-mortem: if controller's buy stops AND price drops below IDR 1,500 within 5 trading days, thesis broken.

### Held Position Updates

- **ITMG**: Mark IDR 26,050 (Yahoo May 6 — needs multi-source confirm at market-open). Unrealized -0.10% from entry 26,075. Buffer to hard-cut 24,250: 6.91% — comfortable. Thesis INTACT — Newcastle coal $130-137 above thesis floor; Q1 2026 earnings release **TODAY (Thu May 7)** binary catalyst; BCA Sek BUY PT 30,100; UBS upgrade Buy. Stop unchanged: hard-cut 24,250 (+6.91% buffer). Below +7% trail trigger 27,900 — hard-cut state still active. Overnight news: ITMG-specific: nothing fresh; broader: Brent crash -6.96% removes oil sympathy but coal not directly correlated; basic materials sector +1.91% led IDX which is supportive. Technical: at 50-day MA ~26,500; range-bound. **Action: HOLD into print today.** Pre-commit triggers as above.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: EM-OUTFLOW / RISK-CAUTIOUS (unchanged).**

- Reasoning: Foreign net sell continues (-IDR 518B May 5; ~6 sessions of net sell). IDR sticky 17,414. 10Y SUN eased to 6.81% from 6.96% — modest constructive flow. Q1 GDP 5.61% beat + April CPI 2.42% = dovish for risk via BI cut runway. UNVR + SMGR + AKRA + ANTM Q1 prints all genuine = catalyst track is open if we can clear gate-checks. Brent crash -6.96% removes geopolitical premium but is net constructive for IDR/CPI.
- Position sizing adjustment: max 15% per RISK-STATE.json (no relaxation). Defensive Quality track suspended (no qualifying valuation × σ data accessible — repeat constraint from yesterday).
- What would shift to CONSTRUCTIVE: foreign-flow inflection (one week of net buy ≥ IDR 1T), IDR break below 17,000, IHSG reclaim 7,200, BI dovish pivot.

### Flagged Risks

- **ITMG Q1 binary TODAY:** Held position 6.91% buffer to hard-cut. Sector consensus EPS -29.5% YoY = downside-skewed. Pre-commit triggers active; intraday discipline mandatory.
- **Foreign net sell streak ~6 sessions:** Even on +1.22% IHSG day (May 5), foreign net sell -IDR 518B. Mega-banks BMRI/BBCA being distributed; BBRI being re-accumulated (asymmetric).
- **MSCI BREN/DSSA HSC removal pending:** ~$270M tail outflow risk; affects LQ45 mega-cap weight (BBCA, BMRI, BBRI, TLKM, ASII most exposed).
- **IDR pressure at 17,414:** Brent crash helps inflation/CPI but foreign sell flow + low BI reserves ($148.2B) = persistent rupiah drag. Break above 17,500 sustained → BI emergency response possible.
- **Banking sector exit watch:** 1 strike (BBRI hard-cut May 1). One more banking failure → sector exit rule. Currently 0 banking exposure.
- **Brent crash -6.96% to $102:** Major oil-complex repricing. MEDC/PGAS theses dead until Brent stabilizes (was 8/3 yesterday for MEDC; downgraded to 3 today).
- **Data infra block (yfinance/GoAPI):** ADV unverifiable for 49 of 50 watchlist tickers (only ITMG broker-verified at 1.5M). Gate-check Gate 7 + Gate 14 cannot fire on any new candidate. **Hard constraint on all market-open entries until ADV verification path restored.** UNVR/SMGR/AKRA are all LQ45 mega-caps with credibly ample ADV but the rule is the rule. Plan: at market-open attempt WebSearch for explicit ADV reading on each prioritized name; if any verifiable, proceed.
- **Chase risk on UNVR:** +8.84% one-day move blocks Gate 9. Wait for pullback.
- **R:R below 2:1 on SMGR/AKRA at current entry levels:** Entry-checklist minimum is 2:1 from hard-cut. Wait for better entry or accept tighter targets if catalyst is fresh.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: EM-OUTFLOW / RISK-CAUTIOUS — but easing on multiple metrics.**

- Reasoning: Foreign net sell streak moderating: −Rp 76.39B May 7 vs −Rp 484B May 6
  vs −Rp 518B May 5 vs −Rp 3T+ Apr 30. **BBRI re-accumulation +Rp 334.8B (top-2 buy)
  contradicts pure-EM-OUTFLOW thesis.** IHSG +1.15% to 7,174.32 broke above 7,200
  intraday for first time since rally start (high 7,207). Indo 10Y eased 6.81%;
  US 10Y dropped 4.35% (−10bp); Asia broadly bid (Hang Seng +1.6%, Shanghai +0.48%,
  Nikkei record). Brent partial recovery removes oil-complex panic. IDR 17,304–17,358
  (Investing) is cleaner than 17,512 (BI Kamis) — directionally improving.
- Position sizing adjustment: max 15% per RISK-STATE.json (no relaxation YET).
  Defensive Quality track suspended (no qualifying valuation × σ data accessible —
  same constraint).
- What would shift to CONSTRUCTIVE: one full week of foreign net buy ≥ Rp 1T,
  IDR break below 17,000 sustained, IHSG holds 7,200 multiple sessions, BI dovish
  signal at May 21–22 meeting.
- Weekly-review (today 16:00 WIB) should evaluate whether to formally update the
  regime label.

### Flagged Risks

- **ITMG Q1 print not yet public:** Held position 6.7–9.7% buffer to hard-cut.
  Coal-complex Q1 prints all positive sets a positive prior, but ITMG-specific
  numbers not yet confirmed. Pre-commit triggers active; intraday discipline mandatory
  at market-open.
- **Coal Newcastle slipped to $132 May 6** (one source) vs $139.7 May 5 (another) —
  source divergence; thesis floor at $130 is not yet breached but proximity warrants
  monitoring. ITMG/PTBA/ADRO theses depend on coal floor holding.
- **Brent partial recovery only:** +0.68% May 7 follows −6.96% May 6. MEDC −5.81%
  sesi I confirms oil-complex repricing. MEDC/PGAS theses dead near-term.
- **Banking sector exit watch (1-strike since BBRI hard-cut May 1):** Re-entering
  BBRI today would put another banking trade at risk of being the second strike,
  triggering sector exit. **Defer banking re-entry to next week post weekly-review.**
- **Data infra block (yfinance/GoAPI):** ADV unverifiable for 49 of 50 watchlist
  tickers; only ITMG broker-verified. **Hard constraint on all market-open entries
  until ADV verification path restored.** PTBA/ADRO/BBRI/AKRA/ANTM/ICBP are all
  LQ45 mega-caps with credibly ample ADV but the rule is the rule.
- **Friday + Weekly-Review day:** No urgency to add positions today. Weekly-review
  at 16:00 WIB evaluates Week 3 grade and may update MACRO-REGIME.md.
- **MSCI BREN/DSSA HSC removal pending:** ~$270M tail outflow risk; affects LQ45
  mega-cap weights this rebalancing.
- **Currency divergence:** USD/IDR Investing 17,304 vs BI Kamis 17,512 — sources
  conflict. Conservative read: 17,400 mid. Break above 17,500 sustained = risk-off
  escalation; break below 17,300 = constructive.
- **Brent–Newcastle spread narrowing:** Coal Brent recovery + nickel firm + IDR
  improving = directionally bullish for Indonesian equity ex-energy. Broad
  constructive macro tape.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. ASII — Foreign net buy +Rp 2.44T cumulative thru May 13, score 7 (CANDIDATE)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Astra International (auto + heavy equip + financial services + agri conglomerate). LQ45 mega-cap. Foreign investors accumulated +Rp 2.44T cumulative through May 13 — the single largest single-name accumulation by foreigners during the MSCI-driven sell-off week. Remains in MSCI Global Standard Index (not affected by rebalancing churn).
- Thesis: Asymmetric flow signal: while banks (BMRI −Rp 1.41T) and big mining names (MDKA, INCO, BREN) bled foreign capital, ASII was bought into weakness. This pattern echoes BBRI re-accumulation May 7 (catalyst-track marker for institutional repositioning).
- Valuation: PE attractive vs 5-year average per typical conglomerate analyst frame (not numerically sourced this cycle).
- Catalyst: foreign flow accumulation is itself the catalyst; defensive carry into BI RDG May 21–22.
- Analyst view: (general buy bias for LQ45 mega-cap conglomerate; specific PT not sourced this cycle).
- Insider activity: (not specifically surfaced).

**Technical context:**
- Price: live mark UNVERIFIED (WebSearch returned MSCI / FX news only this cycle, no clean spot quote).
- 50-day MA / 200-day MA: not surfaced.
- Volume: ADV unverifiable (yfinance blocked); ASII is LQ45 mega-cap, ADV typically 50–100M sh — ample.
- Support / Resistance: not surfaced this cycle.
- Trend: defensive accumulation vs MSCI selloff = relative outperformer.

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: requires live-price verification at /market-open.
- Stop: −7% hard cut from entry.
- Target: +10–12% provisional pending PT.
- R:R: TBD pending entry pricing.
- Position size: 8–10% of equity (defensive carry; lower idiosyncratic-conviction than fundamental Q1 beats).
- Conviction: MEDIUM (flow-driven, not idiosyncratic earnings catalyst).
- ADV: **UNKNOWN — must verify.** ASII is LQ45 mega-cap; ample.

**Action: WATCH-promoted-to-CANDIDATE.** Pre-mortem: if foreign flow reverses (ASII single-name net sell in next 3 sessions) AND price gaps below entry × 0.96 within 5 days, thesis broken.

#### 2. GOTO — First-ever Q1 profit confirmed, score 7 (CANDIDATE)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: GoTo Gojek Tokopedia (Indo super-app, ride-hail/e-commerce/digital). **Historic first Q1 net profit Rp 171B** (vs Q1 2025 net loss −Rp 367B); revenue Rp 5.34T (+26% YoY); EPS Rp 0.24 (vs loss Rp 0.27). Maintained FY EBITDA guidance Rp 3.2–3.4T. This is a structural break-even moment for the franchise.
- Thesis: Post-print stock jumped +7.55% to Rp 57 on Apr 29 (transaction value Rp 235.8B); month +11.76% but YTD still −10.94% (entry zone constructive).
- Valuation: pre-profit comp set difficult; EBITDA Rp 3.2–3.4T guidance vs current market cap implies forward EV/EBITDA in mid-teens — not cheap but improving.
- Catalyst: Q1 print absorbed; next is Q2 print (late July/early August); intermediate driver is continued path-to-FCF disclosures.
- Analyst view: general turn-positive post-print; (specific PT not sourced this cycle).
- Insider activity: (not surfaced).

**Technical context:**
- Price: Rp 57 post-print Apr 29 ref; recent May 11–13 pullback within IHSG drag context — likely 50–55 area; **must verify live**.
- Volume: ADV ample (top IDX tech name; 1B+ sh daily typical).
- Support: ~50 (post-print base) | Resistance: ~62 (Apr breakout high)
- Trend: short-term consolidation post-breakout

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: TBD on live mark at /market-open. If 53–57 range holds: entry plan ~55 with Gate 9 ceiling 56.65 (3% chase).
- Stop: ~51 (−7% hard cut from 55 entry).
- Target: +12–15% to 62 / Rp 64–65.
- R:R: ~1.7–2.1:1.
- Position size: 8–10% of equity (mega-cap-by-share-count but lower-quality-of-earnings vs banks).
- Conviction: MEDIUM (first profit ever; small absolute Rp number; sustainability unproven).
- ADV: ample (LQ45 tech bellwether).

**Action: WATCH-promoted-to-CANDIDATE.** Pre-mortem: if Q2 monthly traffic/GMV data prints negative MoM within next 4 weeks → thesis weakened.

#### 4. AKRA — Insider buy carry-over re-priced lower, score 6 (CANDIDATE)

**Fundamental case:** (refresh)
- Thesis: PT AKR Corporindo. Controller Arthakencana Rayatama Rp 282.5B 3-tranche purchase May 4–6 at 1,520–1,580 (lifted stake to 64.607%). Still the freshest insider catalyst in the universe.
- Catalyst: insider accumulation completed; stock pulled back to 1,455 (May 13 −2.35%) — now **below the insider-buy zone of 1,520–1,580 = pre-mortem trigger T1 PROXIMITY (controller's avg cost ~1,550).**
- Pre-mortem (May 7 carry-over): "if controller's buy stops AND price drops below 1,500 within 5 trading days → thesis broken." Price is now at 1,455 — **below 1,500 trigger by Rp 45 (-3%)**. T+5 trigger window from May 6 controller-buy = expired around May 13. Status ambiguous: trigger fired BUT close to expiry.
- Analyst view: PT 1,605 (avg); consensus 13/0/0 buy.
- Quarterly div Rp 25 / 6.87% yield (annualised).

**Technical context:**
- Price: 1,455 (May 13 close). Below insider-buy floor of 1,520. Trend now down (-2.35% on May 13 plus prior weakness).
- 50-day MA: estimate ~1,500 (insider-buy zone).
- Volume: ADV unverifiable.
- Support: 1,400 (psychological) | Resistance: 1,520 (insider-buy zone) / 1,605 (consensus PT)

- If still considered: entry 1,455 | stop 1,353 (−7%) | target 1,600 (+10%) | R:R ~1.4:1 — sub-threshold.
- Position size: 8% if entered.
- Conviction: LOW-MEDIUM (insider catalyst diluted by pre-mortem trigger).

**Action: CANDIDATE-with-risk.** Sub-trigger price + EM-OUTFLOW + BMRI-driven banking weakness sympathy = lower confidence. **Defer entry; re-evaluate at midday scan.**

### Held Position Updates

- **ITMG**: Mark **24,300 (May 8 post-print close, −4.72%)** → rebounded to ~25,475 (May 11 midday TRADE-LOG) → uncertain mid-week with mining sector −4.43% May 13. **Q1 2026 net US$55.7M = −16% YoY MISS** vs market priors and vs prior-quarter Q4 2025 +114% surprise. This is a **material thesis-break event**. Pre-commit from 2026-05-08 RESEARCH-LOG triggers active:
  1. **Material miss → close on open** (Pre-commit #1 from May 8 RESEARCH-LOG and May 11 midday) — print is confirmed material miss (Investortrust: net US$55.7M vs US$66.42M prior year; volume drop −7% QoQ on rainfall seasonality + GPM compression).
  2. Intraday <24,250 → EXIT (hard-cut). May 8 close 24,300 was within Rp 50 of trigger — proximity high.
  3. Intraday >27,900 → transition to TRAILING. **Not triggered** since print.
  - Position 27,300 sh @ 26,075 entry; estimated current 24,300–25,000 = unrealized −4–7%; buffer to hard-cut 24,250 is 0–3% (very tight).
  - **Action recommended: EXIT on open Monday May 18** per pre-commit. Reason: Q1 print confirmed material miss; coal-complex cluster pattern broken; mining sector −4.43% last full session; IDR at record-high adds EM-OUTFLOW pressure; buffer to hard-cut now ≤3%.
  - Sector exit-watch implications: ITMG is the 2nd coal trade. BBRI was banking (May 1). An ITMG exit on Q1 miss does **NOT** trigger sector-exit (that requires 2 *consecutive losing* trades in same sector; ITMG would be the 1st losing coal trade if exited).
  - Technical: below 50-day MA (26,500); below 200-day MA (25,800). Trend now down.

- **ADRO**: Mark **2,520 (May 13 close, −3% session)**. Position 392,100 sh @ 2,550 entry = unrealized −0.74M = **−0.30% from entry**. Buffer to hard-cut 2,371: (2,520 − 2,371) / 2,520 = **+5.91%** — comfortable but narrower than May 11.
  - Thesis check: Q1 +67% YoY catalyst INTACT (ADRO didn't miss). Newcastle $130 = at floor (not below); ADRO −4% intermediate-pain trigger NOT breached. Brent $109 (well above $95 pre-mortem trigger).
  - Sector context: coal complex pattern broken by ITMG miss BUT ADRO-specific Q1 was a beat — ADRO thesis NOT broken.
  - **Action: HOLD.** If ITMG exits today on Q1-miss pre-commit, ADRO becomes single-name coal exposure (10% of equity = far below 25% cap); no sector-concentration pressure to also sell ADRO.
  - Pre-mortem refresh: still active — Brent <$95 sustained 3 days, Newcastle <$125 sustained, ADRO at −4% intermediate-pain trigger (IDR 2,448; current 2,520 buffer ~3%).
  - Multi-source verification: 2,520 from Investortrust (May 13) + tradingview range consistent.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: EM OUTFLOW / RISK-CAUTIOUS — INTENSIFIED (reverting from "RECOVERING" stance).**

- Reasoning: One of the four "shift to RISK-NEUTRAL" triggers had been partial (foreign-flow inflection May 7). **All four reversed last week:**
  1. IDR — record-high 17,623, broke 17,500 sustained → trigger REVERSED (worse, not better).
  2. BI dovish signal — none surfaced; RDG May 21–22 pending.
  3. Foreign flow — heavy net sell on MSCI rebalancing wave; BMRI alone −Rp 1.41T → trigger REVERSED.
  4. IHSG reclaim 7,200 — failed: closed May 13 at 6,723.32 (−6.6% from 7,168 sesi-I May 8) → trigger REVERSED.
- The "RECOVERING" stance from May 8 is INVALIDATED. Tape voted with index −3.53% and IDR record. Regime reverts to **EM-OUTFLOW / RISK-CAUTIOUS — INTENSIFIED** (one step deeper than May 8 baseline).
- Position sizing adjustment: **drop max position size from 15% to 12%** on any new entry this week. New entries must be defensive sector or have idiosyncratic flow signal (ASII pattern). No new commodity entries this week.
- Risk implication: any move that takes equity drawdown above −5% from peak (currently −1.07%) triggers an additional defensive review.
- Defensive Quality Track suspended (per TRADING-STRATEGY.md: "Regime is not DEFENSIVE" check fails the second the regime would labeled DEFENSIVE). Current label is EM-OUTFLOW / RISK-CAUTIOUS — INTENSIFIED, **not yet DEFENSIVE** but one notch from it. Watch BI RDG May 21–22: hawkish surprise pushes to full DEFENSIVE.
- This warrants a Weekly Review entry refresh on Friday May 22 with new regime label and possible rule change (sizing cap, sector restrictions).

### Flagged Risks

- **ITMG Q1 print confirmed MISS — pre-commit trigger fires at /market-open today.** Position 27,300 sh @ 26,075; current ~24,300–25,000; buffer to hard-cut 24,250 is ≤3%; coal-complex cluster pattern broken by this miss. Estimated realized loss on EXIT: −IDR 30M to −IDR 49M (−4 to −7% on Rp 712M position).
- **IDR record-high 17,623 — EM-OUTFLOW intensified.** Currency stress hurts foreign-flow-sensitive names (banks, MSCI churn names) and consumer-IDR-import names (UNVR, ICBP supply chain). Adds friction to all new entries.
- **IHSG broke −3.53% week ending May 13** — Phintraco testing 6,650–6,700 today; MNC worst-case 6,538–6,711; **support 6,682 then 6,585**. If IDX gaps below 6,682 at open: defensive day, no new entries.
- **Coal Newcastle at $130 floor** (May 11) — proximity to the $130 thesis floor is uncomfortable. If sustained <$125 → ADRO pre-mortem fires (and PTBA/HRUM/BUMI sector ratings cut).
- **LME nickel slipped to $18,580 (May 15)** — below $19K narrative threshold. Hurts ANTM/INCO/NCKL/MBMA group recovery thesis. MDKA −4.88% May 13 + INCO −3.29% confirm sector pressure.
- **US 10Y at 4.59% fresh 1-year high** — keeps Indo-US spread compression noise; hawkish global rate backdrop pressures EM equities and IDR.
- **MSCI rebalancing effective close May 29** — passive outflow Rp 6–9T/name for AMMN/BREN/TPIA/DSSA/CUAN/AMRT. Will weigh on IHSG through end of May. Collateral hits include BSDE (removed from MSCI SmallCap).
- **BI RDG May 21–22** — consensus 4.75% hold but market hyper-sensitive to dovish/hawkish language given IDR record-high. A hawkish surprise on currency-defense would push regime to full DEFENSIVE.
- **Data infra block (yfinance/GoAPI) — Day 28 of blackout.** 48 of 50 ticker prices today are WebSearch-only; ADV unverifiable on all but ITMG/ADRO. Hard constraint on all market-open entries until verification path restored.
- **Trades this week 0/3 used; no urgency to deploy** — patience over activity per Hard Rules. Pre-mortem expected outcome: ITMG exit recovers cash; new entries deferred behind ASII unless live verification at market-open clearly clears all 15 gates.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: ESCALATING — EM-OUTFLOW-INTENSIFIED → likely DEFENSIVE (formal label change pending weekly-review May 22).**

- Reasoning: IDR breached the 17,500 EM-OUTFLOW escalation trigger and pierced 17,700 intraday (record low); IHSG broke the 6,682 May 18 morning support and closed at 6,599.24 (below 6,682); foreign net sell extended into Day 21 of MSCI/FTSE rebal wave (Rp 464B today); ANTM crashed −12% intraday with Rp 315B foreign net-sell on it alone; sector heatmap: basic-mat −8.14%, transport −6.12%, industrial −4.76%, infrastructure −4.16%, healthcare −3.84% — all sectors red. BBCA / TLKM / ASII green is the textbook DEFENSIVE rotation signature, NOT a risk-on reversal.
- Three of the four "shift from EM-OUTFLOW to DEFENSIVE" criteria appear met:
  1. IDR broke 17,500 sustained ✓ (now 17,500+ / intraday 17,700+ record)
  2. IHSG broke key 6,682 support ✓ (closed 6,599.24)
  3. Foreign flow continues net-sell ✓
  4. BI signal awaited — RDG May 21–22 hawkish surprise = full DEFENSIVE confirmation
- Position sizing adjustment: **drop max position size to 10% of equity on any new entry until BI RDG resolves.** Apply RISK-STATE.json max_position_pct edit at next state-write.
- Defensive Quality Track: SUSPENDED per TRADING-STRATEGY.md (regime would be DEFENSIVE one-notch up). No BBCA/BBRI/BMRI/BBNI/TLKM/ASII/UNVR/ICBP/ADRO defensive-track entries this session.
- Risk implication: drawdown from peak now −1.50%; any move that takes it above −5% triggers immediate defensive review.

### Flagged Risks

- **BI RDG May 21–22 hawkish-surprise** — Bloomberg/economist consensus = potential hike to 5.00% from 4.75% to defend rupiah; market hyper-sensitive. Hawkish surprise pushes regime to FULL DEFENSIVE; dovish surprise = rupiah crisis escalation (worse outcome).
- **IDR record-low 17,500+ / 17,700+ intraday** — broke EM-OUTFLOW escalation; capital-outflow accelerating; reserve depletion (BI spent $8.3B Q1; reserves $148.2B end-March). Consumer-import names (UNVR/ICBP raw-material drag), MSCI churn names hurt.
- **IHSG broke 6,682 support — closed 6,599.24** — next support 6,270 / 6,148 (MNC); 6,307–6,379 test target (Bisnis). If gaps below 6,470 (sesi-I low May 18) at open: defensive day, no new entries.
- **Foreign outflow continuation** — Day 21 of MSCI/FTSE wave; passive outflow $1.6–1.8B continues to weigh through May 29 effective close. ANTM −Rp 315B today / AMMN −Rp 149.1B / BREN −Rp 152.5B leading.
- **LME nickel $18,555 below $19K narrative threshold** — nickel sector confirmed bearish; ANTM/INCO/MDKA/NCKL/MBMA all SKIP.
- **Coal Newcastle $131.70 at floor** — proximity to $130 thesis floor uncomfortable; ADRO pre-mortem trigger $125 not yet breached but watch-list.
- **US 10Y at 4.61–4.63% 1-year high** — keeps Indo-US compression noise; hawkish global rate backdrop.
- **Brent $107.71 down −1.42%** but Iran-tension volatility ongoing.
- **Data infra block — Day 29 of yfinance blackout / GoAPI not set.** All ticker prices today WebSearch-only; ADV unverifiable. Hard constraint on any new entry. Multi-source verification protocol per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-01 binding on any near-hard-cut position.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE-confirmed (all 4 shift triggers now met; formal label change pending weekly-review May 22).**

- Reasoning: IDR sustained above 17,500 (intraday 17,752); IHSG broke through 6,470 (sesi-I May 18 low; today's 6,370.68 close is below that AND a 1-year low); foreign net sell Day 22 of MSCI/FTSE rebal wave with extreme −Rp 464B+ intraday; 10 of 11 sectors red (only healthcare green); commodity index −7.30% / energy −6.94% / transportation −6.58%; broad capitulation. The 3 of 4 triggers from yesterday now extended to **4 of 4**: (a) IDR 17,500+ sustained ✓, (b) IHSG below 6,470 / 6,682 ✓✓, (c) foreign continues net sell ✓, (d) BI signal — **decision TODAY** (calendar tightened from May 21–22 to May 19–20 per news); hawkish would confirm DEFENSIVE, hold = continuation crisis. Either outcome = DEFENSIVE.
- Position sizing adjustment: **drop max position size to 10% of equity until DEFENSIVE label resolves** (write to RISK-STATE.json at next state-write). No new entries today regardless.
- Defensive Quality Track: SUSPENDED per TRADING-STRATEGY.md.
- Risk implication: drawdown from peak −1.97%; cumulative trial alpha vs IHSG +14.51% — alpha is path-fragile. If ADRO hard-cuts at 2,371, additional realized −IDR 70.2M (−0.71% of equity), drawdown widens to ~−2.7% from peak; well above −15% hard limit but bears watching.

### Flagged Risks

- **BI RDG decision TODAY (May 20)** — 16/29 economists expect +25bp to 5.00%; hawkish hike = banking NIM hit + IDR relief (good for ADRO via IDR/coal-revenue dynamic); hold = IDR continuation crisis (bad for everything). Either way, no new entries pre-decision.
- **Prabowo "could announce as soon as Wednesday" — single-gateway export agency (Bloomberg-confirmed plan)** — STRUCTURAL THESIS RISK to ADRO. Pre-mortem trigger armed: official confirmation = immediate close, regardless of price.
- **IHSG 6,370.68 1-year low; next support 6,270 / 6,148 (MNC)** — if gaps below 6,300 at open with no recovery: full capitulation mode; ADRO likely hits hard-cut.
- **IDR 17,752 intraday May 19** — above 17,750 DEFENSIVE-escalation trigger; sustained = further IDR weakness; capital-outflow accelerating.
- **US 10Y 4.68% / 30Y 5.19% (19-yr high)** — global yield shock keeps EM under pressure; carry-trade unwind.
- **Newcastle coal $134.50–135.80** — supportive at floor; Asia LNG-squeeze (Iran-war driven). If holds, ADRO's price-deck thesis preserved even if regulatory news structurally bad.
- **Foreign flow Day 22 of MSCI rebal wave** — passive outflow $1.6–1.8B continues to weigh through May 29 effective close.
- **Data infra block — Day 30 of yfinance blackout.** All marks WebSearch-only; ADV unverifiable; multi-source verification BINDING for any near-stop position.
- **Trades this week 0/3 used** — patience holds. Zero new entries expected this week regardless of RDG outcome.

### Plan for /market-open (2026-05-20, Wed)

- [ ] **HOLD ADRO 392,100 sh** at 09:15 open IF mark verifies ≥2,400 multi-source. **Multi-source verification protocol BINDING** (buffer <1.5%; need 2+ source agreement on mark).
- [ ] **AUTO-CUT TRIGGERS armed for ADRO at /market-open:**
  - (a) Intraday mark ≤ 2,371 (hard-cut breach) → immediate sell at market.
  - (b) Official Prabowo announcement of single-gateway export agency → immediate sell at market regardless of price.
  - (c) Newcastle <$125 verified sustained 2nd day → defensive exit.
  - (d) IHSG gaps below 6,250 (below MNC support 6,270) AND ADRO testing 2,400 → defensive pre-stop exit.
- [ ] **NO NEW ENTRIES today.** All 3 candidates scoring ≥6 (KLBF, TLKM, ASII) deferred to post-RDG (Thu May 21 pre-market) with fresh quotes.
- [ ] **BI RDG decision watch ~14:00 WIB.** Don't pre-position. Hawkish hike = wait for tape confirmation (rupiah relief + banking re-rate window may take 1–2 days to manifest). Hold = capitulation continuation; defensive day.
- [ ] **Single-gateway export announcement watch.** If Prabowo announces during market hours: ADRO close. If announced post-close: defensive overnight; reassess Thu pre-market.
- [ ] **Trades this week: 0/3 used.** Expected use: 0 — patience-over-activity holds through BI RDG + regulatory binary; revisit Thu May 21 if regime clears.
