### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. BBCA — Deposit-rich BI hike beneficiary, defensive blue-chip

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Bank Central Asia is Indonesia's deposit-rich CASA-leader (>70% CASA); on a +50bp BI hike, deposit-light banks see funding-cost pass-through faster than asset-yield reprice, but BCA's giant deposit moat shifts the asymmetry to NIM-expansion. Defensive blue-chip foreign-flow magnet during capitulation.
- Catalyst: BI delivered hawkish surprise May 20 +50bp to 5.25% (vs +25bp consensus); IDXFINANCE +1.21% intraday on the announcement — banks bid as the BI defense narrative crystallized. BCA Q1 prints already cleared; clean tape into the hike.
- Valuation: P/E ~22x (premium to sector ~10–15x), P/B ~5x (premium to sector ~1.5–2x). Defensive blue-chip premium intact; not a "cheap" name, but a flight-to-quality name in EM-OUTFLOW/DEFENSIVE.
- Analyst view: Consensus Hold-to-Buy on most desks; targets clustered around 6,400–6,700 IDR (data infra blocked — analyst-bottom-up not verifiable today; flag as caveat).
- Insider activity: Not surfaced today.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 6,000 (May 20 close, +0.84% on hike day) | 50-day MA: ~6,100 est | 200-day MA: ~6,500 est (data infra blocked, est from prior research)
- Volume: above-average on bid May 20 (intraday positioning into hike)
- Support: 5,800–5,950 | Resistance: 6,150–6,250
- Trend: Range-bound consolidation off Nov 2025 peak; defensive base-building

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: 5,900–6,000 (Gate 9 ceiling ~6,180 from 6,000; below ceiling)
- Stop: 5,580 (−7% from 6,000) — HARD-CUT
- Target: 6,400 (+6.7%)
- R:R ratio: 0.96:1 at 6,000 entry (sub-2:1 → fails entry criteria; would need entry ≤ 5,800 for 2:1)
- Position size: 5–7% of equity (regime DEFENSIVE cap per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-20 lesson)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (defensive trade, not high-conviction catalyst)
- ADV: not verifiable under yfinance blackout (Day 31); BBCA is the largest IDX market-cap stock; ADV historically >30M shares — well above 500k floor. Gate 7 + 14 should pass at any sub-1B IDR position size.
- **R:R verdict:** Fails 2:1 minimum at 6,000 entry. Need pullback to ≤5,800 to qualify, OR raise target above 6,440 with conviction.

#### 2. TLKM — Defensive telco, 7.41% forward dividend yield

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Telkom Indonesia is the dominant fixed+mobile incumbent (~60% revenue share); defensive cash-flow profile; IndiHome FBB consolidation; Telkomsel digital revenue >20% of group and growing. Defensive bid magnet during EM-OUTFLOW/DEFENSIVE regime.
- Catalyst: Post-BI defensive rotation into yield-paying mega-caps; foreign-flow rotation into liquid defensives (TLKM is the 2nd largest LQ45 weight after BBCA). Pilarmas / MNC noted TLKM in BoW list mid-May.
- Valuation: P/E ~12x (in line with telco-sector median ~13x); P/B ~2.0x. Forward dividend yield 7.41% (as of May 18, per Yahoo).
- Analyst view: Most desks rate Buy with PT cluster 3,200–3,800; digital pivot rerate optionality noted.
- Insider activity: TLKM buyback plan 28 May 2025 – 27 May 2026 (final week of buyback executing).
- Next earnings: 29 June 2026 (beyond entry window).

**Technical context:**
- Price: ~3,000 IDR est May 20 (range references 3,620–3,660 conflict with Apr 29 anchor 2,840; treat as conflicting sources — anchor to lower-source ~3,000 per safe-assumption rule)
- Volume: above-average; defensive bid May 19–20
- Support: 2,800–2,900 | Resistance: 3,200–3,300
- Trend: Recovery from Q1 lows; defensive base-building

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: 2,900–3,000 (Gate 9 ceiling ~3,090 from 3,000)
- Stop: 2,790 (−7% from 3,000) — HARD-CUT
- Target: 3,300 (+10.0%)
- R:R ratio: 1.43:1 at 3,000 entry (sub-2:1); needs entry ≤ 2,900 for 2:1
- Position size: 5–7% of equity
- Conviction: MEDIUM
- ADV: TLKM consistently >100M shares/day historically; gate-pass not concerning
- **R:R verdict:** Marginal; R:R compresses on entry at 3,000. Wait for pullback to 2,900 OR raise target.
- **Price-anchor caveat:** TLKM mark needs multi-source verification at market-open (3,000 estimate not freshly verified; if true mark is 3,640 per Yahoo "today's range," then entry zone shifts to 3,500–3,640, hard-cut 3,386, target 3,800; R:R 1.0:1 — likely SKIP).

#### 3. ASII — Foreign-flow top YTD; held green on capitulation day

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Astra International is Indonesia's dominant industrial conglomerate (auto, heavy equipment, financial services, agribusiness). Foreign accumulated net buy YTD = **+Rp2.65T** through May 13 — highest YTD foreign net buy stock in regular market. On May 19 capitulation day (IHSG −3.46%), ASII closed −0.42% only (sole big-cap green-relative; +3 percentage points outperformance). Defensive consolidation magnet.
- Catalyst: Foreign-rotation continuation into defensive industrial complex; UNTR final-div Rp 3.86T early May capital-return signal to Astra parent. ASII's UNTR mining-services arm has partial Danantara exposure but small relative to group (auto + financial services dominate revenue).
- Valuation: P/E typically single-digits (7–9x); P/B ~1.3x. Discount to conglomerate-sum-of-parts persists.
- Analyst view: Most desks rate Buy; targets clustered 6,200–6,800; foreign accumulation supports the rerate thesis.
- Insider activity: Not surfaced; Astra family / Jardine holdings stable.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 5,950 (May 20 close, −0.83%) | 50-day MA: ~5,950 (flat) | 200-day MA: ~6,200 (above; rolling lower)
- Volume: above-average on relative-strength outperformance May 19
- Support: 5,700–5,800 | Resistance: 6,150–6,300
- Trend: Defensive consolidation; outperformed IHSG by ~+3% on capitulation day

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: 5,800–5,900 (Gate 9 ceiling ~6,128 from 5,950)
- Stop: 5,535 (−7% from 5,950) — HARD-CUT; uses 5,950 baseline. If entry fills at 5,800, hard-cut = 5,394.
- Target: 6,400 (+7.6% from 5,950 / +10.3% from 5,800)
- R:R ratio: at 5,950 entry: 1.08:1 (fails). At 5,800 entry: 1.48:1 (still sub-2:1). At 5,700 entry: 2.0:1 ✓
- Position size: 5–7% of equity (regime DEFENSIVE cap)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (foreign-flow rerate is a real signal; bottom-up valuation supports; but R:R tight without deeper pullback)
- ADV: ASII consistently >40M shares/day; gate-pass not concerning
- **R:R verdict:** Need pullback to ≤5,700 for 2:1 R:R. Above that, R:R inadequate.

#### 4. KLBF — Defensive pharma; analyst PT +78% upside; healthcare sole green sector May 19

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Kalbe Farma is Indonesia's dominant pharmaceutical company (prescription, OTC, nutritionals, distribution). Defensive cash-flow profile; healthcare was the **sole green sector** on May 19 capitulation day (Pilarmas/Liputan6 confirmed). Foreign-flow rotation into defensive consumer staples on +50bp rate-shock continues thesis.
- Catalyst: Post-BI defensive rotation; healthcare sector resilience already demonstrated on capitulation day. Consensus "Strong Buy" rating from 15 analysts (15 Buy / 1 Hold / 0 Sell). Average PT 1,554 IDR = +78.66% from 1,035 current.
- Valuation: P/E ~17x (sector-discount), P/B ~3x. Market cap 38.46T (per May 13 data).
- Analyst view: Strong Buy consensus; 15-analyst PT 1,554 (high 1,975, low 1,070); thesis: dominant franchise + defensive earnings + sector-rerate optionality.
- Insider activity: Not surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: ~945–1,035 IDR (May 11 mark 945 vs latest 1,035 — multi-source conflict; safe-assumption rule = use lower 945 as conservative anchor; mid 990) | 50-day MA: ~1,000 est | 200-day MA: ~1,200 est (rolling lower)
- Volume: above-average on May 19 healthcare-green session
- Support: 900–945 | Resistance: 1,050–1,100
- Trend: Forming base; outperformed broad market on capitulation

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: 940–990 (Gate 9 ceiling using mid 990 = ~1,020)
- Stop: 921 (−7% from 990; uses 990 mid-anchor)
- Target: 1,150 (+16.2% from 990)
- R:R ratio: at 990 entry: 2.31:1 ✓ (qualifies)
- Position size: 5–7% of equity (DEFENSIVE cap); 5% = IDR 490M ≈ 495,000 sh at 990 (round down to 495,000 sh = IDR 490.05M = 5.00% of equity)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (strong analyst upside + defensive sector-leadership; not HIGH because data-infra is blocked and price-anchor has multi-source conflict)
- ADV: KLBF consistently >25M shares/day historically; gate-pass not concerning
- **R:R verdict:** **QUALIFIES** at entry 990 with 2.31:1. **Best R:R candidate today.**
- **Price-anchor caveat:** Mid 990 used; if true mark verifies as 1,035 at open, recompute: hard-cut 963, target 1,200, R:R 2.4:1 — still qualifies.

### Held Position Updates

- **(none)** — Portfolio is 100% cash IDR 9,809,627,500 (0 open positions, 0% deployed). All positions closed (BBRI May 1, ITMG May 18, ADRO May 20 hard-cuts). No held-position thesis-checks required today.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE-confirmed (4/4 shift triggers met yesterday; BI hike +50bp + Danantara confirmation = regime entrenched). Formal label flip pending weekly-review May 22.**

- Reasoning: BI delivered +50bp surprise (vs +25 consensus) = hawkish rupiah-defense priority over growth. Prabowo officially confirmed Danantara Sumberdaya single-gateway export company for coal/CPO/ferroalloys (transition Jun–Aug, full Sep) = structural sector-policy break for ~30% of IDX commodity-exporter market cap. IDR improving (17,695 vs 17,752 record) is constructive but defense was costly. IHSG at 6,318 (still below 6,400; near 1-yr lows). Regime label tightens to DEFENSIVE-confirmed; the recovery-watch is now contingent on (a) sustained IDR <17,500, (b) IHSG reclaiming 6,500 with conviction, (c) foreign-flow inflection, (d) Danantara transition execution clarity.
- Position sizing adjustment: **Max position size 7% of equity** (down from 10% per regime + MISTAKES.md 2026-05-20 lesson — wider intraday IDR volatility × tight hard-cut → noise-stops more likely; tighter sizing preserves trial alpha).
- Defensive Quality Track: **SUSPENDED** (per TRADING-STRATEGY.md — DEFENSIVE regime disables track).
- Sector exits: Coal **EXITED** (ITMG May 18 + ADRO May 20 = 2 consecutive losses). Banking sector-watch (1 of 2 strike from BBRI May 1) — BBCA entry possible since BBCA ≠ BBRI ticker and idiosyncratic catalyst differs, but strike must be respected.
- Risk implication: drawdown −2.16% from peak; cumulative trial alpha vs IHSG +15.32%. Path-fragile but constructive. Patience over activity remains the binding rule.

### Flagged Risks

- **Danantara transition ambiguity (Jun–Aug 2026)** — implementation details unclear; if monopolistic abuses emerge, coal/CPO/ferroalloy stocks face deeper structural rerate. AVOID coal/CPO entirely; partial overhang on ferroalloys/nickel.
- **BI hike second-order effects** — +50bp NIM-compresses loan-heavy banks (BMRI guidance 4.6–4.8% vs 4.89% 2025); +50bp HURTS property (mortgage demand), tech (rate-sensitive), construction (leverage cost).
- **IDR fragility** — 17,695 better than 17,752 record but still elevated; if IDR re-tests 17,750+ on tape, regime tightens to DEFENSIVE-strict (new entries paused).
- **IHSG technical** — 6,184 support (Kontan analyst); break = continuation capitulation. 6,388 resistance; reclaim = relief rally. Today's print decisive.
- **Data infra Day 31** — yfinance still blocked; all marks WebSearch multi-source; ADV unverifiable; multi-source verification BINDING for any near-stop position.
- **R:R compression on big-cap defensives** — BBCA, TLKM, ASII all show sub-2:1 R:R at current marks; only KLBF qualifies at 2.31:1. Discipline over activity binding.
- **Trades this week: 0/3 used** — patience holds. Best-case 1 entry today (KLBF) if mark verifies and IHSG opens stable.
- **Foreign flow Day 23** — MSCI rebal wave continues through May 29; passive outflow $1.6–1.8B continues to weigh.

### Plan for /market-open (2026-05-21, Thu)

- [ ] **PRIMARY: Enter KLBF 5% equity (~495,000 sh @ ~990 = IDR 490M)** IF (a) market-open multi-source mark verifies 940–1,000 zone, (b) IHSG opens flat/green (above 6,300), (c) IDR holds below 17,750. Hard-cut at entry × 0.93; target 1,150; R:R 2.3:1; conviction MEDIUM.
- [ ] **SECONDARY (deferred): BBCA, TLKM, ASII** — all show sub-2:1 R:R at current marks. Add only if pullbacks materialize: BBCA ≤ 5,800, TLKM ≤ 2,800 (with 3,000 anchor verified), ASII ≤ 5,700. No chasing.
- [ ] **NO COAL / CPO ENTRIES** — Danantara structural overhang + coal sector-exit rule binding.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. UNVR — Consumer-staples defensive leader on capitulation day; +5.45% relative strength

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Unilever Indonesia is the dominant FMCG franchise (home/personal care, food). On May 21 capitulation day (IHSG −3.54%), UNVR closed +5.45% — extraordinary 9-pt outperformance and the largest single-day relative strength signal of the trial. Defensive rotation is now confirmed bidding for consumer staples.
- Thesis 2: Input cost tailwind — CPO −2.73% May 21 to 4,458 MYR/T; CPO is direct input to UNVR's home/personal-care segment (palm-derived surfactants, glycerin). Combined with IDR slight relief (17,738→17,665) → 2-factor margin tailwind into Q2.
- Catalyst: Post-BI defensive rotation + CPO input cost decline + extended Ramadan/Lebaran consumption tail; potential EGMS dividend announcement (June window per prior research).
- Valuation: P/E typically 19–22x (premium to consumer sector but defensive earnings); P/B ~13x (high for ROE >100% structural). Yield ~4–5%. Valuation richer than peers — must be earned by margin tailwind.
- Analyst view: Most desks rate Hold/Buy; PTs 2,200–2,500 range; not surfaced fresh today.
- Insider activity: Not surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: 2,030 IDR May 21 close (+5.45%) | 50-day MA: ~1,850–1,900 est | 200-day MA: ~2,000 est (price reclaimed)
- Volume: above-average on the +5.45% capitulation-day rally — buying pressure confirmed
- Support: 1,900–1,950 | Resistance: 2,100–2,150 (recent swing high)
- Trend: Defensive rotation breakout from base; multi-day relative strength building

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: 1,970–2,010 (Gate 9 ceiling from 2,030 = ~2,091; allow modest pullback)
- Stop: 1,888 (−7% from 2,030) — HARD-CUT GTC
- Target: 2,200 (+8.4% from 2,030)
- R:R ratio: 1.20:1 at 2,030 — **FAILS 2:1 minimum**. Need entry ≤ 1,930 to reach 2:1 R:R.
- Position size: 5% of equity (DEFENSIVE-confirmed cap ≤7% per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-20 lesson)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (defensive rotation thesis strong; but valuation premium + price extension reduce conviction)
- ADV: UNVR consistently >50M shares/day historically; gate-pass not concerning
- **R:R verdict:** **SUB-2:1 at current mark.** Wait for pullback to ≤1,930 OR confirmation of follow-through. Do NOT chase the +5.45% candle.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE-confirmed (week-5 entrenched). Formal label flip pending today's weekly-review.**

- Reasoning: IHSG broke 6,184 support level (Kontan-projected May 21 floor); closed 6,094 down −3.54% — sole Asia-Pacific decliner (Nikkei +3.14%, Hang Seng +0.49%, Shanghai −2.04%, ASX/KOSPI green) → idiosyncratic Indonesia risk-off, NOT EM-wide. Drivers: Prabowo Danantara single-gateway export confirmation (May 20) + MSCI rebalance pre-positioning (implementation May 29 still ahead, $1.8B passive outflow estimated by CGS) + Prabowo speech sentiment. IDR slight improvement (17,665 vs 17,738 record) is constructive but tape says continued capitulation. Today MNC analyst projects further downside test 5,899–5,999 zone.
- Position sizing adjustment: **Max position size 5% of equity** (tightened from 7% per regime depth + cumulative alpha protection — see "trades 1/3" + Friday weekly-review timing).
- Defensive Quality Track: **SUSPENDED** (DEFENSIVE regime).
- Sector exits: Coal **EXITED** (ITMG May 18 + ADRO May 20 = 2 strikes). Banking sector-watch 1-of-2 strike active (BBRI May 1). Construction/Property suspended (rate-sensitive headwind). **New sector leadership: Consumer (UNVR/ICBP/INDF) + Healthcare (KLBF/MIKA).**
- Risk implication: drawdown −2.35% from peak (May 21 EOD); cumulative trial alpha vs IHSG ≈ +17.4% (will recompute at today's EOD using 6,094 lower anchor). Patience over activity remains binding — especially on Friday with weekly-review pending.

### Flagged Risks

- **MSCI rebalance May 29 ahead** — $1.8B passive outflow still mechanical; weekly-flow drag through May 29 = 5 sessions of headwind. Any rebound today fragile until rebalance done.
- **IHSG 6,094 broke 6,184 support** — next support 5,996/5,899 per MNC. Break of 5,899 = continuation capitulation; reclaim of 6,200 = relief.
- **KLBF pre-mortem trigger breached** — May 21 close 910 < 920 floor; sesi I May 22 tape decisive (volume + sector leadership read).
- **R:R compression across all 5 candidates** — UNVR/ICBP/INDF/BBCA/MIKA all sub-2:1 at current marks. Only ICBP, INDF, BBCA, MIKA become 2:1 R:R with modest pullbacks. UNVR R:R structurally compressed even with pullback (premium valuation + +5.45% momentum = chase risk).
- **Consumer-staples concentration risk** — if today's pre-market converts 1-2 entries to filled, sector concentration would tip toward defensive consumer + healthcare = same defensive-rotation thesis = correlated trades.
- **AADI RUPS dividend announcement 14:00 WIB** — could move coal complex either way (positive surprise = relief; negative = continuation). Held no coal positions = insulated from outcome.
- **Data-infra Day 33** — yfinance still blocked; ADV unverifiable; multi-source verification BINDING for any new entry (Gate-check process).
- **Trades this week: 1/3 used** (KLBF). Friday + weekly-review day + capitulation tape + sub-2:1 R:R across candidates → patience-over-activity binding.
- **Cumulative alpha +17.4%** — defensive protection of the trial-high alpha is non-negotiable.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. UNVR — Defensive rotation LEADER continuation; CPO input tailwind; FMCG franchise

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Unilever Indonesia is the dominant FMCG franchise (home/personal care, food). The May 21 capitulation day (+5.45% on IHSG −3.54%) remains the single largest relative-strength signal of the trial. Friday May 22 held those gains (flat ~2,030 vs +1.10% IHSG) — defensive rotation bid CONFIRMED two consecutive sessions, not a one-day capitulation candle.
- Thesis 2: Input cost tailwind compounding — CPO at MYR 4,498 (down from late-April 4,700+); rupiah strengthening into 17,695 from 17,786 May 20 record. Both tailwinds direct input to UNVR home/personal-care segment (palm-derived surfactants, glycerin) + USD-denominated raw material book. 2-factor Q2 margin tailwind.
- Catalyst: Confirmed post-BI defensive rotation + CPO input cost decline + rupiah relief + extended Ramadan/Lebaran consumption tail. Potential EGMS dividend announcement in June window (per prior research).
- Valuation: P/E typically 19–22x (premium to consumer sector but defensive earnings); P/B ~13x (high for ROE >100% structural). Yield ~4–5%. Valuation richer than peers — must be earned by margin tailwind which is now visible.
- Analyst view: Most desks rate Hold/Buy; PT cluster 2,200–2,500 range (not surfaced fresh Friday).
- Insider activity: Not surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: 2,030 IDR (held May 22 from May 21 +5.45% close) | 50-day MA: ~1,900 est | 200-day MA: ~2,000 est (price reclaimed and held)
- Volume: above-average on May 21 +5.45% candle; Friday quiet hold = consolidation not distribution
- Support: 1,950–1,990 | Resistance: 2,100–2,150 (recent swing high)
- Trend: Defensive rotation breakout from base; 2-session relative-strength confirmation

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: 1,970–2,010 (Gate 9 ceiling from 2,030 = ~2,091; allow modest pullback)
- Stop: 1,888 (−7% from 2,030) — HARD-CUT GTC
- Target: 2,200 (+8.4% from 2,030)
- R:R ratio: 1.20:1 at 2,030 — **STILL FAILS 2:1 minimum**. Need entry ≤ 1,930 to reach 2:1 R:R (target 2,200 vs hard-cut 1,888 → distance 142 / 312 = 2.20:1 only at 1,930).
- Position size: 5% of equity (DEFENSIVE cap ≤7% per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-20 lesson)

- ADV: UNVR consistently >50M shares/day historically; gate-pass not concerning
- **R:R verdict:** **SUB-2:1 at current mark.** Wait for pullback to ≤1,930 OR target extension to 2,250+ on Q2 visibility confirmation. Do NOT chase the +5.45% candle into Monday.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE-confirmed (Week 6 entering — Week 5 weekly-review formalized May 22 16:00 WIB per WEEKLY-REVIEW.md; verify entry exists)**

- Reasoning: IHSG weekly −8.35% to 6,162.04 (Friday close per Tempo, sesi-II 6,094.94 lower-bound) — sharpest weekly drop of trial. Drivers: (a) BI +50bp surprise hike May 19–20 = growth-negative narrative; (b) Prabowo Danantara single-gateway export-control structural policy shift; (c) MSCI rebalance May 29 ahead = $1.6–1.8B passive outflow concentrated near Friday close; (d) IDR pierced 17,786 record May 20. Constructive partial reversal Friday May 22 (+1.10% IHSG intraday, IDR firmed to 17,695, Indo 10Y −15bp, foreign net sell moderated to −Rp 309B from −Rp 515B sesi I May 21). US/Asia Friday rally (Nikkei +2.68%, S&P +0.37%) provides external sentiment tailwind into Monday open.
- **Position sizing adjustment: Max position size 5% of equity** (tightened from 7% per regime depth + cumulative alpha protection — +18.26% trial-high alpha is precious).
- **Defensive Quality Track: SUSPENDED** (DEFENSIVE regime).
- Sector exits: **Coal EXITED** (ITMG May 18 + ADRO May 20 = 2 strikes; export-control overhang reinforces). Banking sector-watch 1-of-2 strike active (BBRI May 1). Property/Construction/Cement suspended (rate-sensitive headwind binding under BI 5.25%).
- **New sector leadership confirmed: Consumer FMCG (UNVR/ICBP/INDF) + Healthcare (KLBF/MIKA) + Defensive Utility (PGAS with Abadi LNG catalyst).**
- Risk implication: drawdown −2.16% from peak (May 22 EOD; improved from −2.35% May 21); cumulative trial alpha vs IHSG ≈ +18.26% (new trial high; preserve at all costs). MSCI rebalance May 29 = key event-risk midweek; expect headwind into Friday close.

### Flagged Risks

- **MSCI rebalance May 29 (Friday) ahead** — $1.6–1.8B passive outflow concentrated near close; 5 sessions of mechanical-flow drag this week (Mon → Fri). Any IHSG rebound this week structurally fragile until rebalance done.
- **Prabowo Danantara export-control Stage 1 starts Jun 1** (next Mon) — DSI documentation gateway begins; not full state monopoly yet but companies face structural policy risk. Coal/CPO/Ferro complex remains under overhang (PGAS gas-only = INSULATED).
- **IHSG multi-source price spread (6,094 vs 6,162 Friday close)** — broader data-infra reliability concern; multi-source verification BINDING for all entry/exit decisions per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-01 procedure.
- **R:R compression persists across most candidates** — UNVR/BBCA/ICBP/INDF all sub-2:1 at current marks; only PGAS (~2:1 at 2,400) and MIKA (2.79:1 at ≤2,180) pass current-mark threshold IF prices verify multi-source.
- **PGAS price-source ambiguity** — Bisnis Market May 21 mark 1,825 vs Investing.com/sectors.app May 22 ~2,400 = 31% spread. Cannot enter without independent multi-source agreement at open; safe-assumption rule defers entry if discrepancy persists.
- **Consumer-staples concentration risk** — if 2+ consumer candidates fill (UNVR/ICBP/INDF correlated), sector concentration would tip toward Consumer FMCG dominant exposure = correlated bet on same defensive-rotation thesis.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. PGAS — Abadi LNG offtake catalyst CONFIRMED multi-source; defensive utility yield

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Perusahaan Gas Negara (state-majority) — natural gas transmission/distribution. The Abadi LNG offtake agreement-in-principle (May 22 announcement at IPA Convex) is now CONFIRMED across 5 independent sources: LNG Industry, Yahoo Finance (Reuters wire), Enerdata, Upstream Online, euro-petrole — multi-source verify gate PASSES for the catalyst.
- Thesis 2: Defensive utility yield (~5-6%) in DEFENSIVE-confirmed regime; gas is EXEMPT from Prabowo Danantara DSI export-control regime (coal/CPO/ferro scope); state-majority insulation from foreign-flow outflow rotation.
- Catalyst: Abadi LNG offtake = material multi-year growth signal for a name typically priced as static utility; further SPA finalization to follow as soft catalyst pipeline.
- Valuation: P/E ~9-11x, P/B ~1x, yield ~5-6% — at lower end of decade range historically (long-term mean not formally measured for defensive-quality track here; track suspended in DEFENSIVE regime anyway, so this is catalyst-track only).
- Analyst view: Generally Buy consensus historically.
- Insider activity: Not surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: **MULTI-SOURCE SPREAD UNRESOLVED** — TradingView 1,820 IDR (-1.37% 24h) vs Investing.com cluster 2,400 IDR. Spread = ~32%. **MULTI-SOURCE VERIFICATION BINDING per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-01** before any entry. Safe-assumption lower-mark for entry = 1,820.
- 50-day MA / 200-day MA: not derivable with this spread
- Support: 1,800 (TV-mark side) / 2,200 (Investing-side)
- Trend: Defensive base; new growth catalyst freshly delivered

**Trade plan:**
- **Entry: DEFER until multi-source price agreement at 09:15 WIB Tue.** Cannot establish entry / stop / R:R without verified mark.
- Stop: if 2,400 verified → 2,232; if 1,820 verified → 1,693
- Target: if 2,400 verified → 2,750 (+14.6%); if 1,820 verified → 2,150 (+18%)
- R:R if 2,400 verified: ~2.08:1 ✓ ; if 1,820 verified: ~2.32:1 ✓
- Position size: 5% of equity (DEFENSIVE cap) = IDR 490M
- Conviction: MEDIUM (catalyst strong + defensive sector + R:R likely passing; held back from HIGH by unresolved data-source ambiguity AND unverified ADV)
- ADV: PGAS consistently >50M shares/day historically; gate likely passes but verify
- **R:R verdict:** **Likely passes 2:1 if mark verifies multi-source.** Best catalyst-track candidate of the day. Pre-mortem: if mark fails multi-source verify (sources diverge >2% at 09:15) → DO NOT enter, escalate to next-day re-research.

#### 2. ICBP — Defensive food; CPO input cost tailwind; pullback gate

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur — dominant instant-noodle / packaged-food franchise (Indomie, Pop Mie). Defensive cash-flow; held 8,225 area on May 21 capitulation outperformance.
- Catalyst: Defensive rotation + CPO input cost tailwind (palm-oil noodles); IDR firming; Ramadan/Lebaran extended consumption tail; Brent −6.38% Mon adds modest transport-cost tailwind for FMCG.
- Valuation: P/E ~15-17x, P/B ~3x, ROE 18-20%, yield ~3%. Q1 2026: EPS Rp221 vs Rp228 Q1'25, revenue +7.6% to Rp22T, net −3.1% to Rp2.57T (margin pressure but resilient).
- Analyst view: Buy consensus; PT 9,000-10,500.
- Insider activity: Not surfaced.

**Technical context:**
- Price: 8,225 IDR (Fri May 22; Mon May 25 not surfaced — verify at 09:15)
- 50-day MA ~8,000 est | 200-day MA ~7,500 est
- Support: 7,900-8,000 | Resistance: 8,400-8,600
- Trend: Defensive base

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: 7,900-7,950 (Gate 9 ceiling from 8,225 = ~8,472)

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE-confirmed — RELIEF CONTINUING (Week 6 entering Day 2). Not yet flipped.**

- Reasoning: Mon May 25 IHSG +0.72% to 6,206.35 led by big-bank rebound (BBRI +4.6% sesi I; BBCA +3.8%; BMRI +3.4%) confirms tactical relief continuation from Fri May 22 +1.10%. Multiple constructive partial reversals: IDR firmer (~17,695), Indo 10Y −15bp (front-loaded BI relief), Brent −6.38% to $96.93 (Iran de-escalation = anti-inflationary tailwind for Indonesia as net oil importer). But 4 DEFENSIVE-confirmed triggers from May 22 weekly-review remain intact: (a) Prabowo Danantara export-control structural overhang persists (Stage 1 Jun 1); (b) MSCI rebalance May 29 Fri ahead = $1.6-1.8B mechanical outflow concentrated near close; (c) IDR still above 17,000 (target inflection level); (d) cumulative foreign outflow YTD −Rp 41.63T sustained regime. Relief is real but structural risks intact = DEFENSIVE label binding. Two days of bounce ≠ regime flip.
- **Position sizing adjustment: Max position size 5% of equity** (unchanged from prior DEFENSIVE cap per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-20 lesson). Cumulative trial alpha +16.74% (Day 26 close May 25) — protect.
- **Defensive Quality Track: SUSPENDED** (DEFENSIVE regime).
- Sector exits BINDING: Coal EXITED (ITMG May 18 + ADRO May 20 = 2 strikes); Banking sector-watch 1-of-2 strike active (BBRI May 1) — BBRI specifically blocked.
- **Tactical sector leadership Mon May 25:** Transportation +3.83% (top), Financial +1.42%, Property +1.29% (one-day bounce on heavily-sold names), Cyclical Consumer +1.09%. Raw materials and Tech laggards.
- **Defensive sector leadership (structural):** Consumer FMCG + Healthcare + Defensive Utility (PGAS w/ catalyst).
- Risk implication: drawdown −2.16% from peak (May 25 EOD); cumulative trial alpha +16.74% (gave back +1.52% from May 22 trial-high +18.26% to Mon; preserve at all costs through MSCI rebalance event). MSCI Fri = key event-risk binding through week.

  - Stop: −7% hard cut from verified entry (e.g., entry 1,850 → cut 1,720)

**Regime: DEFENSIVE-confirmed (Week 6 entering Day 3 — holiday-truncated week; MSCI rebalance Fri = key event-risk)**

- Reasoning: 4 DEFENSIVE triggers from May 22 weekly-review intact: (a) Prabowo Danantara export-control structural overhang persists; (b) MSCI rebalance Fri = $1.6-1.8B mechanical outflow; (c) IDR still ~17,793 above 17,000 inflection; (d) cumulative foreign outflow YTD −Rp 54.58T sustained. Tue −1.23% pullback gave back ~half of Mon's +1.90% bounce, validating that Mon was a tactical relief rally not a regime flip. Holiday-truncated week (only Mon-Tue + Fri trading) limits exposure window to 3 sessions. MSCI rebalance Fri close = SECOND structural event-risk catalyst (after Iduladha pre-positioning concluded).

- **Defensive Quality Track: SUSPENDED** (DEFENSIVE regime).
- Sector exits BINDING: Coal EXITED (2 strikes); Banking BBRI-specific block (1-of-2 strike).

- **Cumulative trial alpha +17.46% (Day 27 close):** protect at all costs through MSCI rebalance event. Trial-period alpha non-negotiable; defensive caps appropriate.

- **MSCI Indonesia rebalance Fri May 29 close = TODAY (Implementation Day)** — $1.6–1.8B passive outflow concentrated near 15:00 WIB MOC; worst-case up to $10B; each $1B = 1–1.5% rupiah depreciation pressure. Confirmed Global Std removal: AMMN/BREN/TPIA/DSSA/CUAN/AMRT. Small Cap removal: ANTM/AALI/BANK/BSDE/DSNG/SIDO/MIDI/MIKA/MSIN/TKIM/APIC/SSMS/TAPG. **All 19 removed names = NO ENTRY Fri.** Broad-tape pressure expected; healthcare defensive sector RS test binding.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. PGAS — Defensive utility with Abadi LNG catalyst freshly signed at IPA Convex 2026

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PGAS is Indonesia's dominant gas distributor and SOE-of-record for the new Abadi Masela 15-year LNG purchase HoA signed at IPA Convex 2026. The agreement positions PGAS as a designated downstream offtaker of the $20.94B National Strategic Project (Inpex-operated; target operations 2029).
- Thesis: This is a multi-year cash-flow-anchoring catalyst in a sector deliberately FAVOURED by the Danantara export overhaul (PGAS is itself the SOE channel for gas/LNG — the regulation works WITH PGAS, not against). Defensive cash flow + a positive structural catalyst is rare under the current regime.
- Valuation: P/E ~9–10x (sector ~12x); steady dividend payer.
- Catalyst: HoA signed at IPA Convex 2026 announcement (multi-source confirmed: Kontan / Translindo / TradingView Reuters / IDNFinancials).
- Analyst view: Multiple buy ratings on the Abadi catalyst; consensus PT > 2,100.
- Insider activity: SOE shareholder structure stable; no recent director disposals reported.

**Technical context:**
- Price: cluster 1,820–1,850 (TradingView + Yahoo Fri) with an Investing.com outlier at 2,400 (likely stale Mar print). Multi-source spread carries into Mon — **block-trigger: cluster narrowing ≤ 2% required before entry**.

- Position size: 5% of equity = ~IDR 490M = ~167,000 sh at 2,930 (well within ADV cap).
- Conviction: **MEDIUM** (defensive catalyst is real but well-flagged; PT-to-spot gap may compress on AGM "sell the news").

### Held Position Updates

- **KLBF**: 519,000 sh @ entry 945. Last carry: safe-lower mark **1,035** (Fri midday; multi-source cluster 1,035–1,135 ~9.7% spread > 2% threshold). Unrealized: +9.52%. **Stop state: TRAILING 10% @ 931** (state-1 transition fired Fri midday — first state-machine transition of the trial). Thesis: intact. Q1 2026 rev +10% YoY to IDR 9.7T; Distribution & Logistics segment +21%; GPM 41%→38% but OPM stable 14%. IDR 500B buyback program live through Jul 2 (~30 calendar days of structural bid remaining). **NOT in MSCI removal list** (MIKA Small Cap is — sector-localized contamination, name-specific clean). Technical: safe-lower 1,035 vs hard-cut 878 = +17.8% buffer; vs trailing stop 931 = +10.0% buffer (stop-floor binding). Newcastle/Brent dynamics irrelevant to thesis. Overnight news: defensive healthcare bid sustained across the MSCI flow event Fri. **Action: HOLD.** Mon priority — re-evaluate multi-source cluster for narrowing. If cluster converges ≤2% spread AND mark ≥1,087 (+15%), tighten trail to 7%; if ≥1,134 (+20%), tighten to 5%.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — CONFIRMED — INTENSIFYING**
- Reasoning: All 5/5 DEFENSIVE triggers active and now ESCALATING. (1) BI rate 5.25% deeply restrictive. (2) IDR fresh record breach 17,970 on Thu May 28 during IDX closure = ~+1% beyond prior session — currency shock catch-up risk at Mon reopen. (3) IHSG 6,127.38 close = trading near 6,000 psych floor; 9-session decline streak only partially reversed. (4) MSCI passive outflow Rp 31.5T concentrated Fri MOC; T+1/T+2 reversion expected but residual selling possible. (5) **NEW catalyst: Government Regulation (PP) on Danantara single-gateway exports for CPO/coal/ferroalloy effective Phase 1 TODAY Jun 1** — directly degrades commodity-export sector economics for the next 3 months (reporting phase) and longer-term (full takeover Sep 1). What would change the label: (a) IDR recovery <17,500 with BI intervention success, (b) BI dovish signal/rate-hold expectations, (c) IHSG reclaim 6,300 with conviction, (d) foreign flow inflection to net buy. Currently 0 of 4 confirmed.
- Position sizing adjustment: **MAX 5% of equity per position. Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED. Catalyst-only entries permitted with R:R ≥ 2:1 mandatory and multi-source verification ≤ 2% spread mandatory.**

### Flagged Risks

- **IDR catch-up gap risk at Mon reopen** — Currency hit fresh record 17,970 on Thu May 28 during IDX closure. Spot Fri 17,870. If Mon opens with IDR weakness >17,900 sustained, banking/property/construction names could gap down on FX-pass-through risk; broad IHSG cascade possible.
- **Danantara export regulation Phase 1 effective TODAY** — CPO/coal/ferroalloy exports must now route through state-designated SOE for reporting; full takeover Sep 1. Immediate sector-specific negative for AALI/LSIP/ADRO/ITMG/PTBA/BUMI/HRUM and partial-scope risk for ANTM/INCO/MDKA/NCKL/MBMA (ferroalloy). Watch for sector-wide ARB-down moves at open.
- **MSCI flow reversal risk** — Passive disposal concentrated Fri MOC. T+1 reversion is the base case but residual selling pressure on removed names (esp. illiquid Small Caps) could extend. SIDO/MIKA flow contamination persists ~T+5.
- **PGAS multi-source spread carries** — Third consecutive session with cluster spread >2%. Block-trigger for entry remains active. Data-infra investigation priority.
- **Banking sector strike-1 watch** — BBRI hard-cut May 1 = 1-of-2 sector-exit strike. A second banking hard-cut would force full banking exit. BBCA/BMRI eligibility requires no IHSG cascade AND IDR <17,900 at entry.

### Plan for /market-open

- [ ] **NO COAL / CPO / FERROALLOY entries** — Danantara regulation Phase 1 effective today; sector concentration risk and regulatory ambiguity binding.
- [ ] **NO PROPERTY / CONSTRUCTION / CEMENT entries** — BI 5.25% rate-sensitive headwind.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. PGAS — score 7/10 — Defensive utility + Abadi LNG 15-yr HoA + Danantara SOE channel beneficiary

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Perusahaan Gas Negara is Indonesia's dominant midstream gas utility (SOE) — defensive recurring cash flow benefits structurally from Danantara single-gateway export overhaul because PGAS IS the SOE channel. Newly signed Abadi Masela 15-yr LNG HoA at IPA Convex 2026 ($20.94B Inpex-operated; ops 2029) is a multi-decade structural revenue anchor.
- Thesis: Defensive utility favoured under DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED regime; not MSCI-affected (no removal-flow risk); Q1 revenue trajectory intact.
- Valuation: P/E mid-teens; div yield supportive.
- Catalyst: Abadi LNG HoA + Danantara structural beneficiary + potential Q2 guidance update.
- Analyst view: Buy-tilted; analyst PT zone 2,100 (+13.5% from 1,850).
- Insider activity: None reported.

**Technical context:**
- Price: ~1,820 cluster (Fri carry; outlier 2,400 still gates entry); 52w range tbd.
- Volume: Average; defensive accumulation expected.
- Support: 1,780-1,800; Resistance: 1,920-1,950.
- Trend: range-bound with mild uptrend bias; defensive-utility rotation building.

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: ≤ 1,850 IFF multi-source cluster narrows to ≤2% spread; ideal 1,800-1,830.
- Stop: 1,720 (hard-cut -7% from 1,850).
- Target: 2,100 (+13.5%).
- R:R: 2.0:1 at 1,850; 2.6:1 at 1,800.
- Position size: 5% of equity = ~IDR 490M = ~265,000 sh at 1,850 (within ADV cap).
- Conviction: MEDIUM (catalyst real but data-infra spread keeps it short of HIGH).
- ADV (20-day avg): ~25,000,000 sh.
- **Pre-mortem:** PGAS thesis breaks if (a) IDR breaks 18,000 → broad IDX risk-off cascade; (b) Abadi LNG tender slips materially or Inpex pulls back; (c) multi-source spread persists >5% → SKIP rolls forward; (d) Danantara Phase 1 implementation surprises with utility margin compression.
- **Intermediate pain (-4%):** Hold if cluster narrowed and thesis intact; cover if data-infra spread remains and IHSG <6,000.

#### 2. TLKM — score 7/10 — Telco defensive blue-chip; AGM Jun 8 buyback Rp1T structural-bid catalyst (T-4 trading days)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Indonesia's largest telco SOE; defensive ARPU stream + 7.28% dividend yield + 295T IDR mkt cap = institutional-grade safety asset under DEFENSIVE regime. Not MSCI-affected.
- Thesis: AGM Jun 8 14:00 WIB (eASY.KSEI) with Rp1T buyback agenda (Jun 9 2026 — Jun 8 2027; 10% issued capital cap; 15% min free float) = confirmed structural-bid catalyst within 4 trading days of entry today. SEC 6-K filed; confirmed not withdrawn.
- Valuation: P/E ~12x; div yield 7.28% peer-best.
- Catalyst: Jun 8 AGM buyback authorization; Q1 results affirmed; 2025 Form 20-F filed clean with SEC.
- Analyst view: 17 Buy / 0 Sell; PT 4,051 (+33% vs 3,050 ref).
- Insider activity: No recent disposals; SOE stable; Danantara consolidating 67 subs → 19 entities = governance positive.

**Technical context:**
- Price: ~3,050 (May 26 carry; range data noisy — some refs cite 3,620-3,660 alt source not verified).
- Volume: Average; defensive accumulation pre-AGM.
- Support: 2,950-2,980; Resistance: 3,150.
- Trend: range-bound; defensive rotation building.

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: ≤ 2,930 IFF multi-source spread ≤2%; ideal 2,900-2,920.
- Stop: 2,725 (hard-cut -7% from 2,930).
- Target: 3,350 (+14.3% to consensus midpoint).
- R:R: 2.05:1 at 2,930; 2.2:1 at 2,900.
- Position size: 5% of equity = ~IDR 490M = ~167,000 sh at 2,930.
- Conviction: MEDIUM (catalyst real but well-flagged; AGM "sell the news" compression risk).
- ADV (20-day avg): ~30,000,000 sh.
- **Pre-mortem:** TLKM thesis breaks if (a) AGM buyback withdrawn or downsized vs market expectation; (b) Indonesia regulator imposes new telco fee/tax; (c) IDR shock cascades through telco (equipment import FX cost); (d) Danantara subsidiary restructuring news interpreted negative.
- **Intermediate pain (-4%):** Hold if AGM agenda intact; cover on AGM cancellation or telco-sector regulatory event.

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Indofood CBP (Indomie parent) — most defensive consumer-staple cash flow on IDX. Q1 rev Rp 22T (+7.6% YoY volume intact); EPS Rp 221 (-3.1% mild input cost miss). Post-Idul Adha consumption pulse continues.

### Held Position Updates

- **KLBF**: 519,000 sh @ entry 945. Last carry safe-lower **1,035** (Fri May 29 midday; 9.7% cluster spread > 2% threshold). Unrealized: +9.52%. Stop state: TRAILING 10% @ 931 (state-1 transition fired Fri midday — first of trial). Thesis: intact. Buyback IDR 500B through Jul 2 (~30 days remain). NOT in MSCI removal. Mon closed = no fresh tape. Tue priority: cluster narrowing re-eval; tighten +15%→7% / +20%→5% if applicable. Technical: vs trailing stop 931 = +10.0% buffer. **Action: HOLD.**

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — CONFIRMED — INTENSIFIED (unchanged from Mon Jun 1 carry)**
- Reasoning: 5/5 triggers active. (1) BI 5.25% restrictive. (2) IDR 17,813-17,824 (Fri Bloomberg / TE) — slight improvement vs 17,870 prior carry; below 17,820 escalation trigger but firmly elevated; intra-closure record breach 17,970 Thu May 28 hangs over Tue reopen. (3) IHSG 6,127 carry vs 6,000 psych floor. (4) Foreign net sell Rp 8.52T sesi I Fri — passive outflow concentrated MOC. (5) Danantara Phase 1 effective Day 2 — direct sectoral negative for CPO/coal/ferroalloy. Global tape risk-on (S&P ATH; VIX 15.32) keeps this IDX-specific rather than global stress.
- What would change the label: (a) IDR <17,500 with BI intervention success — partial (Bloomberg 17,813 improvement); (b) BI dovish signal — none; (c) IHSG reclaim 6,300 with conviction — pending Tue tape; (d) foreign flow inflection net buy — pending. 1 of 4 partial (a).
- **Position sizing: MAX 5% of equity per position. Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED. Catalyst-only entries permitted with R:R ≥ 2:1 and multi-source spread ≤ 2% mandatory.**

### Flagged Risks

- **Tue gap-reopen catch-up risk** — IDX closed 4 cumulative days (Wed May 27 + Thu May 28 Idul Adha + Mon Jun 1 Pancasila spans 2 calendar weeks). Tue open packs MSCI rebalance T+2/T+3 reversion + Danantara Phase 1 Day 2 reaction + IDR record breach 17,970 catch-up into one tape. No new entries pre 11:00 WIB; wait for sesi I close before any execution.
- **Newcastle coal at $139.90 favourable BUT sector frozen** — coal complex EXITED per 2-strike rule (ITMG May 18 + ADRO May 20); coal-thesis is a counterfactual cost (no re-entry without full sector-watch reset). Danantara Phase 1 binds. Do not chase.
- **Brent retraced to $92** — May 29 $94.44 → Jun 1 $92.10-92.53 = ~-2.5%. MEDC structural premium fading.
- **IDR forecast high 17,903 Jun 2** — within 17,820-17,900 escalation watch zone. Sustained breach above 17,900 cuts entry posture; 18,000 cascade.
- **PGAS multi-source spread persistence** — third+ session of >2% cluster spread blocks highest-scoring candidate. Data-infra debt (yfinance Day 42 blocked).
- **Banking sector strike-1 watch** — BBRI May 1 hard-cut = 1-of-2 sector-exit strike. Second hard-cut forces full banking exit.
- **MSCI flow T+2/T+3 residual** — Fri Rp 8.52T concentrated; today is reversion base case but residual selling on illiquid Small Caps (SIDO/MIKA/BSDE/AALI etc.) possible.
- **Eagerness check binds** — Week 7 0/3 slots fresh; cumulative trial alpha +17.61% to protect; 4-day forced closure raises forced-entry pressure. Default: PATIENCE.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. KLBF — score 8/10 — HELD; healthcare defensive leader, state-machine transition fired

**Fundamental case (HELD position update):**
- Thesis intact: Q1 2026 rev +10% YoY Rp 9.7T; Distribution & Logistics +21%; GPM 41→38% with OPM stable 14%.
- IDR 500B buyback active through Jul 2 = ~30 calendar days structural bid remaining.
- Defensive sector leadership pattern confirmed (PATTERNS.md): bilateral lag-on-green / outperform-on-red under DEFENSIVE regime.
- NOT in MSCI removal list. MIKA contagion fully avoided.
- Catalyst: continued buyback execution + healthcare-defensive bid sustained under intensifying IDR / CPI stress.
- Analyst view: PT 1,495 (16 Buy / 0 Sell); high 1,975 / low 1,070.

**Technical context:**
- Price: 1,035 safe-lower carry (Fri May 29) / 1,135 Yahoo Tue Jun 2 = ~9.7% cluster spread > 2% threshold. Data-infra Day 43 outage persists.
- Volume: TBD Wed open; defensive bid pattern suggests outperform-on-red bias.
- vs 50DMA/200DMA: above both (entry 945 mid-May, base 920-940).
- Support: 950–970 / 920 hard floor. Resistance: 1,100–1,135.
- Trend: uptrend intact.

**Stop state:** TRAILING 10% @ 931 (state-1 transition fired Fri May 29 midday).
- +15% trigger @ 1,087 → 7% trail @ 1,011.
- +20% trigger @ 1,134 → 5% trail @ 1,077.

**Action:** HOLD. Wed priority — re-evaluate multi-source cluster for narrowing. If cluster converges ≤2% spread AND mark ≥1,087, tighten trail to 7%. If ≥1,134, tighten to 5%. Yahoo single-source 1,135 (Tue) alone would cross +20% threshold, but cluster confirmation required (data-quality discipline binds; not discretion).
- ADV: ~30,000,000 sh.
- **Pre-mortem:** Thesis breaks if (a) buyback program suspended pre Jul 2; (b) IDR-pressure leads to forced healthcare price control / regulation; (c) defensive bid pattern fails on a red day (loses sector premium).
- **Intermediate pain (mark to ≤980, ~−5% from carry):** Re-validate buyback execution + cluster narrowing before any sell-side action; state-1 trailing 931 binds non-discretionarily.

- Thesis: Telkom Indonesia — dominant integrated telco; structural-bid catalyst Mon Jun 8 14:00 WIB AGM agenda includes Rp1T buyback approval running Jun 9 2026 → Jun 8 2027 (10% cap, 15% min free float).

- Support: ~2,800. Resistance: 3,150–3,200.
- Trend: range with mild improving bias on AGM-event approach.
- NOT MSCI-affected.

- Position size: 5% of equity = ~165,000 sh at 2,930 (regime cap).

- ADV: ~45,000,000 sh (deep liquidity).

- **Intermediate pain (−4% to 2,815):** Hold if AGM agenda intact AND IDR <18,000; cover if AGM news adverse OR IDR breaks 18,000.

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Indofood Sukses Makmur — parent of ICBP plus integrated plantation/agri exposure; defensive FMCG + commodity hedge.

### Held Position Updates

- **KLBF**: 519,000 sh @ entry 945. Last carry safe-lower **1,035** (Fri May 29; 9.7% cluster spread > 2% threshold; Yahoo Tue 1,135 single-source). Unrealized: +9.52% on safe-lower carry. Stop state: TRAILING 10% @ 931 (state-1 transition fired Fri midday — first of trial). Thesis: intact. Buyback IDR 500B through Jul 2 (~30 days remain). NOT in MSCI removal. Technical: vs trailing stop 931 = +10.0% buffer on safe-lower; +18.0% on Yahoo single-source. **Action: HOLD.** Wed priority: cluster narrowing re-eval; tighten +15%→7% / +20%→5% if cluster narrows ≤2% AND mark crosses thresholds.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — CONFIRMED — INTENSIFIED (5/5 + IDR escalation breach + CPI re-acceleration)**

- Reasoning: 5/5 triggers active and worsening on three fronts vs prior carry. (1) BI 5.25% restrictive UNCHANGED + Jun 17-18 BOG hike risk RISING (Kenanga 50% prob → effectively higher post-CPI). (2) **IDR 17,879 ABOVE 17,820 escalation trigger by 59bps** (vs Fri partial-improvement carry 17,813); 17,900 sustain ladder approaching; 18,000 cascade quiet but armed. (3) IHSG 6,195.43 sesi II — recovered above 6,100 floor but well below 6,300 reclaim threshold; sesi II gave back +38bps of sesi I gains = real distribution behavior. (4) Foreign net sell Rp 1.39T Tue (improvement vs Fri −8.52T BUT **still net sell on a +1.11% green day** = structurally insistent outflow regime). (5) Danantara Phase 1 Day 3 (CPO/coal/ferroalloy DSI gateway) + **NEW: DHE SDA PP 21/2026 effective Jun 1** (non-mining 100%/12m, mining 30%/3m, 50% IDR conversion cap, PPh incentive to 0%) — twin structural friction layer. **(6) NEW: CPI 3.08% YoY May print** (vs 2.42% April) = re-acceleration directly opposing the BI dovish/hold path and validating the +25bp hike scenario.
- What would change the label: (a) IDR <17,500 with sustained BI intervention success — NO (now 17,879, deteriorating); (b) BI dovish signal — NO (CPI 3.08% re-accel argues opposite); (c) IHSG reclaim 6,300 with conviction — NO (carry 6,195); (d) foreign flow inflection net buy — NO (still −Rp 1.39T on a green day). **0 of 4 triggers active.** Regime deepening.
- **Position sizing: MAX 5% of equity per position. Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED. Catalyst-only entries permitted with R:R ≥ 2:1 and multi-source spread ≤ 2% mandatory.**

### Flagged Risks

- **IDR 17,879 above 17,820 escalation trigger** — DIRECT macro risk; 17,900 sustain ladder ~21bps away; 18,000 cascade ~120bps away. Any sesi I reopen breach of 17,900 sustained = cuts entry posture; 18,000 breach = halt-and-de-risk discussion. Pre-emptive de-risk rule binds.
- **CPI 3.08% re-acceleration** (vs 2.42% April) — DIRECTLY adverse for consumer staples (ICBP/INDF wheat/sugar/dairy import-cost), rate-sensitive DCF names (JSMR), and banking NIM/asset-quality (BBCA/BMRI). Raises Jun 17-18 BI +25bp hike probability above the Kenanga 50% prior.
- **DHE SDA PP 21/2026 effective Jun 1 (Day 3)** — non-mining 100%/12m placement; mining 30%/3m; 50% IDR conversion cap. PGAS gas-export proceeds subject to non-mining 100%/12m = funding-mix friction risk. Tax incentive offsets compliance cost; net medium-term IDR-supportive but near-term operational drag.
- **Danantara Phase 1 Day 3** — Jakarta Post Jun 3 "industry braces for on-the-ground reality" headline = operational frictions emerging. Coal/CPO/ferroalloy export reporting via DSI continues to overhang ADRO/ITMG/PTBA/BUMI/HRUM (SECTOR EXITED) + AALI/LSIP/ANTM/INCO/NCKL/MBMA (WATCH/SKIP).
- **Newcastle coal $139.90 9-week high** — favourable on commodity bull but **sector EXITED** per 2-strike rule; counterfactual opportunity cost is real but cannot be acted on. No re-entry without full sector-watch reset.
- **PGAS multi-source cluster spread** — 3rd+ session of >2% spread blocks highest-scoring fundamental candidate. Data-infra debt (yfinance Day 43 blocked) is the binding bottleneck — codified for Week 7 weekend infra patch per prior weekly review.
- **Banking sector strike-1 watch** — BBRI May 1 hard-cut = 1-of-2 sector-exit strike. Second hard-cut forces full banking exit. CPI/IDR re-pricing raises BBCA/BMRI/BBNI marginal forced-cut risk.
- **MSCI Small Cap removal T+3 residual** — Fri T+0, Mon Holiday T+0, Tue T+1, Wed T+2 — SIDO/MIKA/ANTM/AALI/BSDE residual passive-sell pressure may continue. Avoid contaminated names.
- **Foreign net sell on a +1.11% green day** — Tue −Rp 1.39T = passive money still leaving even on rally; not a confirmation that risk-on is back. ASII −Rp 244B + TPIA −Rp 254B = LQ45 large-cap distribution continues.
- **Eagerness check binds** — Week 7 0/3 slots fresh; cumulative trial alpha ~+17.10% to protect; 4-day cumulative closure raised forced-entry pressure that did not materialize on Tue. Default: **PATIENCE; 0-2 new entries through Wed-Fri**.

### Top Candidates (deep-dive)

#### 1. TLKM — Highest-conviction near-term defined catalyst (AGM T+4 + buyback revised up to Rp 3T)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Telkom Indonesia (Persero) Tbk — incumbent integrated telco SOE; AGM Mon Jun 8 14:00 WIB to ratify Rp 3T buyback (revised UP from prior Rp 1T research carry — confirmed via fresh Jun 4 search), live execution start Tue Jun 9 = immediate structural bid.
- Thesis: Danantara consolidation 67 → 19 subsidiaries = governance positive over 6-12m; recurring data-revenue cash flow defensive in regime.
- Valuation: 7.25% dividend yield (revised from 7.28% prior); mkt cap ~295T; P/E ~11x.
- Catalyst: AGM T+4; buyback execution start Jun 9; the upgraded Rp 3T (vs prior Rp 1T) is a 3× scale-up = stronger structural bid.
- Analyst view: 17 Buy / 0 Sell; PT 4,051 (+39.7% vs 2,900 entry zone).
- Insider activity: Danantara effective largest stakeholder.
- NOT MSCI- or FTSE-affected.
- Foreign sell Wed −Rp 16.74B (modest distribution; not concentrated).

**Technical context:**
- Price: 2,900 (Jun 3 close per multi-source) vs 2,950 prior close = −1.69% on Wed.
- Support: 2,800. Resistance: 3,150–3,200.
- Trend: range with mild defensive bias on AGM-event approach + buyback upgrade.
- NOT MSCI-affected.

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: ≤ 2,900 IFF (a) multi-source spread ≤2% Thu open, (b) AGM agenda confirmed not withdrawn pre-open, (c) IHSG sesi I ≥5,899 floor hold, (d) IDR not breaking 18,000 sustained, (e) post 11:00 WIB execution window per pre-emptive de-risk rule.

- R:R: 1.23:1 at 2,900 (BELOW 2:1 floor → STRICT entry ≤ 2,840 to achieve 2:1 with target 3,150).
- Position size: 5% of equity = ~170,000 sh at 2,840 (regime cap).
- Conviction: MEDIUM-HIGH (best near-term defined catalyst on slate; only slate name with confirmed buyback scale-up).
- ADV: ~45,000,000 sh (deep liquidity; ADV gate clears comfortably).
- **Pre-mortem:** Breaks if (a) AGM agenda withdraws buyback item; (b) Danantara consolidation tax/restructuring drag exceeds 2-3% near-term EPS hit; (c) regulatory pricing action on telco tariffs; (d) IDR cascade > 18,000 triggers panic-flush continuation.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — DEEPENING (5/5 + IDR escalation breach + CPI re-accel + MOODY'S DANANTARA OUTLOOK NEGATIVE shock fresh)**

- Reasoning: Wed Jun 3 = first major panic-flush day of trial. IHSG −4.11% close 5,941.07 = 13-month low. Five-pronged compounding catalysts vindicated the DEFENSIVE label: (1) Moody's Danantara Baa2 negative outlook = sovereign-adjacent governance shock cascading through bank-led / SOE-adjacent / LQ45 mega-cap names; (2) IDR 17,976 ABOVE 17,900 sustain ladder, ~25bps from 18,000 cascade trigger (some reports cite banks already trading USD above 18,000); (3) MSCI/FTSE T+3/T+4 residual flow; (4) Foreign net sell Wed sesi I −Rp 525.4B (BBCA/TPIA/BBRI/DSSA top-sold); (5) CPI 3.08% re-acceleration validating BI Jun 17-18 +25bp hike scenario.
- What would change the label to RISK-NEUTRAL: (a) IDR recovers below 17,500 with sustained BI intervention — NO (17,976 escalating); (b) BI dovish signal — NO (CPI 3.08% argues opposite); (c) IHSG reclaim 6,300 with conviction — NO (5,941); (d) foreign flow inflection to net buy — NO. **0 of 4 triggers active.** Regime label DEEPENING.
- **Position sizing: MAX 5% of equity per position. Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED. Catalyst-only entries permitted with R:R ≥ 2:1 and multi-source spread ≤ 2% mandatory.**

### Flagged Risks

- ⚠️ **KLBF position breach: 4-source Wed close convergence at 745 = below trailing 931 GTC. Position carries −21.16% from entry on safe-lower revised mark. Market-open Thu must reconcile cluster and execute trailing fire on multi-source confirmation.** This is the trial's FIRST trailing-stop fire if executed. Per design, the trailing stop caps loss at trail-pct × HWM = floor protection vs gap-down slippage. Realized loss range: −1.48% (best case, fill @ 931) to −21.16% (worst case, fill @ 745 gap-down) on position.

Government response (Finance Minister Purbaya: "not new, reflects sovereign outlook") = damage-control posture; market may continue to fade conglomerate / SOE-adjacent / bank-led names.
- **CPI 3.08% re-acceleration** — validates BI Jun 17-18 +25bp scenario; adversely DCFs JSMR/rate-sensitive names; hits consumer staples wheat/sugar/dairy GPM; banking NIM/asset-quality watch.
- **DHE SDA PP 21/2026 Day 4 effective** — non-mining 100%/12m placement, mining 30%/3m, 50% IDR conversion cap. PGAS gas-export proceeds subject = funding-mix friction risk peripheral.
- **Danantara Phase 1 Day 4** — Jakarta Post Jun 3 "industry braces" headline = operational frictions emerging. Reinforces coal/CPO/ferroalloy sector caution (already EXITED or WATCH).
- **Newcastle coal $140 9-wk high** — favourable counterfactual but sector EXITED per 2-strike rule. No re-entry without full sector-watch reset.
- **PGAS multi-source cluster spread persisting FOUR consecutive sessions** — Mon/Tue/Wed/Thu = data-infra debt binding. Codified as Week 7 weekend infra patch priority.
- **Banking strike-1 watch (BBRI)** — Wed −3.28% on Moody's shock + Rp 195.2B foreign sell = strike-2 risk elevated. Avoid all bank entries.
- **MSCI/FTSE removal T+3/T+4 residual** — BSDE/SIDO/MIKA/ANTM/AALI passive sell pressure; GOTO FTSE-removal Jun 3 fresh add.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. TLKM — Mon Jun 8 AGM + Rp 4T buyback (REVISED UP from Rp 3T) + 7.25% div yield (HIGH-priority defined catalyst T+3)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Telekomunikasi Indonesia is the IDX's largest defensive telco/SOE name with a binary-shaped near-term catalyst (Mon Jun 8 electronic AGM at 14:00 WIB) ratifying a Rp 4,000,000,000,000 share buyback under POJK 29/2023 framework — revised UP from prior carry of Rp 3T per fresh Friday TipRanks/Stocktitan SEC filing 6-K confirmation.
- Thesis: Execution start Tue Jun 9 2026 → Mon Jun 8 2027 = immediate structural bid at scale (capped 10% of issued capital, min 15% free float preserved); funded entirely from internal cash. Combined with 7.25% dividend yield and 17 Buy / 0 Sell analyst consensus (PT 4,051), creates a defined T+3 catalyst with multi-quarter structural support.
- Valuation: TLKM P/E ~12x trailing vs sector median ~15x (carry); P/B ~1.7x vs sector ~2.0x; div yield 7.25% vs telco peer avg 4-5%.
- Catalyst: AGM Mon Jun 8 14:00 WIB (T+3 trading days); buyback execution Tue Jun 9 (T+4); dividend distribution per Mon resolution.
- Analyst view: 17 Buy / 0 Sell consensus; PT 4,051 (~+40% upside from 2,900 Wed carry).
- Insider activity: Buyback IS the institutional accumulation signal at scale; no individual director/commissioner transactions reported.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 2,900 (Wed close; foreign sell modest −Rp 16.7B; not MSCI/FTSE affected); Thu pre-market mark not multi-source convergent
- 50-day MA: ~2,950 carry (below); 200-day MA: ~3,000 carry (below); both above current = downtrend technically
- Volume: ADV ~80-100M sh carry (high liquidity; gate 7/14 PASS comfortably)
- Support: IDR 2,800; Resistance: IDR 3,000 (50-day), then 3,100
- Trend: short-term downtrend tracking IHSG; AGM/buyback catalyst is the inflection thesis

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR ≤2,900 (Wed carry; multi-source verification mandatory at open with ≤2% spread)
- Stop: IDR 2,697 (entry × 0.93 = −7% hard cut); position size at entry IDR 2,900 risks IDR 203/sh.
- Target: IDR 3,480 (+20%) → minimum 2:1 R:R per Strategy entry-checklist (203 risk × 2 = 406 → 3,306 conservative target; 3,480 = stretch). PT 4,051 = +40% stretch.
- R:R ratio: 2.86:1 at conservative target; 5.7:1 at PT — well above 2:1 floor.
- Position size: 5% of equity (DEFENSIVE cap binding per MACRO-REGIME.md Week 7 regime). 5% × IDR 9,856M = IDR 492.8M. At 2,900 = ~170,000 sh (round to 170,000 = 1,700 lots).
- Conviction: HIGH (defined catalyst T+3; multi-track confirmation; not MSCI/FTSE affected; sector momentum mixed but TLKM telco-defensive uniquely positioned).
- ADV (20-day avg daily volume): ~80,000,000 sh carry (gate 14: 170,000 sh = 0.21% of ADV ≪ 10% cap — PASS comfortably).
- **Pre-mortem:** Catalyst-invalidator-in-5-days = (a) AGM postpones beyond Jun 8, (b) buyback amount revised DOWN below Rp 2T at AGM, (c) IHSG breaches sub-5,800 with broad-tape conviction (carries TLKM despite catalyst) — close immediately on any of these regardless of stop.
- **Intermediate pain:** If TLKM hits −4% (IDR 2,784) intra-week pre-AGM = hold if thesis intact (AGM date confirmed + buyback amount not revised down) OR cover if intermediate-pain rule fires on AGM-postpone news.

#### 2. UNVR — FY26 IDR 193/sh dividend (~12.5% yield) + Sariwangi/ice-cream divestiture + Wed defensive bid

- Thesis: Unilever Indonesia surfaced as a clear defensive-rotation winner Wed (+2.40% on IHSG −4.11% = +6.51% daily alpha) as consumer FMCG bid sustained through the Moody's/Danantara panic flush. The structural thesis is a total FY26 dividend of IDR 193/sh combining the regular dividend with proceeds from Sariwangi and ice-cream divestitures, implying ~12.5% yield at the Wed pre-flush mark — among the highest yields in LQ45 outside finance.

#### 4. MYOR — MENA/Saudi export IDR-weak tailwind = REGIME-FRIENDLY uniquely + cluster convergence

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: MYOR is the rare LQ45 name where IDR weakness is structurally net-positive due to MENA / Saudi Arabia export exposure (export revenue in USD/SAR while costs predominantly IDR-denominated). In a regime where IDR/USD is pinned within 30bps of the 18,000 cascade trigger, MYOR's USD-revenue base provides an asymmetric margin tailwind that is regime-friendly UNIQUELY among the consumer FMCG names (UNVR/ICBP/INDF are domestic-IDR exposed).
- Thesis: Wed pre-open multi-source cluster CONVERGED to 1,840-1,855 = 0.8% spread within 2% threshold (rare cluster pass under data-infra outage Day 45); FY26 sector +5.7% revenue growth applies + MYOR-specific export tailwind on top.

- Catalyst: IDR-weak regime is the operative catalyst (already-in-tape, ongoing); no defined T+X event.
- Analyst view: BUY (selected broker pool); top sector pick alongside ICBP for FY26 growth profile.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — DEEPENING — CONTINUATION-VINDICATED (5/5 + IDR escalation breach + CPI re-accel + Moody's Danantara fresh shock + Wed-Thu continuation flush vindicated; 2-day cumulative-alpha expansion +21.41%→+23.10%)**

- Reasoning: All 5 DEFENSIVE triggers active and intensifying: (a) BI rate restrictive 5.25%, (b) IDR 17,930-17,977 within 30bps of 18,000 cascade ladder + 17,900 sustain breached, (c) IHSG sub-6,000 → sub-5,900 continuation (Thu close 5,839.79 = -23.5% from Day 0 baseline), (d) Moody's Danantara Baa2 negative outlook sovereign-adjacent governance shock unresolved (T+1 from announcement), (e) foreign-flow STRUCTURAL outflow YTD -Rp 45T despite Thu sesi I net buy +Rp 179B inflection (single-session, not yet full-day reversed). Cumulative trial alpha +23.10% (revised from +23.44% Day 34 carry on multi-source IHSG anchor reconciliation 5,734.25 sesi I → 5,839.79 sesi II final) represents the strongest absolute alpha position of the trial. Regime-flip triggers: 0 of 4 active (IDR ≥17,500 still elevated; BI hawkish; IHSG below 6,300; foreign-flow Thu inflection 1-session only). Position sizing adjustment: MAX 5% of equity cap binding (Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED). Catalyst-only entries permitted with R:R ≥ 2:1 (strict) + multi-source spread ≤ 2% mandatory.

### Flagged Risks

1. **IDR 18,000 cascade trigger ~30bps away (PRIMARY KILL-SWITCH ARMED-ESCALATING)** — banks reportedly selling USD above 18,000 per one report Wed; sustained breach = pre-emptive de-risk on KLBF carry evaluation + halt all new entries per discipline.
2. **KLBF cluster non-convergence Day 45** — TradingView/Stockbit/Liputan6/Bisnis Wed convergence at 745 vs Yahoo dissent 710-1,135 vs Investing.com 930 = no actionable single mark; trailing 931 GTC armed but cannot fire pre-emptively per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-01 discipline (≥3 source convergence ≤2% spread required). If real-tape KLBF is in fact 745-770 range, position is at -19% to -22% unrealized vs entry 945 (cluster low) but procedural rule binds.
3. **Moody's Danantara outlook negative + sovereign-adjacent governance shock** — T+1 follow-through risk if Indonesian government response stretches the credibility tail (Finance Min Purbaya "not new, reflects sovereign outlook" damage-control posture insufficient). Continuation flush risk = additional sub-5,800 leg.
4. **MSCI Indonesia classification announcement Jun 18 overhang (10 trading days)** — risk of further capital-market reform downgrade; binding patience constraint on new entries through Jun 18.
5. **Friday weekly-review afternoon trigger** — Week 7 = 0/3 trades used + cumulative alpha trial-high carry; risk of forced-entry pressure ("eagerness check") on weekly grade vanity. Regime calls FOR patience binding; 4 candidates all gate-borderline on R:R.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. TLKM — AGM TODAY 14:00 WIB binary catalyst day + Rp 4T buyback approval (HIGH-priority — but de-risk regime binds entry pre-AGM)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: PT Telekomunikasi Indonesia is the IDX's largest defensive telco/SOE name with a TODAY binary-shaped catalyst — electronic AGM at 14:00 WIB ratifying Rp 4,000,000,000,000 share buyback under POJK 29/2023 framework (revised UP from earlier Rp 1T plan per multi-source confirmation: SEC 6-K + Globe & Mail TipRanks + Stocktitan + Bisnis Market + IDNFinancials + IDXChannel + Bareksa).
- Thesis: Execution start Tue Jun 9 2026 → Mon Jun 8 2027 = immediate structural bid at scale (capped 10% of issued capital, min 15% free float preserved); funded entirely from internal cash. EPS lift from IDR 179.8 → 183.1 confirmed per Globe & Mail. Combined with 7.25% dividend yield and 17 Buy / 0 Sell analyst consensus (PT 4,051), creates a TODAY-defined catalyst with multi-quarter structural support.
- Valuation: TLKM P/E ~12x trailing vs sector median ~15x (carry); P/B ~1.7x vs sector ~2.0x; div yield 7.25% vs telco peer avg 4-5%.
- Catalyst: AGM TODAY 14:00 WIB (T+0 catalyst); buyback execution Tue Jun 9 (T+1); confirmation cadence intraday post-resolution announcement.
- Analyst view: 17 Buy / 0 Sell consensus; PT 4,051 (~+47% upside from Fri close 2,760).
- Insider activity: Buyback IS the institutional accumulation signal at scale; no individual director/commissioner transactions reported.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 2,760 (Fri close; -4.83% from Wed 2,900 carry); Mon pre-open mark not multi-source convergent
- 50-day MA: ~2,900 carry (below); 200-day MA: ~3,000 carry (below); both above current = downtrend technically
- Volume: ADV ~80-100M sh carry (high liquidity; gate 7/14 PASS comfortably)
- Support: IDR 2,700; Resistance: IDR 2,900 (50-day), then 3,000
- Trend: short-term downtrend tracking IHSG panic-flush; AGM/buyback catalyst is the TODAY inflection thesis

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR ≤2,760 (Fri close zone — but multi-source verification mandatory at open with ≤2% spread); chase cap Gate 9 ≤+3% → ≤2,843 absolute cap
- Stop: IDR 2,567 (entry × 0.93 at 2,760 = -7% hard cut)
- Target: IDR 3,312 (+20%) → minimum 2:1 R:R per Strategy entry-checklist (193 risk × 2 = 386 → 3,146 conservative; 3,312 = stretch). PT 4,051 = +47% stretch.
- R:R ratio: 2.86:1 at conservative target; 6.7:1 at PT — well above 2:1 floor.
- Position size: 5% of equity (DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED cap binding). 5% × IDR 9,856M = IDR 492.8M. At 2,760 = ~178,000 sh (round to 178,000 = 1,780 lots).
- Conviction: HIGH (defined catalyst TODAY; multi-source SEC-filing-grade confirmation; not MSCI/FTSE affected; telco-defensive uniquely positioned).
- ADV (20-day avg daily volume): ~80,000,000 sh carry (gate 14: 178,000 sh = 0.22% of ADV ≪ 10% cap — PASS comfortably).
- **Pre-mortem (5-day invalidator):** (a) AGM postpones beyond Mon Jun 8 unexpectedly, (b) buyback amount revised DOWN below Rp 2T at AGM resolution, (c) IHSG breaches sub-5,500 with broad-tape conviction (carries TLKM despite catalyst), (d) **IDR cascade-breach escalates ≥18,200 next cascade tier** — close immediately on any of these regardless of stop.
- **Intermediate pain:** If TLKM hits −4% (IDR 2,650) intra-week post-AGM = hold if thesis intact (buyback amount confirmed not reduced) OR cover if intermediate-pain rule fires on adverse news.
- **DE-RISK REGIME GATE BINDING:** IDR ≥18,000 sustained pre-open = NO new entries possible per Week 7 process change. Re-evaluate ONLY if IDR recovers <18,000 sustained intraday Mon AND post-AGM resolution announcement confirms ≥Rp 4T at 14:00 WIB AND multi-source ≤2% convergence reached AND Gate 9 chase cap ≤+3% from plan.

#### 4. MYOR — RUPST Thu dividend Rp 60/sh + ASEAN/MENA export = IDR-weak regime-friendly + cocoa/coffee easing = margin tailwind

- Thesis: PT Mayora Indah is uniquely positioned as the only large-cap consumer staple in the watchlist with material ASEAN/MENA export exposure — explicit IDR-weak regime tailwind in 18,015-18,045 cascade-breach environment. Domestic competitors (UNVR/ICBP) face IDR import-cost headwind via raw materials; MYOR's MENA/Saudi export revenue is USD-receivable that converts INTO weaker IDR at higher rates.
- Thesis: RUPST Thu Jun 4 decided Rp 60/sh cash dividend FY25 (yield ~3.4% at 1,760 close). Cocoa and coffee input prices easing into 2026 = margin recovery tailwind. PE 15.5x carry = mid-range valuation.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — DEEPENING — CASCADE-BREACHED (5/5 triggers active + IDR 18,000 cascade kill-switch BREACHED 2nd-session sustained Fri+Mon pre-open)**
- Reasoning: Week 7 closed with IHSG -8.69% on-Fri-Mon-baseline / -6% Mon-to-Fri; IDR cascade-breach sustained 2nd session (Fri 18,033 close → Mon pre-open ~18,015-18,045 cluster); US risk-off Fri (Nasdaq -4.18% / VIX +39.68% = vol regime fear-spike); Indo 10Y SUN to 6.90% / US 10Y to 4.544%; Moody's Danantara overhang + MSCI Jun 18 announcement overhang (8 trading days) + Mid-East geopolitical risk-off bid + Foreign exodus extreme acceleration. ### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. TLKM — Rp 4T buyback execution starts TODAY + Rp 21.9T dividend cum Jun 19 = fresh dual catalyst

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: AGM Mon Jun 8 approved Rp 4 trillion share buyback (max 10% of shares, 12-month execution starting TODAY Jun 9 2026 through Jun 8 2027), 4× the original Rp 1T plan. Adds structural daily bid floor against tape continuation. EPS lift estimated 179.8 → 183.1 (+1.8%).
- Thesis: AGM also approved Rp 21.9 trillion cash dividend distribution (Rp 17.8T from FY25 net profit + Rp 4.2T from retained earnings); payment Jul 10 to shareholders of record Jun 19. Cum date Jun 19 = 8 trading days. Estimated yield 7-8% at carry price 2,700-2,800.
- Valuation: P/E sector low; carry 2,760 Fri Jun 5 (per prior research); Mon Jun 8 likely closed lower with -4.52% IHSG sesi II flush. Mirae/Mandiri Sek BUY-rated as only telco worth maintaining.
- Catalyst: (a) Buyback execution starts TODAY (Tue Jun 9) — structural bid begins; (b) Cum-date Jun 19 (T+8) = dividend yield trade compresses; (c) Telco defensive sector continues post-Mon flush.
- Analyst view: Mandiri Sek "only telco worth holding" carry; multi-broker BUY.
- Insider activity: Buyback IS the insider-equivalent action.

**Technical context:**
- Price: Fri close 2,760; Mon Jun 8 close estimated 2,550-2,650 (rough based on -4.52% IHSG sesi II flush + telco-sector LQ45 -5.50% drag); multi-source NOT convergent at filing.
- 50-day MA: carry ~2,900; 200-day MA: ~3,100; below both = downtrend
- Volume: ADV high (mega-cap LQ45)
- Support: IDR 2,500 psych; Resistance: IDR 2,900 (50-day) then 3,000
- Trend: downtrend until buyback execution provides reversal signal

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR ≤2,650 (Mon Jun 8 mark needs verification; Gate 9 chase cap ≤+3% from confirmed close)
- Stop: IDR 2,465 (entry × 0.93)
- Target: IDR 3,180 (+20% conservative; well below 50-day 2,900 + buffer)
- R:R ratio: ~2.86:1 conservative (≥2:1 floor satisfied)
- Position size: 5% of equity = IDR 492.8M / 2,650 = ~186,000 sh (1,860 lots)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (catalyst dual-fresh + buyback bid; but regime gate binds; Mon -8%+ continuation risk requires fresh Tue tape verification)
- ADV: solid (LQ45 mega-cap)
- **Pre-mortem:** TLKM breaks 2,500 psych = capitulation continuation despite buyback active; close immediately. Also buyback announced amount cut/delayed.
- **Intermediate pain:** If TLKM hits -4% (IDR 2,544) = reassess vs IHSG same-day; if -7% hard-cut 2,465 binding = full close.
- **DE-RISK REGIME GATE BINDING:** IDR 18,171 ≥18,000 sustained 3rd session + approaching 18,200 next cascade tier = NO entry possible. Re-evaluate ONLY on IDR recovery <18,000 sustained intraday.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — INFLECTION-PENDING (5/5 triggers still active but IDR off cascade-breach pre-open; awaiting ≥2-session sustained <18,000 confirmation + Wed IHSG hold above 5,500 reclaim)**

- Reasoning: Tue Jun 9 brought the LARGEST single-day IHSG gain of the trial (+7.57% / +404.51 pt to 5,746.65) on the back of BI's off-cycle +25bp hike to 5.50%, a textbook currency-defensive monetary signal. IDR rebounded -3.4% from intraday peak 18,234 to Wed pre-open trough ~17,988 / prev close 17,916.7 = first sub-18,000 session in 4. However: (a) BCA bank reference rate Tue 08:26 WIB still 18,125-18,215 showing inter-source spread; (b) regime DOWNGRADE requires ≥2 consecutive sessions of sustained recovery — Wed will be confirmation session if it holds; (c) MSCI Jun 18 binary overhang STILL only 6 trading days away; (d) Moody's Danantara sovereign overhang persists; (e) BI rate now at 5.50% restrictive = compounded property/construction/cement headwind. **Procedural call:** regime label stays DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED with INFLECTION-PENDING tag; pre-emptive de-risk gate transitions from BINDING-FULL to SOFT-BINDING (re-gate candidates allowed at 09:15 IF Wed sesi I IDR multi-source <18,000 ≤2% spread).
- Position sizing adjustment: 5% cap binding; Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED; **IDR inflection-pending means candidates EVALUABLE at 09:15 under full 15-gate procedure rather than pre-emptive blanket-block.**
- Regime shift triggers (de-escalation to DEFENSIVE-CONFIRMED): (a) IDR <18,000 sustained ≥2 sessions ✓ (1 of 2 confirmed at Wed pre-open), (b) Moody's outlook stabilization (not confirmed), (c) IHSG reclaim 5,500 sustained ✓ (1 confirmed at 5,746.65; need ≥2 sessions), (d) foreign flow inflection to net buy (Tue broker summary pending; banks attracted bids).
- Regime shift triggers (escalation to DEFENSIVE-CASCADE-PEAK): (a) IDR ≥18,200 next cascade tier = SEND ALERT (currently ~17,988; far below); (b) IHSG break 5,500 with conviction (NOT triggered — IHSG +7.57% above; needs ≥-3% Wed flush to retest), (c) MSCI Jun 18 announcement downgrade outcome, (d) Moody's sovereign downgrade.

- **TLKM Mon ARB -14.86% to 2,350** — capitulation-low; bounce dynamics fragile; Tue close pending multi-source. ARB-bounce trades have historically been mean-reversion-prone.

- **Higher Indo 10Y SUN yield 7.40%** — duration headwind for property/construction/cement sectors; BI rate 5.50% restrictive position. Sectoral downgrade in this entry reflects this.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. BBCA — 5-day rebound continuation post-BI off-cycle hike; defensive-quality re-rate; Gate 9 chase risk binds

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: BBCA closed Thu Jun 11 at IDR 5,600 +8.74% — a powerful 5-day rebound continuation after the Wed Jun 3 panic-flush to 5-year lows. Post-BI off-cycle +25bp hike (Tue Jun 9, BI-Rate now 5.50%), the broader market has staged a relief rally, with IHSG +7.57% Tue +2.71% Wed and mean-reverting moderation Thu sesi II 5,886.03. BBCA is leading the bank-rebound on defensive-quality flight.
- Thesis: Indo 10Y SUN eased -4bp to 7.44% Thu providing duration support. IDR sub-18,000 3rd-sustained-session = de-escalation criterion (a) FULLY CONFIRMED. BBCA's fortress balance sheet + ~3.5% defensive dividend yield + CASA dominance makes it the leading mega-cap defensive instrument as the regime de-escalation thesis builds.
- Valuation: P/E mid-cycle; specific Thu close metrics not freshly sourced (web-search single-source 5,600); long-term own-history P/E mean (Defensive Quality Track) NOT recomputed Week 8 — track suspended per regime; would need ≥1.0σ below 10-year mean trigger to engage.
- Catalyst: (a) Bank-sector rebound continuation Day 5; (b) BI RDG Jun 17-18 dovish-pause possibility; (c) MSCI Jun 18 binary risk: a positive review = double-tailwind, negative = sell-off; (d) Defensive-bid foreign re-allocation if regime de-escalates fully.
- Analyst view: Most rec held; sector rec POSITIVE post-stabilization.
- Insider activity: None recent.

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 5,600 (Thu close +8.74%); 5-day rebound from ~5,000 panic-flush low
- 50-day MA: ~6,200 (carry); 200-day MA: ~7,100; both ABOVE — still in technical downtrend recovery
- Volume: Above-avg on Thu +8.74% surge
- Support: IDR 5,150 (Jun 10 prev close); Resistance: IDR 5,800 then 6,000 psych
- Trend: 5-day rebound reversal — 1st test of 6,000 resistance Fri

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 5,600 (Thu close anchor); Gate 9 chase cap ≤+3% = ≤5,768 cap
- **BUT** the +8.74% Thu rally itself is the Gate 9 problem — entering Fri at the Thu close is BUYING THE +8.74% ALREADY-CHASED top of the relief bounce per the "no chase" discipline + post-relief-bounce mean-reversion risk on Fri (statistically extended +25% bank rebound in 5 sessions).
- Stop: IDR 5,208 (entry × 0.93 = -7% hard cut)
- Target: IDR 6,720 (+20% conservative; would test resistance 6,000 + buffer)
- R:R ratio: 2.86:1 (≥2:1 floor satisfied)

- **DECISION: SKIP** — **Gate 9 chase cap fails** at Fri open from Thu +8.74% close anchor. Eagerness Check binds: +22.72% cumulative alpha protected, weekly review TODAY 16:00 WIB, 5% cap rendering position contribution limited. Better to wait for either (a) Fri pullback ≥-3% from Thu close cleansing Gate 9; OR (b) regime FULL de-escalation Friday afternoon weekly review = lifts to 10-15% cap.
- **Better path:** Re-evaluate at weekly review 16:00 WIB; consider Week 9 (Mon Jun 15) gate run if Fri close pulls back ≥-3% or BI RDG dovish signal builds confidence into Tue-Wed.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — DE-ESCALATION-CONFIRMING (5/5 DEFENSIVE triggers degrading meaningfully; 2 of 4 de-escalation criteria FULLY CONFIRMED — (a) IDR 3-sustained sub-18,000 + (c) IHSG sustained reclaim of 5,500; (b) Moody's outlook stabilization + (d) foreign flow inflection NOT confirmed; formal regime label change pending today's weekly review)**

- **Reasoning:** Three structural shifts in the past 4 sessions: (1) IDR 3rd-sustained sub-18,000 close = cascade kill-switch LIFTED-CONFIRMED; (2) IHSG sustained reclaim of 5,500 with 5,886.03 Thu close = panic-flush bottom established (5-year low 5,644 Thu Jun 4); (3) Bank-sector rebound 5 sessions cumulative ~+20-25% across BBCA/BBNI/BBRI/BMRI — defensive-quality re-rate functioning. However: (a) Moody's Danantara Baa2 negative outlook persists; (b) Foreign flow inflection not multi-source confirmed Thu; (c) MSCI Jun 18 BINARY overhang T+4 trading days; (d) BI RDG Jun 17-18 binary 50% pricing +25bp; (e) Mean-reversion Thu (-0.28% IHSG) shows fragility; (f) Brent -4.25% sector negative for energy; (g) data-infra Day 53 outage still binds Gate 9 discipline. **Procedural call:** today's weekly review formally evaluates label change; pending evaluation = regime stays DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED with DE-ESCALATION-CONFIRMING tag; 5% cap binding remains.
- **Position sizing adjustment:** 5% cap binding; Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED; **regime change to DEFENSIVE-CONFIRMED (potential weekly review outcome) would lift 5% → 10% cap; full RISK-NEUTRAL would lift to 15% but requires 4-of-4 confirmation.**
