> "if 3 consecutive weeks of 0/3 slot usage AND cumulative trial alpha >+15%, the next pre-market routine MUST evaluate at least one candidate at relaxed multi-source threshold (≤4% under outage) AND document why no entry cleared the relaxed gate."

**Relaxed ≤4% multi-source threshold applied to TLKM (Week 9 Priority 1) and KLBF (held — for state-machine transition check):**

- **TLKM:** Only multi-source point on file is stale Investing.com Jun 7 carry 2,760 IDR. No Jun 15 sesi I source surfaces in WebSearch as of 09:15 WIB. Even relaxed ≤4% threshold cannot be applied — there is only ONE source, not 2+ for comparison. **Binding constraint: source count, not spread.** Cannot fire entry on a single 8-day-stale source under any threshold.
- **KLBF:** TradingView 745 vs Investing 930 = 2-source spread ~25% on Jun 15 timeframe. FAILS relaxed ≤4% threshold by 6x. **Binding constraint: multi-source spread.** Safe-lower carry 1,035 holds per MISTAKES.md 2026-05-01 discipline; trailing 931 GTC remains armed broker-side; state-3 (+15% ≥1,087) and state-4 (+20% ≥1,134) transition triggers ARMED but blocked Day 55 consecutive.

**Conclusion:** Process change #3 satisfied — two candidates evaluated at relaxed threshold, binding constraint documented per candidate. Data-infrastructure outage Day 55 is the structural binding constraint, not over-defensive gate stacking. Patience holds.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — DOWNGRADE-PENDING (3 of 4 de-escalation criteria CONFIRMED or STRONGLY CONFIRMING; criterion (b) Moody's stabilization the lone gating constraint)**

- **Reasoning:** 3 distinct structural shifts confirmed by Mon Jun 15 data: (a) IDR sub-18,000 5-of-5 sustained = primary kill-switch FULL-LOCKED; (b) IHSG +4.12% close 6,254.97 = +13.7% buffer above 5,500 psych floor, restored above 6,000 with multi-source convergence; (c) Foreign flow 2-of-2 net-buy days (Fri Rp 287B + Mon Rp 257.8B) breaks 13-day outflow streak — 3rd consecutive day next would be CONFIRM. However: criterion (b) Moody's Danantara Baa2 negative outlook PERSISTS as of latest available data — the lone gating constraint. **Procedural call:** Fri Jun 19 weekly review formally evaluates label change; pending evaluation = regime stays DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED with DOWNGRADE-PENDING tag; 5% cap binding remains. **NEW Mon Jun 15 data shifts the call from "DOWNGRADE-PENDING" to "DOWNGRADE-IMMINENT" — but binary events Wed-Thu (BI RDG + MSCI) may swing either direction first.**
- **Position sizing adjustment:** 5% cap binding (DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED); Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED; regime change to DEFENSIVE-CONFIRMED (Fri Jun 19 weekly review possible outcome) would lift 5% → 10% cap.

- **Data infrastructure Day 56 outage persists:** yfinance + GoAPI HTTP 403 blocked; broker.sh quote returns stale stub; multi-source cluster (≥3-source ≤2% threshold) blocks Gate 9 verification for most candidates. Wed Jun 17 open the first opportunity to re-test.
- **KLBF cluster Day 56 non-convergence:** Investing 710 / broker stub 945 / TradingView ~745 spread ~33% FAILS ≤2%. State-3/state-4 transitions blocked 15th consecutive session (Day 41 onward). Weekly review Fri Jun 19 must address.
- **Mon US-Iran peace deal energy negative:** Brent -4% Mon = MEDC + AKRA + indirect coal compounding negative. Sector exit on coal binds; energy/oil names skip.
- **Mean-reversion fragility:** 4-of-5 consecutive Mon-Fri Week 8 + Mon Wk9 IHSG rally days = +13.27% cumulative recovery from Mon Jun 8 ARB. Some give-back probable Wed pre-BI; pre-binary positioning sustains caution.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — DOWNGRADE-PENDING (3 of 4 de-escalation criteria CONFIRMED or STRONGLY CONFIRMING; criterion (b) Moody's stabilization the lone gating constraint)**

- **Reasoning:** 3 distinct structural shifts confirmed by Mon Jun 15 data carry through Tue holiday: (a) IDR sub-18,000 5-of-5 sustained = primary kill-switch FULL-LOCKED; (c) IHSG +4.12% close 6,254.97 = +13.7% buffer above 5,500 psych floor, restored above 6,000 multi-source; (d) Foreign flow 2-of-2 net-buy days (Fri Rp 287B + Mon Rp 257.8B) breaks 13-day outflow streak — Wed Jun 17 3rd consecutive day would lock FULLY CONFIRMED. However: (b) Moody's Danantara Baa2 negative outlook PERSISTS = lone gating constraint. **Procedural call:** Fri Jun 19 weekly review formally evaluates label change; pending evaluation = regime stays DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED-DOWNGRADE-PENDING; 5% cap binding remains.
- **Position sizing adjustment:** 5% cap binding; Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED; regime change to DEFENSIVE-CONFIRMED (Fri Jun 19 possible outcome) would lift 5% → 10% cap.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. BMRI — Foreign top-3 net buy Rp 106.9B Thu DESPITE BI hike = selective bank accumulation signal

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Bank Mandiri is Indonesia's largest SOE bank by assets; ROE leader in mega-cap banking; resilient NIM profile from low-cost CASA deposits and corporate book.
- Thesis: BI +25bp hike Thu Jun 18 confirmed = NIM compression headwind for sector. But Thu foreign net buy Rp 106.9B (3rd-largest single-name foreign inflow alongside EMAS Rp 789B + AMMN Rp 151B) = institutional accumulation despite the hawkish event = selective mega-cap bank rotation signal.
- Valuation: P/E ~12 (carry), P/B ~2.0 (carry) — moderate premium to sector but ROE-justified.
- Catalyst: BI hike absorbed; foreign signal fresh today; cum-div Tue Jun 23 T-2 (dividend yield ~5-6%); MSCI accessibility outcome announced 03:30 WIB Fri pre-open = binary tailwind if FIF freeze lifted.
- Analyst view: Multi-broker overweight; long-running banking-sector lead recommendation among SOE banks.
- Insider activity: No reported insider activity Thu.

**Technical context:**
- Wed Jun 17 close 4,430 −1.56% sesi II profit-taking; Thu Jun 18 price NOT multi-source confirmed at filing.
- Support: 4,200 (Mon Jun 8 ARB rebound base); Resistance: 4,600 then 4,800.
- Trend: Sideways consolidation post-Jun 8 panic-flush recovery; foreign signal Thu shifts micro-trend hint to accumulation.
- Cluster Day 59 caveat: data-infra block persists; Gate 9 multi-source ≤2% convergence required pre-entry.

- Position size: 5% of equity (DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED cap binding) = IDR ~492.8M
- Conviction: MEDIUM (foreign-flow signal fresh but bank NIM compression overhang; regime cap binds)

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — DOWNGRADE-PENDING** (carry from Week 8 close; **FORMAL RE-EVAL TODAY 16:00 WIB weekly review post-BI/MSCI binary**)

**De-escalation criteria status (Thu Jun 18 close):**
- (a) IDR sub-18,000 7-of-7 sustained (17,826 Thu close) = **PERMANENTLY LOCKED + BI +25bp reinforcement** (criterion confirmed with reinforcement)
- (b) Moody's Danantara Baa2 NEGATIVE outlook **PERSISTS** (Jun 3 rating unchanged at filing; lone gating constraint blocking DEFENSIVE-CONFIRMED)
- (c) IHSG >5,500 reclaim sustained = **STRONGLY CONFIRMED** (Thu 6,172.34 = +12.2% buffer)
- (d) Foreign-flow inflection **REGRESSED PERSISTS** — Thu net sell Rp 111.56B continues Wed sesi II reversal; 2 consecutive net-sell days; criterion (d) NOT confirming

**Net: 2 of 4 confirming (a + c), (a) reinforced by BI hike; criterion (d) remains broken; regime label DOWNGRADE-PENDING holds pending weekly-review formal re-eval.**

- **Reasoning:** Thu Jun 18 binary day delivered: BI +25bp HIKE confirmed (consensus hit; reinforces criterion a); MSCI Global Mkt Accessibility Review announcement 03:30 WIB Fri (pending Fri pre-open multi-source confirmation). IDR criterion (a) strengthened; criterion (d) remains broken. Weekly review formal re-eval today 16:00 WIB will determine: (i) hold DOWNGRADE-PENDING, (ii) downgrade to DEFENSIVE-CONFIRMED on MSCI accessibility-pass, OR (iii) re-escalate to INTENSIFIED on MSCI frontier-downgrade.
- **Position sizing adjustment:** 5% cap BINDING pending weekly-review re-eval. Defensive-Quality Track SUSPENDED. New entries POSSIBLE per gate; MSCI outcome + multi-source convergence both binding.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. BMRI — Foreign top-3 net buy Rp 106.9B Thu DESPITE BI hike + Fri pullback to 4,310 + cum-div Tue T-1

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: Bank Mandiri is Indonesia's largest SOE bank by assets; ROE leader in mega-cap banking; resilient NIM profile from low-cost CASA deposits and corporate book.
- Thesis: BI +25bp HIKE Thu Jun 18 = sector NIM compression headwind. But Thu foreign top-3 net buy Rp 106.9B + Fri pullback −3.58% to 4,310 = chase risk relaxes 3.6pp + cum-div Tue Jun 23 T-1 = near-term defensive bid catalyst stacking.
- Valuation: P/E ~12 (carry), P/B ~2.0 (carry) — moderate premium to sector but ROE-justified.
- Catalyst: Cum-div Tue Jun 23 T-1 (yield ~5-6%); MSCI Annual Classification Review Tue Jun 23 binary tail risk (Indonesia retained EM Jun 18 = base case held); foreign signal fresh Thu.
- Analyst view: Multi-broker overweight; long-running banking-sector lead recommendation among SOE banks.
- Insider activity: No reported insider activity Thu/Fri.

**Technical context:**
- Fri Jun 19 close 4,310 −3.58% sesi II = post-BI absorption + pre-cum-div positioning.
- Support: 4,200 (Mon Jun 8 ARB rebound base); Resistance: 4,500 then 4,800.
- Trend: Pullback from Thu Jun 18 ~4,470 carry; foreign signal Thu shifts micro-trend hint to accumulation amid pullback.
- Cluster Day 60 caveat: data-infra block persists; Gate 9 multi-source ≤2% convergence required pre-entry.

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR ≤4,400 Mon Jun 22 09:15 (must clear multi-source ≤2% Gate 9; chase cap = +3% from Fri close 4,310 → ≤4,440)
- Stop: 4,092 (entry × 0.93 hard-cut at 4,400 entry)
- Target: 5,200 (+18% — moderate post-BI rerate scenario) or 5,500 (+25%); R:R 2.59:1 to 3.50:1
- Position size: 5% of equity (DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED cap binding) = IDR ~492.8M = ~112,000 sh @ 4,400
- Conviction: MEDIUM (foreign-flow signal fresh + cum-div catalyst + pullback chase-relaxed; but BI HIKE NIM compression + MSCI Annual T-1 binary tail + Fri foreign net sell Rp 3.19T massive overhang)
- ADV (20-day): est. 25-40M sh/day (LQ45 mega-cap; high liquidity)
- **Pre-mortem:** (a) MSCI Annual frontier-EM downgrade outcome Wed pre-open = sell-off cascade; (b) Foreign net sell continuation Mon = signal Thu was outlier; (c) NIM print Q2 confirms compression severity; (d) Cum-div Tue Jun 23 = profit-taking risk before T-0; (e) Fri net SELL Rp 3.19T is a structural deepening pre-MSCI Annual headwind.
- **Intermediate pain:** −4% (≤4,224) = reassess MSCI Annual direction + foreign flow + bank-sector momentum; close if 2 of 3 negative. −7% = full close at 4,092.
- **DECISION: WATCH Mon pre-open; CONDITIONAL ENTRY at /market-open IF (i) MSCI Annual sentiment Mon pre-open is neutral/positive (no frontier shocker forecast), (ii) multi-source ≤2% convergence Gate 9 on ≤4,400 anchor, (iii) Fri Rp 3.19T net sell reverses Mon (foreign flow inflection signal), (iv) ≤+3% chase from 4,310. Foreign Fri sell Rp 3.19T is the highest bar to clear.**

**De-escalation criteria status (Fri Jun 19 close + Mon pre-open carry):**
- (a) IDR sub-18,000 8-of-8 sustained = **PERMANENTLY LOCKED + BI +25bp reinforcement** (criterion confirmed; intraday Fri touched 18,002 briefly but did NOT close above 18,000 = sustained on close basis)

- (e) MSCI accessibility NEGATIVE info-flow downgrade Jun 18 = NEW headwind active

**Net: 2 of 5 confirming (a + c); (a) reinforced; (d) deeply regressed Fri Rp 3.19T; (e) new negative.** Regime label holds DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED-BINARY-RESOLVED.

Tue Jun 23 MSCI Annual Classification Review = remaining binary tail risk; BBRI/BMRI cum-div Tue T-0; MDKA RUPSLB Tue 14:00 WIB (private placement + dividend).

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. TLKM — Telco-defensive post ex-div mechanical drop + Rp 4T buyback combo

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis sentence 1: Telkom Indonesia is Indonesia's incumbent telecom monopoly with diversified revenue (mobile, fixed, towers, data centres); generates structurally high free cash flow with 6.07% dividend yield carry — a defensive cash-flow compounder.
- Thesis sentence 2: TLKM ex-div Fri Jun 19 IDR 221/sh mechanical drop creates entry candidate near 2,960 IDR (below 2,990 3% chase cap from ~3,100 pre-ex reference); Rp 4T share buyback window through 2027 reinforced Jun 4 = paired return-of-capital catalyst.
- Valuation: P/E ~12-13x carry vs sector ~10-15x; P/B ~2x; dividend yield 6.07% — above 5% defensive bar.
- Catalyst: Mechanical ex-div drop absorbed Mon Jun 22; 4-day post-ex absorption window through Fri; buyback floor support.
- Analyst view: 19 analysts Strong Buy consensus carry (target ~3,300-3,500 range).
- Insider activity: Rp 4T buyback program effectively management endorsement.

**Technical context:**
- Price: ~2,960 (Mon Jun 22 ref post ex-div) | 50-day MA: ~3,050 carry | 200-day MA: ~2,900 carry
- Volume: not multi-source confirmed Day 61; likely above 20-day avg on ex-div absorption
- Support: ~2,900 (200-day MA + psychological); Resistance: ~3,100 (pre-ex level)
- Trend: post-ex consolidation; defensive setup

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 2,960 (Mon ref); Stop: 2,753 (−7% hard cut) | Target: 3,260 (+10%) — R:R ≈ 1.43:1 ← FAILS minimum 2:1 R:R
- Position size: max 5% of equity = IDR 492.8M / 2,960 = ~166,500 sh (round to 166,500)
- Conviction: MEDIUM (defensive-quality + same-day catalyst clears re-eligibility but R:R below 2:1)
- ADV (20-day avg daily volume): ~30,000,000 sh estimate carry (data-infra Day 61 unverifiable)
- **Pre-mortem (Strategy Entry Checklist #5):** If MSCI Annual Wed Jun 24 03:30 WIB pre-open release shows ANY frontier-downgrade tail signal (yellow card escalation, accessibility re-rating), TLKM as LQ45 telco gets caught in $13B passive outflow basket — thesis breaks before −7% hard-cut fires. Monitor MSCI release pre-open Wed.
- **Intermediate pain (Entry Checklist #6):** If TLKM hits −4% intraday (mark ≤2,842), reassess immediately — would indicate MSCI absorption deeper than ex-div mechanical; cover if MSCI outcome adverse.
- **Gate 9 (Data Quality Gate):** REQUIRES multi-source ≤2% (canonical) or ≤4% (relaxed-under-outage) convergence pre-entry; Day 61 outage state binding; deferred to market-open verification.
- **R:R FAIL:** 1.43:1 below 2:1 minimum — entry blocked unless target widens to ≥3,374 (+14% from 2,960) which requires upside catalyst beyond ex-div mechanical.

**Decision:** WATCH at market-open. Re-eval if (a) Gate 9 multi-source ≤2% achievable at fresh open mark, (b) R:R widens to ≥2:1 (target ≥3,374), (c) pre-MSCI tone absent adverse leak. Most likely DEFER per R:R fail + pre-MSCI T-0 binary tail risk + Gate 9 binding.

#### 2. ASII — Defensive-Quality Track candidate (LQ45 mega-cap + IDR 8T buyback signal)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis sentence 1: Astra International is Indonesia's largest conglomerate (4 main segments: automotive 56% market share, financial services, heavy equipment/mining, infrastructure/agribusiness); LQ45 + market cap >100T IDR eligible for Defensive-Quality Track per TRADING-STRATEGY.md.
- Thesis sentence 2: IDR 8T share buyback announced Jun 2026 = strongest management capital-return signal of trial; Q1 2026 net −16% YoY on heavy equipment 79% earnings drop is contained risk (cyclical bottom signal) while automotive +4pp market share gain (52% → 56% Apr → May) suggests core franchise strengthening; defensive-quality long-term P/B discount.
- Valuation: P/E ~6-7x carry vs sector ~10-12x; P/B ~0.9-1.0x — at or below 10-year mean per available carry data; **defensive-quality Track requires citing numeric mean + σ**: 10-year P/B mean ~1.5x, σ ~0.3, current ~0.9-1.0x = ~1.5-1.7σ below own-history mean (citation: carry estimate; needs verification via fundamentals.sh which is Day 61 blocked).
- Catalyst: IDR 8T buyback Jun 2026 (same-day catalyst exception clears re-eligibility); 56% auto market share May.
- Analyst view: mixed; heavy equipment drag the binding negative.
- Insider activity: IDR 8T buyback = effective management endorsement.

**Technical context:**
- Price: ~4,570 (Jun 6 carry; Mon Jun 22 likely lower post-MSCI derisk pattern); 50-day MA: ~4,650 carry | 200-day MA: ~4,800 carry
- Volume: not multi-source verified Day 61
- Support: ~4,400 (psychological + recent low); Resistance: ~4,800 (200-day MA)
- Trend: range-bound below 200-day MA; below 50-day MA = downtrend short-term

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 4,570 (Jun 6 ref); Stop: 4,250 (−7% hard cut) | Target: 5,030 (+10%) | R:R ≈ 1.44:1 ← FAILS minimum 2:1
- Position size: Defensive-Quality Track max 10% of equity = IDR 985.6M / 4,570 = ~215,700 sh (round to 215,700) BUT regime DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED-BINARY-RESOLVED caps at 5% per RISK-STATE.json; defensive-quality Track cap supersedes only if regime ≠ DEFENSIVE → BLOCKED, 5% cap binds = IDR 492.8M / 4,570 = ~107,800 sh

- **Intermediate pain (Entry Checklist #6):** If ASII hits −4% (mark ≤4,387), reassess immediately — could indicate MSCI absorption + Q2 leak.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — BINARY-RESOLVED (carry from 2026-06-19 Week 9 close; pre-MSCI Annual T-0 binary tail-risk window today)**

- Reasoning sentence 1: 2 of 5 de-escalation criteria CONFIRMED ((a) IDR sub-18,000 9-of-9 sustained FULL-LOCKED + BI +25bp Jun 18 reinforcement; (c) IHSG >5,500 reclaim sustained 10+ sessions); criterion (b) Moody's stabilization PENDING-REGRESSED (BI HIKE re-anchoring opposite signal); criterion (d) foreign-flow inflection REGRESSED to PARTIAL-NEGATIVE; criterion (e) MSCI accessibility status NEGATIVE flag re-introduced Jun 18.
- Reasoning sentence 2: Tue Jun 23 = pre-MSCI Annual T-0 (release Wed Jun 24 03:30 WIB pre-open) — binary tail-risk window binds wait-and-see across the broad universe; Indonesia confirmed EM per Jun 18 release with "yellow card" info-flow downgrade absorbed; frontier-downgrade probability slim but non-zero ($13B Goldman outflow tail risk).
- Reasoning sentence 3: What would change it — Wed Jun 24 MSCI release outcome (frontier-downgrade = re-escalation to DEFENSIVE-CASCADE-PEAK; no change = DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED-BINARY-RESOLVED holds; positive surprise = de-escalation candidate).

**Position sizing adjustment:** max 5% of equity binding (DOWNGRADE-PENDING qualifier dropped Wk 9; cap restoration deferred per regime).
**Defensive-Quality Track:** SUSPENDED per "Regime is not DEFENSIVE" clause = BLOCKED for ASII.
**Data Quality Gate:** binding — multi-source ≤2% canonical or ≤4% relaxed-under-outage with explicit RESEARCH-LOG documentation (Day 61 outage state qualifies for relaxed gate IF ≥3 fresh same-day sources available).

#### 1. BBCA — MSCI relief rebound post Tue −2.0% pre-binary derisk

- Thesis: BBCA is Indonesia's most defensive-quality mega-cap bank (IDR ~700T market cap), with sector-best CASA franchise (~80% low-cost deposits) and lowest NPL (~2%). Tue Jun 23 −2.00% sell-off to 6,100 was pre-MSCI defensive derisk, not idiosyncratic — relief outcome (Indonesia REMAINS EM) confirmed 03:30 WIB Wed = bid restoration candidate.
- Thesis: BI 5.75% restrictive but NIM-supportive for BBCA's franchise (deposit re-pricing lag favors low-cost-deposit names); FY26 ROE forecast ~21-22% per Sectors.app; structurally insulated from MSCI Information Flow yellow-card vs MSCI EM-status maintained.

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — BINARY-RESOLVED-FAVORABLY (de-escalation criterion (e) MSCI accessibility/classification status NEUTRAL-LOCKED post Wed Jun 24 03:30 WIB Annual Classification Review confirming Indonesia EM-status; net 5-criterion picture moves from 2 of 5 confirmed → 2.5 of 5 with (e) downgrade overhang REMOVED)**
- Reasoning: MSCI Annual Classification Review Wed 03:30 WIB pre-open CONFIRMED Indonesia REMAINS Emerging Market. This removes the binary tail risk of frontier downgrade ($13B Goldman outflow scenario) that had compressed banks/large-caps through Mon-Tue defensive derisk. The "yellow card" Information Flow NEGATIVE downgrade from Jun 18 remains a structural overhang but it was already absorbed Fri Wk 9. Net effect Wed: relief-rally bias for oversold banks/large-caps; Asian semis weakness from US Mon Nasdaq −2.21% acts as offset for tech names. Sub-18,000 IDR criterion (a) PERMANENTLY-LOCKED with BI +25bp Jun 18 reinforcement (Tue spot 17,929 modest backslide but 9-of-9 sustained); IHSG >5,500 criterion (c) STRONGLY-CONFIRMED (Tue close 6,101 = +9.4% buffer); Moody's stabilization criterion (b) REGRESSED to HAWKISH-REANCHORING signal; foreign-flow criterion (d) PARTIAL-NEGATIVE pending Wed flow direction. Cap restoration to 10% deferred pending criterion (b) OR (d) re-confirmation.
- Position sizing adjustment: 5% cap binding (regime not yet downgraded; MSCI relief removes (e) overhang but criteria (b)+(d) still binding); max position size 5% × 9,856M = IDR 493M.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. BBCA — Pullback into R:R-clear zone post Wed MSCI cascade −2.87%

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: BBCA is Indonesia's most defensive-quality mega-cap bank (IDR ~700T market cap), with sector-best CASA franchise (~80% low-cost deposits) and lowest NPL (~2%). Wed Jun 24 −2.87% pullback to 5,925 from Tue 6,100 = MSCI Frontier-risk persistence cascade overshooting fundamentals — relief bid INVERTED to risk-off discount = mean-reversion candidate.
- Thesis: BI 5.75% restrictive but NIM-supportive for BBCA's franchise (deposit re-pricing lag favors low-cost-deposit names); structurally insulated from MSCI Information Flow yellow-card given Indonesia EM-classification maintained (only Frontier downgrade by Nov 2026 review remains a binary tail risk if reforms don't progress).
- Valuation: P/E 12.7x (TTM) vs banking sector 13.3x = 4.5% discount; 5-yr historical mean P/E ~17x = own-history undervalued.
- Catalyst: Post-MSCI absorption Day 1 technical rebound (MNC Sekuritas IHSG resistance 5,930 / support 5,750-5,847; BRI Danareksa flags rebound possibility); bank-cluster oversold bounce candidate; chase risk now favourable (Wed close 5,925 within $25 IDR of R:R-clear 5,950 entry).
- Analyst view: PT avg 8,654 IDR (+46.1% upside from 5,925); high 10,900 / low 5,500; 23 analysts Strong Buy / 0 Sell — url: https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/PT-BANK-CENTRAL-ASIA-TBK-6493397/consensus/
- Insider activity: not surfaced fresh; foreign net sell Rp 976.66B IDX-wide Wed = bank-cluster pressure binding

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR 5,925 Wed close | 50d MA estimate ~6,200 (carry, below) | 200d MA estimate ~8,000 (well below = downtrend) — url: https://www.investing.com/equities/bnk-central-as

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — CASCADE-RESUMED (MSCI accessibility (e) flipped from NEUTRAL-LOCKED back to NEGATIVE-LOCKED on Wed Frontier-risk persistence headline; net 5-criterion 2.0 of 5 confirming — regression from Wed's 2.5 of 5 BINARY-RESOLVED-FAVORABLY)**
- Reasoning: MSCI Annual Classification Review Wed maintained Indonesia EM but flagged Information Flow indicator downgrade from "+" to "-" + explicit consultation possibility on Frontier-downgrade by Nov 2026 review if accessibility reforms don't progress = MSCI accessibility status NEGATIVE-LOCKED (not removed as Wed pre-market had assumed). IHSG −3.56% close 5,883.88 = 6,000 psychological broken; criterion (c) IHSG >5,500 still confirmed (+6.97% buffer) but materially weakened. Foreign flow Wed Rp 976.66B net sell = +3.1x acceleration vs Tue = JCI worst-performer Asia-Pacific EM. Sub-18,000 IDR criterion (a) 10-of-10 sustained LOCKED at risk (Thu forecast 17,912; max 18,181 within forecast range = ARMED-WATCH if Thu pierces). Moody's stabilization (b) PENDING-REGRESSED on hawkish-reanchoring re-anchoring. Net effect: regime label flips to DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED-CASCADE-RESUMED from BINARY-RESOLVED-FAVORABLY.
- Position sizing adjustment: 5% cap binding (no cap restoration; criterion (e) re-introduced NEGATIVE-LOCKED = cap restoration deferred further); max position size 5% × 9,820M = IDR 491M.

- **MSCI Frontier-downgrade Nov 2026 binary tail risk:** if accessibility reforms don't materialize by Nov 2026 review, MSCI may consult on downgrade to Frontier Market = ~$3-13B passive outflow scenario; risk premium now structurally elevated through Nov.
- **IDR backslide risk:** Thu forecast 17,912; forecast max 18,181 within 30rates range = re-arm cascade kill-switch if Thu spot ≥17,950 sustained 2 sessions.
- **R:R 2:1 gate persistent binding constraint:** BBCA passes only at ≤5,890 entry (Wed close 5,925 needs +0.6% pullback); TLKM passes at ≤2,830 (cluster non-convergent at filing); BMRI cluster non-convergent. Likely 0-trade day unless Thu open converges into R:R-clear zone.
- **Data Quality Gate Day 64 persistent:** yfinance + GoAPI both blocked 23 consecutive sessions; multi-source WebSearch the binding cluster source.
- **Brent collapse −5.23% to $73.05:** lowest since Feb 2026; revenue compression severe for MEDC/MEDC-adjacent + sustains Trump-Iran-deal momentum as macro deflationary force for EM commodity exporters.
- **Nickel 2-mo low $16,885:** ANTM/INCO/MDKA/NCKL/MBMA sector pressure binding; MNC/BRI technical picks on rotation = no fundamental edge.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. UNVR — MNC Sekuritas Fri TOP PICK + Brent collapse CPI-relief tailwind + B50 CPO supportive

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: UNVR is Indonesia's incumbent defensive-consumer giant (FMCG: personal care, foods, household products); structurally pressured by IDR/cost-of-goods compression through 2025-2026 cycle but trading at depressed multiples. Post-ex Rp Mon Jun 22 mechanical drop completed Tue Jun 23; thesis re-engages with Brent collapse near $70 = CPI-relief tailwind for defensive consumer demand recovery + B50 CPO effective Jul 1 = supportive plantation input dynamic.
- Thesis: MNC Sekuritas Fri Jun 26 TOP PICK Spec Buy zone 1,685-1,675 with target 1,885-2,000 (+11-19%) and CL 1,670 = analyst-defined R:R-clean setup; aligns with watchlist defensive bias under DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED regime.
- Valuation: PT avg 1,885-2,000 (MNC Fri); historical 5-yr range typically depressed but recent compression makes upside asymmetric.
- Catalyst: (a) MNC Fri top pick = sell-side bid catalyst T+0; (b) Brent collapse near $70 = CPI-relief tailwind for defensive consumer staples; (c) B50 program effective Jul 1 = T+3 structural CPO input dynamic; (d) IHSG sideways 5,850-6,100 supportive
- Analyst view: MNC Spec Buy target 1,885-2,000 CL 1,670; consensus aggregated softer (UNVR has been a chronic underperformer 2024-2026)
- Insider activity: not surfaced fresh

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR ~1,685 (Thu estimate; MNC Fri buy zone reference) | 50d MA estimate ~1,700 (near) | 200d MA estimate ~1,800 (below)
- Volume: not surfaced
- Support: 1,670 (MNC CL); below: 1,650 prior swing low
- Resistance: 1,885 (MNC TP1); above: 2,000 (MNC TP2)
- Trend: depressed range; oversold bounce on MNC catalyst + CPI-relief macro

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: ≤ IDR 1,690 (≤+0.30% chase from MNC zone midpoint 1,680; well within Gate 9 ≤+3% cap)
- Stop: IDR 1,572 (entry × 0.93 = hard-cut −7%)
- Target: IDR 1,885 (MNC TP1; +11.5% upside vs 7% stop distance = R:R 1.65:1 at 1,690 entry → **FAILS 2:1**); target IDR 2,000 (MNC TP2; +18.3% upside = R:R 2.62:1 PASSES at 1,690 entry)

#### 2. BBCA — Bank franchise sector-best CASA + Thu sesi I foreign bid attempt + BEI passive headwind T+3 binds

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: BBCA is Indonesia's most defensive-quality mega-cap bank (IDR ~700T market cap), sector-best CASA franchise (~80% low-cost deposits), lowest NPL (~2%). Thu Jun 25 sesi I foreign top net buy bid (BBCA/DSSA per Katadata Rp 187B sesi I net buy) attempted relief reversal, faded to full-day net sell Rp 201B — partial flow inflection NOT confirmed. Post-MSCI absorption Day 1 relief-bounce mechanical; Day 2 fade.
- Thesis: BI 5.75% restrictive but NIM-supportive for BBCA's CASA franchise; structurally insulated from MSCI Information Flow downgrade given Indonesia EM-classification maintained (only Nov 2026 Frontier-downgrade remains a binary tail risk). BEI IHSG weight cut −53bp effective Jul 1 (T+3) = passive-headwind drag; net negative carry vs BMRI/BBNI gainers — but BBCA chronic outperformer absorbed many such re-weightings before.
- Valuation: P/E 12.7x trailing / 12.0x forward vs banking sector forward avg 7x = 71% premium to sector (quality compounder premium standard); 5-yr historical mean P/E ~16-17x = own-history undervalued.
- Catalyst: Post-MSCI absorption Day 2 mean-reversion candidate; Thu sesi I bid attempt = institutional accumulation interest at relief zone; chase risk favourable if Fri pullback to ≤5,890 entry zone.
- Analyst view: PT avg 8,909 IDR (+48% upside from carry ~6,000); high 11,445 / low 6,060 (low is now within striking distance); consensus rating Strong Buy — url: https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/PT-BANK-CENTRAL-ASIA-TBK-6493397/consensus/
- Insider activity: not surfaced fresh; Thu sesi I foreign net buy attempt = institutional accumulation interest signal

**Technical context:**
- Price: IDR ~6,000 (carry from Thu sesi I rebound; cluster non-convergent at filing) | 50d MA estimate ~6,200 (near; below) | 200d MA estimate ~7,800-8,000 (well below = downtrend; 200d MA ~50% upside)
- Volume: Thu trading frequency 1.7M times / volume 22.57B / value Rp 13.64T (Liputan6) = above-avg

- Resistance: 6,100 (Tue close); above: 6,200 Mon Jun 22 close; 6,300+ Wk 9 cluster
- Trend: ranging in 5,850-6,200 post-MSCI absorption zone; oversold bounce candidate if foreign flow inflects Fri

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: ≤ IDR 5,890 (≤−1.83% pullback from carry 6,000; PASSES Gate 9 ≤+3% cap as entry is BELOW carry, not chasing)

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — CASCADE-ABSORBED-DAY2 (post-MSCI absorption Day 2; relief-bounce Day 1 sustained Thu; foreign flow inflection criterion (d) FAILS Thu — sesi I bid faded to full-day net sell)**

Thu sesi I foreign net buy Rp 187B (BBCA/DSSA bid) FADED to full-day net sell Rp 201B regular / Rp 299B total — flow inflection (d) UNCONFIRMED; BMRI top net sell Rp 224B = BEI weight +24bp tailwind ARBITRAGED pre-effective. MSCI accessibility (e) NEGATIVE-LOCKED persists. BI 5.75% restrictive carry. IDR sub-18,000 13-of-13 sustained PERMANENTLY-LOCKED (Thu 17,943; cascade kill-switch 17,950 within 6bps but NOT pierced). Brent collapse near $70 sustained = CPI-relief tailwind for defensive consumer staples + EM commodity importers (Indonesia neutral as net commodity exporter offset by import-side relief). Net 5-criterion 1.5 of 5 confirming = regime label MAINTAINED at CASCADE-ABSORBED-DAY2 from Thu's DAY1.
- Position sizing adjustment: 5% cap binding (no cap restoration; criterion (e) NEGATIVE-LOCKED + criterion (d) flow inflection failed Thu); max position size 5% × 9,820M = IDR 491M.

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive)

#### 1. BBCA — 3rd-session inflection CONFIRMED but GATE-BLOCKED by IDR escalation

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: BBCA absorbed Mon-Tue Wk 11 cascade; Wed Rp 155.22B foreign net BUY inflection + Thu absent + Fri absent from top net sell = 3-session INFLECTION FULLY CONFIRMED. Fri +4.31% intraday to 6,050 with bank rally leading (BBCA/ISAT top movers per multi-source).
- Thesis: BI 5.75% NIM-positive carry; BBCA is defensive mega-cap on any post-cascade recovery. IHSG 3-day rebound sustained (Wed +0.84% / Thu +0.87% / Fri +2.46%).
- Valuation: P/E ~13x trailing at 6,050 vs banking sector 7x = premium-quality; 5-yr historical mean ~16-17x = own-history undervalued after cascade.
- Catalyst: (a) 3-session foreign flow INFLECTION FULLY CONFIRMED; (b) IHSG Day 3 rebound momentum; (c) Bank-cluster paired inflection with BMRI.
- Analyst view: avg PT 8,428 (23 analysts BUY, 0 SELL); Simply Wall St PT 9,101.
- Insider: not fresh.

**Technical context:**
- Price: Fri close ~6,050 | 50d MA est ~6,000 (right at) | 200d MA est ~7,800
- Volume: Fri elevated on Day 3 rebound
- Support: 5,850 (Thu day high); 5,700 psych
- Resistance: 6,100; 6,300 (recent swing high pre-cascade)
- Trend: 3-day rebound Wed-Fri = V-bottom confirmation

- Entry: ≤ IDR 6,050 (no chase >3% above Fri close = ceiling 6,231)
- Stop: IDR 5,626 (entry × 0.93 = hard-cut −7%)
- Target: IDR 6,900 (R:R 2.00:1 at 6,050/6,900 = +14.0% upside vs 7% stop = **PASSES 2:1 at minimum**; at 5,990 fill R:R 2.15:1)
- Position size: 5% cap × IDR 9,820M = IDR 491M; at 6,050 = ~81,100 sh round to 81,000 sh

- ADV (20-day avg): ~50,000,000 sh; ADV gate not binding

#### 2. BMRI — Paired-conditional 3rd-session confirmation but GATE-BLOCKED

### Top Candidates (score ≥ 6, deep-dive required)

#### 1. BBCA — Bank-cluster reversal setup post-Wk 11 Fri sesi I banking rally; paired-conditional first-mover

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: BBCA is Indonesia's dominant retail-savings-franchise bank with cyclically depressed valuation post 6-week cascade → 4-week absorption round-trip. NIM headwind from BI HIKE +25bp Jun 18 is broadly priced in.
- Thesis: 3-session foreign-flow-net-sell ABSENCE Wk 11 Wed-Thu-Fri (BBRI absorbed the sector's foreign selling as top net-sell 3rd consecutive session Fri Rp 176.24B) = paired inflection CONFIRMED — BBCA has stopped being sold by foreign holders, an inflection signal that historically precedes multi-week bounces.
- Valuation: cluster non-convergent (Google Finance Tue morning 6,100 vs Investing/Yahoo Jul 2 stub 5,800 = spread >5%); analyst 12-mo target average IDR 8,428 (range 5,500–10,900) implies material upside vs low-cluster mid-5,800s.
- Catalyst: post-Wk 11 bank-rally continuation into Wk 12 + IDR partial DE-ARM (Mon Jul 6 close 17,945 = 1st sub-17,950 in 6 sessions).
- Analyst view: "Undervalued" narrative Suara.com Jul 1; Mirae Asset gradual accumulation Jun 3 in prior cascade.
- Insider activity: no recent director/commissioner transactions surfaced Wk 12.

**Technical context:**
- Price: cluster non-convergent — best reads: Google Finance IDX 6,100 (+4.31%) Tue morning vs Investing Jul 2 5,800. 52-week range 4,820–8,975.
- Volume: not multi-source convergent for today; Fri Jul 3 sesi I banking-rally elevated.
- Support: recent swing low Wk 11 Wed cascade intraday 5,550 (falling-knife bottom). Resistance: 6,100 short-term / 6,300–6,500 mid-term.
- Trend: post-cascade recovery leg; Wk 11 Wed-Fri: 5,550 → 5,875 sesi I Fri lead = reversal setup active.

**Trade plan:**
- Entry: IDR 5,900–5,950 (only if cluster narrows ≤4% relaxed under outage — canonical ≤2% not achievable in Data Quality Gate Day 72) | Hard-cut: entry × 0.93 = ~5,487–5,533 | Target: 6,500–6,750 (+9-14% upside)
- R:R ratio: hard-cut delta ~7% vs upside ~10-13% = R:R 1.4:1 to 1.9:1 = **FAILS 2:1 R:R gate at planned entry** unless cluster confirms below 5,900 with ≤4% spread AND upside reaches ≥6,700
- Position size: 5% of equity (regime cap DEFENSIVE-INTENSIFIED-CASCADE-INFLECTION-CONFIRMED-DAY2) = 5% × 9,716M = ~486M IDR; at 5,900 entry = 82,300 shares round to 82,300
- Conviction: **MEDIUM** (bank-cluster paired reversal with 3-session foreign inflection confirmation; never HIGH per DEFENSIVE regime + Data Quality Gate outage + R:R 2:1 near-binding)
- ADV: ~130,000,000; gate not binding
- **CONDITIONAL ENTRY at /market-open ONLY IF:** (i) IDR sustained sub-17,950 Tue → Mon-Tue 2-of-2 DE-ARM CONFIRMED (currently Tue morning 17,987 = PIERCING, DE-ARM NOT MET), (ii) BBCA cluster ≥3 fresh sources ≤4% relaxed, (iii) IHSG opens ≥5,800, (iv) R:R ≥2:1 achieved at fresh cluster mark
- **Pre-mortem:** BBCA breach 5,550 first 5 sessions = falling-knife re-activation → thesis broken; OR IDR pierce back above 17,950 sustained close 2+ sessions Tue-Wed → IDR-ESCALATION re-arms; OR foreign flow BBCA appears in top net-sell list ANY single session = paired inflection broken
- **Intermediate pain:** −4% (5,900 × 0.96 = 5,664) = REASSESS IMMEDIATELY; EXIT if IHSG cascade re-activates OR BBCA foreign net-sell re-appears OR IDR re-arms escalation
- **TOP PRIORITY for /market-open Tue Jul 7 IF all four conditions above cleared.** As of pre-market filing: IDR gate FAILS (Tue open 17,987 piercing), Data Quality Gate spread FAILS canonical + relaxed → highest-probability action is CONTINUED SKIP at /market-open unless intraday IDR sub-17,950 sustained close develops.

#### 2. BMRI — Bank-cluster reversal setup (paired-second, conditional on BBCA fill)

**Fundamental case:**
- Thesis: BMRI is SOE-bank complementing BBCA in retail/corporate lending. Post-BEI weight re-eval (BMRI +24bp passive inflow +BBNI +4bp inflow) is a mechanical tailwind delivered Wk 11 Wed.

- Valuation: cluster non-convergent Wk 12 open; last convergent search-based ~4,010 (+2.82%) early Jul; analyst overweight consensus.

- Analyst view: Overweight consensus; Verdhana / Mirae "solid" recommendation post-cascade Jun 3.
- Insider activity: none surfaced Wk 12.

- Volume: not multi-source convergent.
- Support: Wk 11 low ~3,900 area; Resistance: 4,200 short-term.

- Position size: 5% of equity (paired-second smaller sizing recommended: 3-4% preferred = ~292–389M IDR; at 5,150 entry = 56,700–75,500 sh round to nearest 100)

- ADV: ~60,000,000; gate not binding
- **CONDITIONAL ENTRY at /market-open ONLY IF:** (i) BBCA entry fills first (BMRI is paired-second, not standalone), (ii) BMRI cluster ≤5,200 ≥3 fresh sources ≤4% relaxed, (iii) IHSG opens ≥5,800, (iv) Bank-sector Tue first 30-min NOT extending BBRI 4th consecutive net sell

### Macro Regime Assessment

**Regime: DEFENSIVE — INTENSIFIED — CASCADE-INFLECTION-CONFIRMED-DAY2 with IDR-ESCALATION-ENGAGED overlay (PARTIAL DE-ARM 1-of-2)**

- Reasoning: Mon Jul 6 IDR close 17,945 = 1st sub-17,950 in 6 sessions = **1-of-2 DE-ARM watch** — encouraging first-step but not full DE-ARM. Tue Jul 7 morning IDR 17,987 = pierce back above 17,950 = **PARTIAL DE-ARM only, ESCALATION-ENGAGED overlay persists**. Global tape constructive (Dow record, VIX 15.57, S&P +0.72%) providing external tailwind. IHSG >5,800 buffer against 5,500 psych floor STRONGLY MET. Foreign flow inflection signals: 3-session BBCA foreign-flow-absence Wk 11 + Fri marginal +Rp 6.08B first inflow after multi-week outflow = tentative constructive signal. Coal + Nickel + CPO commodities all weak = sector rotation away from resources continues. What would change regime: (a) Tue Jul 7 IDR sustained close ≤17,950 → full DE-ARM criterion met → DEFENSIVE-CONFIRMED downgrade eligible; (b) opposite: Tue-Wed 2+ sustained closes ≥17,950 → escalation re-arms + pre-emptive de-risk procedure engaged.
- Position sizing adjustment: **5% cap binding** (unchanged from Wk 11); no cap restoration until (b) Moody's stabilization OR (d) foreign-flow inflection re-confirmed multi-week.

1. **IDR gate — TOP RISK:** Tue morning 17,987 (piercing 17,950) = if sustained close ≥17,950 Tue, that's the 2nd consecutive session breach post 1-day DE-ARM, and full IDR-ESCALATION re-arms with pre-emptive de-risk procedure engaged. Blocks all bank-cluster entries even if all other gates clear.

4. **Coal / nickel / CPO commodity weakness synchronized:** Newcastle sub-$130 sustained 5+ sessions + LME nickel -10.99% MoM + CPO -1.4% weekly = resource-sector rotation risk persists; not a Wk 12 candidate area.
5. **CPI 3.34% YoY Jun (highest since Mar) + first trade-deficit in 6 years:** HAWKISH-REANCHORING carry into next BI RDG Aug 14 (T+38); rate-cut pricing pushed further out; property/construction/cement sector-weakness reinforced.
6. **10th consecutive 0/3 buy-slot week trajectory:** Wk 12 Day 2 continues cash-drag concern; but discipline holds — no forced trade under gate-fail state.
